The core model is `sentry.models.group.Group`; the repository has many unrelated “group” concepts, so I’m narrowing on imports of `sentry.models.group`, foreign keys to `sentry.Group`, and typed/API contracts using issue IDs. Next I’m separating durable dependents from incidental UI labels and tests.

    88	def parse_short_id(short_id_s: str) -> ShortId | None:
    89	    match = _short_id_re.match(short_id_s.strip())
    90	    if match is None:
    91	        return None
    92	    slug, id = match.groups()
    93	    slug = slug.lower()
    94	    try:
    95	        short_id = base32_decode(id)
    96	        # We need to make sure the short id is not overflowing the
    97	        # field's max or the lookup will fail with an assertion error.
    98	        max_id = Group._meta.get_field("short_id").MAX_VALUE
    99	        if short_id > max_id:
   100	            return None
   101	    except ValueError:
   102	        return None
   103	    return ShortId(slug, short_id)

   110	def get_group_with_redirect(
   111	    id_or_qualified_short_id: int | str,
   112	    queryset: BaseQuerySet[Group, Group] | None = None,
   113	    organization: Organization | None = None,
   114	) -> tuple[Group, bool]:
   115	    """
   116	    Retrieve a group by ID, checking the redirect table if the requested group
   117	    does not exist. Returns a two-tuple of ``(object, redirected)``.
   118	    """
   119	    if queryset is None:
   120	        if organization:
   121	            updated_qs = Group.objects.filter(project__organization=organization)
   122	            getter = updated_qs.get
   123	        else:
   124	            updated_qs = Group.objects.all()
   125	            # When not passing a queryset, we want to read from cache
   126	            getter = Group.objects.get_from_cache
   127	    else:
   128	        if organization:
   129	            updated_qs = queryset.filter(project__organization=organization)
   130	        else:
   131	            # No updates!
   132	            updated_qs = queryset
   133	        getter = updated_qs.get
   134	
   135	    if not (isinstance(id_or_qualified_short_id, int) or id_or_qualified_short_id.isdigit()):
   136	        short_id = parse_short_id(id_or_qualified_short_id)
   137	        if not short_id or not organization:
   138	            raise Group.DoesNotExist()
   139	        params = {
   140	            "project__slug": short_id.project_slug,
   141	            "short_id": short_id.short_id,
   142	            "project__organization": organization,
   143	        }
   144	    else:
   145	        short_id = None
   146	        # Validate that the numeric ID doesn't exceed the max value for the
   147	        # bounded field, otherwise the ORM will raise an AssertionError.
   148	        max_id = Group._meta.get_field("id").MAX_VALUE
   149	        if int(id_or_qualified_short_id) > max_id:
   150	            raise Group.DoesNotExist()
   151	        params = {"id": id_or_qualified_short_id}
   152	
   153	    try:
   154	        return getter(**params), False
   155	    except Group.DoesNotExist as error:
   156	        from sentry.models.groupredirect import GroupRedirect
   157	
   158	        if short_id:
   159	            # Known because we only set short_id in a path that raises if org is None
   160	            assert organization is not None
   161	            params = {
   162	                "id__in": GroupRedirect.objects.filter(
   163	                    organization_id=organization.id,
   164	                    previous_short_id=short_id.short_id,
   165	                    previous_project_slug=short_id.project_slug,
   166	                ).values_list("group_id", flat=True)[:1]
   167	            }
   168	        else:
   169	            params = {
   170	                "id__in": GroupRedirect.objects.filter(
   171	                    previous_group_id=id_or_qualified_short_id,
   172	                ).values_list("group_id", flat=True)[:1]
   173	            }
   174	
   175	        try:
   176	            return updated_qs.get(**params), True
   177	        except Group.DoesNotExist:
   178	            raise error  # raise original `DoesNotExist`

   371	    def by_qualified_short_id(
   372	        self,
   373	        organization_id: int,
   374	        short_id: str,
   375	        *,
   376	        project_ids: Collection[int] | None,
   377	    ):
   378	        return self.by_qualified_short_id_bulk(
   379	            organization_id, [short_id], project_ids=project_ids
   380	        )[0]
   381	
   382	    def by_qualified_short_id_bulk(
   383	        self,
   384	        organization_id: int,
   385	        short_ids_raw: list[str],
   386	        *,
   387	        project_ids: Collection[int] | None,
   388	    ) -> Sequence[Group]:
   389	        """
   390	        Resolve qualified short ids (e.g. ``PROJECT-123``) to groups.
   391	
   392	        Always scoped to ``organization_id``. ``project_ids`` is **required** (keyword-only, no
   393	        default) to prevent an accidental in-org IDOR: when it is a collection (including an
   394	        empty one), the lookup is additionally scoped to those projects so a short id
   395	        referencing a project the caller cannot access does not resolve. Callers with an
   396	        authorized-project set (the projects the actor is allowed to see) MUST pass it so
   397	        project-level permissions are enforced at the query layer rather than via a post-hoc
   398	        check. Pass ``project_ids=None`` ONLY when the caller legitimately operates
   399	        organization-wide (e.g. commit/PR linking, system RPCs, or reads already scoped to the
   400	        requested projects downstream); doing so is explicit and reviewable at the call site.
   401	        """
   402	        short_ids = []
   403	        for short_id_raw in short_ids_raw:
   404	            parsed_short_id = parse_short_id(short_id_raw)
   405	            if parsed_short_id is None:
   406	                raise Group.DoesNotExist()
   407	            short_ids.append(parsed_short_id)
   408	        if not short_ids:
   409	            raise Group.DoesNotExist()
   410	
   411	        project_short_id_lookup = defaultdict(list)
   412	        for short_id in short_ids:
   413	            project_short_id_lookup[short_id.project_slug].append(short_id.short_id)
   414	
   415	        short_id_lookup = reduce(
   416	            or_,
   417	            [
   418	                Q(project__slug=slug, short_id__in=short_ids)
   419	                for slug, short_ids in project_short_id_lookup.items()
   420	            ],
   421	        )
   422	
   423	        base_group_queryset = self.exclude(
   424	            status__in=[
   425	                GroupStatus.PENDING_DELETION,
   426	                GroupStatus.DELETION_IN_PROGRESS,
   427	                GroupStatus.PENDING_MERGE,
   428	            ]
   429	        ).filter(project__organization=organization_id)
   430	
   431	        if project_ids is not None:
   432	            base_group_queryset = base_group_queryset.filter(project_id__in=project_ids)
   433	
   434	        groups = list(base_group_queryset.filter(short_id_lookup).select_related("project"))
   435	        # Key the lookup by ShortId(project_slug, short_id): short ids are only unique per
   436	        # project, so the integer short_id alone collides across projects (PROJ-A-1 vs PROJ-B-1).
   437	        # parse_short_id lowercases the slug, so lowercase the project slug to match.
   438	        group_lookup: set[ShortId] = {
   439	            ShortId(group.project.slug.lower(), group.short_id) for group in groups
   440	        }
   441	
   442	        # If any requested short_ids are missing after the exact slug match,
   443	        # fallback to a case-insensitive slug lookup to handle legacy/mixed-case slugs.
   444	        # Handles legacy project slugs that may not be entirely lowercase.
   445	        missing_by_slug = defaultdict(list)
   446	        for sid in short_ids:
   447	            if sid not in group_lookup:
   448	                missing_by_slug[sid.project_slug].append(sid.short_id)
   449	
   450	        if len(missing_by_slug) > 0:
   451	            ci_short_id_lookup = reduce(
   452	                or_,
   453	                [
   454	                    Q(project__slug__iexact=slug, short_id__in=sids)
   455	                    for slug, sids in missing_by_slug.items()
   456	                ],
   457	            )
   458	
   459	            fallback_groups = list(
   460	                base_group_queryset.filter(ci_short_id_lookup).select_related("project")
   461	            )
   462	
   463	            groups.extend(fallback_groups)
   464	            group_lookup.update(
   465	                ShortId(group.project.slug.lower(), group.short_id) for group in fallback_groups
   466	            )
   467	
   468	        # Throw an error if we cannot find a group for any short id requested
   469	        for short_id in short_ids:
   470	            if short_id not in group_lookup:
   471	                raise Group.DoesNotExist()
   472	        return groups
   473	
   474	    def from_event_id(self, project, event_id):
   475	        """Resolves the 32 character event_id string into a Group for which it is found."""
   476	        group_id = None
   477	
   478	        event = eventstore.backend.get_event_by_id(project.id, event_id)
   479	
   480	        if event:
   481	            group_id = event.group_id
   482	
   483	        if group_id is None:
   484	            # Raise a Group.DoesNotExist here since it makes
   485	            # more logical sense since this is intending to resolve
   486	            # a Group.
   487	            raise Group.DoesNotExist()
   488	
   489	        return self.get(id=group_id)

   492	        events = eventstore.backend.get_events(
   493	            filter=eventstore.Filter(
   494	                event_ids=[event_id],
   495	                project_ids=project_ids,
   496	                conditions=[["group_id", "IS NOT NULL", None]],
   497	            ),
   498	            eap_conditions=and_trace_item_filters(
   499	                build_event_id_in_filter([event_id]),
   500	                TraceItemFilter(
   501	                    exists_filter=ExistsFilter(
   502	                        key=AttributeKey(name="group_id", type=AttributeKey.TYPE_INT)
   503	                    )
   504	                ),

   506	            limit=max(len(project_ids), 100),
   507	            referrer="Group.filter_by_event_id",
   508	            tenant_ids=tenant_ids,
   509	        )
   510	        return self.filter(id__in={event.group_id for event in events})

   543	    def update_group_status(
   544	        self,
   545	        groups: Iterable[Group],
   546	        status: int,
   547	        substatus: int | None,
   548	        activity_type: ActivityType,
   549	        activity_data: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
   550	        send_activity_notification: bool = True,
   551	        from_substatus: int | None = None,
   552	        detector_id: int | None = None,
   553	        update_date: datetime | None = None,
   554	    ) -> None:
   555	        """For each groups, update status to `status` and create an Activity."""
   556	        from sentry.incidents.grouptype import MetricIssue
   557	        from sentry.models.activity import Activity
   558	        from sentry.models.groupopenperiod import update_group_open_period
   559	        from sentry.workflow_engine.models.incident_groupopenperiod import (
   560	            update_incident_based_on_open_period_status_change,
   561	        )
   562	
   563	        modified_groups_list = []
   564	        selected_groups = Group.objects.filter(id__in=[g.id for g in groups]).exclude(
   565	            status=status, substatus=substatus
   566	        )
   567	
   568	        should_update_priority = (
   569	            from_substatus == GroupSubStatus.ESCALATING
   570	            and activity_type == ActivityType.AUTO_SET_ONGOING
   571	        )
   572	
   573	        # Track the resolved groups that are being unresolved and need to have their open period reopened
   574	        should_reopen_open_period = {
   575	            group.id: group.status == GroupStatus.RESOLVED for group in selected_groups
   576	        }
   577	        resolved_at = update_date if update_date is not None else timezone.now()
   578	        updated_priority = {}
   579	        for group in selected_groups:
   580	            group.status = status
   581	            group.substatus = substatus
   582	            if status == GroupStatus.RESOLVED:
   583	                group.resolved_at = resolved_at
   584	            if should_update_priority:
   585	                priority = get_priority_for_ongoing_group(group)
   586	                if priority and group.priority != priority:
   587	                    group.priority = priority
   588	                    updated_priority[group.id] = priority
   589	
   590	            modified_groups_list.append(group)
   591	
   592	        Group.objects.bulk_update(
   593	            modified_groups_list, ["status", "substatus", "priority", "resolved_at"]
   594	        )
   595	
   596	        for group in modified_groups_list:
   597	            activity = Activity.objects.create_group_activity(
   598	                group,
   599	                activity_type,
   600	                data=activity_data,
   601	                send_notification=send_activity_notification,
   602	                datetime=update_date,
   603	                detector_id=detector_id,
   604	            )
   605	            record_group_history_from_activity_type(group, activity_type.value)
   606	
   607	            action_cls = ACTIVITY_TYPE_TO_GROUP_ACTION.get(activity_type.value)
   608	            if action_cls is not None:
   609	                publish_action_from_context(
   610	                    action_cls(),
   611	                    group_id=group.id,
   612	                    project=group.project,
   613	                )
   614	
   615	            if group.id in updated_priority:
   616	                new_priority = updated_priority[group.id]
   617	                Activity.objects.create_group_activity(
   618	                    group=group,
   619	                    type=ActivityType.SET_PRIORITY,
   620	                    data={
   621	                        "priority": new_priority.to_str(),
   622	                        "reason": PriorityChangeReason.ONGOING,
   623	                    },
   624	                    datetime=update_date,
   625	                    detector_id=detector_id,
   626	                )
   627	                record_group_history(group, PRIORITY_TO_GROUP_HISTORY_STATUS[new_priority])
   628	
   629	            is_status_resolved = status == GroupStatus.RESOLVED
   630	            is_status_unresolved = status == GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED
   631	
   632	            # The open period is only updated when a group is resolved or reopened. We don't want to
   633	            # update the open period when a group transitions between different substatuses within UNRESOLVED.
   634	            if is_status_resolved:
   635	                update_group_open_period(
   636	                    group=group,
   637	                    new_status=GroupStatus.RESOLVED,
   638	                    resolution_time=activity.datetime,
   639	                    resolution_activity=activity,
   640	                )
   641	            elif is_status_unresolved and should_reopen_open_period[group.id]:
   642	                update_group_open_period(
   643	                    group=group,
   644	                    new_status=GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED,
   645	                )
   646	
   647	            should_update_incident = is_status_resolved or (
   648	                is_status_unresolved and should_reopen_open_period[group.id]
   649	            )
   650	            # TODO (aci cleanup): remove this once we've deprecated the incident model
   651	            if group.type == MetricIssue.type_id and should_update_incident:
   652	                if detector_id is None:
   653	                    logger.error(
   654	                        "Call to update metric issue status missing detector ID",
   655	                        extra={"group_id": group.id},
   656	                    )
   657	                    continue
   658	                update_incident_based_on_open_period_status_change(group, status)
   659

I found the durable contract points: `Group` itself, issue satellite models with `FlexibleForeignKey("sentry.Group")` or stored `group_id`, and services/tasks that pass `Group` or `group_id` across queues/API boundaries. I’m checking backend guidance now and then I’ll sample the key files for exact line-level handoff details.

1	# Backend Development Guide
     2	
     3	> For critical commands, see the "Command Execution Guide" section in `/AGENTS.md` in the repository root.
     4	
     5	## Security Guidelines
     6	
     7	### Preventing Indirect Object References (IDOR)
     8	
     9	**Indirect Object Reference** vulnerabilities occur when an attacker can access resources they shouldn't by manipulating IDs passed in requests. This is one of the most critical security issues in multi-tenant applications like Sentry.
    10	
    11	**Core Principle: Always Scope Queries by Organization/Project**
    12	
    13	When querying resources, ALWAYS include `organization_id` and/or `project_id` in your query filters. Never trust user-supplied IDs alone.
    14	
    15	```python
    16	# WRONG: Vulnerable to IDOR - user can access any resource by guessing IDs
    17	resource = Resource.objects.get(id=request.data["resource_id"])
    18	
    19	# RIGHT: Properly scoped to organization
    20	resource = Resource.objects.get(
    21	    id=request.data["resource_id"],
    22	    organization_id=organization.id
    23	)
    24	
    25	# RIGHT: Properly scoped to project
    26	resource = Resource.objects.get(
    27	    id=request.data["resource_id"],
    28	    project_id=project.id
    29	)
    30	```
    31	
    32	**Project ID Handling: Use `self.get_projects()`**
    33	
    34	When project IDs are passed in the request (query string or body), NEVER directly access or trust `request.data["project_id"]` or `request.GET["project_id"]`. Instead, use the endpoint's `self.get_projects()` method which performs proper permission checks.
    35	
    36	```python
    37	# WRONG: Direct access bypasses permission checks
    38	project_ids = request.data.get("project_id")
    39	projects = Project.objects.filter(id__in=project_ids)
    40	
    41	# RIGHT: Use self.get_projects() which validates permissions
    42	projects = self.get_projects(
    43	    request=request,
    44	    organization=organization,
    45	    project_ids=request.data.get("project_id")
    46	)
    47	```
    48	
    49	## Development Services
    50	
    51	Local dependencies are managed by `devservices` (config: `devservices/config.yml`).
    52	
    53	📖 Full devservices documentation: https://develop.sentry.dev/development-infrastructure/devservices.md
    54	
    55	## AI Assistant Quick Decision Trees
    56	
    57	### "User wants to add an API endpoint"
    58	
    59	1. Check if endpoint already exists: `grep -r "endpoint_name" src/sentry/api/`
    60	2. Inherit from appropriate base:
    61	   - Organization-scoped: `OrganizationEndpoint`
    62	   - Project-scoped: `ProjectEndpoint`
    63	   - Region silo: `RegionSiloEndpoint`
    64	3. File locations:
    65	   - Endpoint: `src/sentry/api/endpoints/{resource}.py`
    66	   - URL: `src/sentry/api/urls.py`
    67	   - Test: `tests/sentry/api/endpoints/test_{resource}.py`
    68	   - Serializer: `src/sentry/api/serializers/models/{model}.py`
    69	
    70	### "User wants to add a Celery task"
    71	
    72	1. Location: `src/sentry/tasks/{category}.py`
    73	2. Use `@instrumented_task` decorator
    74	3. Set appropriate `queue` and `max_retries`
    75	4. Test location: `tests/sentry/tasks/test_{category}.py`
    76	
    77	### Serializers: Avoiding N+1 Queries
    78	
    79	**Rule**: NEVER query the database in `serialize()` for bulk requests, always use `get_attrs()`.
    80	
    81	The `serialize()` function in `base.py` calls `get_attrs()` once with all objects, then `serialize()` once per object:
    82	
    83	```python
    84	# ❌ WRONG: Query runs once per object (N+1)
    85	class MySerializer(Serializer):
    86	    def serialize(self, obj, attrs, user, **kwargs):
    87	        data = RelatedModel.objects.filter(obj=obj).first()  # NO!
    88	        return {"id": obj.id, "data": data}
    89	
    90	# ✅ CORRECT: Bulk query in get_attrs
    91	class MySerializer(Serializer):
    92	    def get_attrs(self, item_list, user, **kwargs):
    93	        # Query once for all items
    94	        data_by_id = {
    95	            d.object_id: d.value
    96	            for d in RelatedModel.objects.filter(object__in=item_list)
    97	        }
    98	        return {item: {"data": data_by_id.get(item.id)} for item in item_list}
    99	
   100	    def serialize(self, obj, attrs, user, **kwargs):
   101	        return {"id": obj.id, "data": attrs.get("data")}
   102	
   103	# Extending serializers
   104	class DetailedSerializer(MySerializer):
   105	    def get_attrs(self, item_list, user, **kwargs):
   106	        attrs = super().get_attrs(item_list, user)  # Call parent first
   107	
   108	        # Add more bulk queries
   109	        extra_by_id = {e.id: e for e in Extra.objects.filter(item__in=item_list)}
   110	        for item in item_list:
   111	            attrs[item]["extra"] = extra_by_id.get(item.id)
   112	        return attrs
   113	```
   114	
   115	## API Development
   116	
   117	### Adding New Endpoints
   118	
   119	1. Create endpoint in `src/sentry/api/endpoints/`
   120	2. Add URL pattern in `src/sentry/api/urls.py`
   121	3. Document with drf-spectacular decorators
   122	4. Add tests in `tests/sentry/api/endpoints/`
   123	
   124	### API Documentation
   125	
   126	- OpenAPI spec generation: `make build-api-docs`
   127	- API ownership tracked in `src/sentry/apidocs/api_ownership_allowlist_dont_modify.py`
   128	
   129	### API Design Rules
   130	
   131	1. Route: `/api/0/organizations/{org}/projects/{project}/`
   132	2. Use `snake_case` for URL params
   133	3. Use `camelCase` for request/response bodies
   134	4. Return strings for numeric IDs
   135	5. Implement pagination with `cursor`
   136	6. Use `GET` for read, `POST` for create, `PUT` for update
   137	7. **Error responses MUST use `"detail"` key** (Django REST Framework convention)
   138	
   139	### Error Response Convention
   140	
   141	Following Django REST Framework standards, all error responses must use `"detail"` as the key for error messages.
   142	
   143	```python
   144	from rest_framework.response import Response
   145	
   146	# ✅ CORRECT: Use "detail" for error messages
   147	return Response({"detail": "Internal server error"}, status=500)
   148	return Response({"detail": "Invalid input"}, status=400)
   149	
   150	# ❌ WRONG: Don't use "error" or other keys
   151	return Response({"error": "Internal server error"}, status=500)
   152	return Response({"message": "Invalid input"}, status=400)
   153	```
   154	
   155	**Why `detail`?**
   156	
   157	- Standard Django REST Framework convention
   158	- Consistent with existing Sentry codebase
   159	- Expected by API clients and error handlers
   160	
   161	## Common Patterns
   162	
   163	### Feature Flags
   164	
   165	See **Feature Flags (FlagPole)** in `/AGENTS.md` for registration, the `features.has(...)` check, and test usage.
   166	
   167	### Permissions
   168	
   169	```python
   170	from sentry.api.permissions import SentryPermission
   171	
   172	class MyPermission(SentryPermission):
   173	    scope_map = {
   174	        'GET': ['org:read'],
   175	        'POST': ['org:write'],
   176	    }
   177	```
   178	
   179	### Options System
   180	
   181	Sentry uses a centralized options system where all options are registered in `src/sentry/options/defaults.py` with required default values.
   182	
   183	```python
   184	# CORRECT: options.get() without default - registered default is used
   185	from sentry import options
   186	
   187	batch_size = options.get("deletions.group-hash-metadata.batch-size")
   188	
   189	# WRONG: Redundant default value
   190	batch_size = options.get("deletions.group-hash-metadata.batch-size", 1000)
   191	```
   192	
   193	**Important**: Never suggest adding a default value to `options.get()` calls. All options are registered via `register()` in `defaults.py` which requires a default value. The options system will always return the registered default if no value is set, making a second default parameter redundant and potentially inconsistent.
   194	
   195	### Logging Pattern
   196	
   197	```python
   198	import logging
   199	from sentry import analytics
   200	from sentry.analytics.events.feature_used import FeatureUsedEvent  # does not exist, only for demonstration purposes
   201	
   202	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
   203	
   204	# Structured logging
   205	logger.info(
   206	    "user.action.complete",
   207	    extra={
   208	        "user_id": user.id,
   209	        "action": "login",
   210	        "ip_address": request.META.get("REMOTE_ADDR"),
   211	    }
   212	)
   213	
   214	# IMPORTANT: LOG005 use exception() within an exception handler
   215	# WRONG: Calling logger.error() when capturing exception
   216	try:
   217	    risky_operation()
   218	except ValidationError as e:
   219	    logger.error("error.invalid_payload")
   220	
   221	# RIGHT: Use logger.exception() with a message when capturing an exception
   222	try:
   223	    risky_operation()
   224	except ValidationError:
   225	    logger.exception("error.invalid_payload")
   226	
   227	# IMPORTANT: Avoid LOG011 - Never pre-format log messages with f-strings or .format()
   228	# WRONG: Pre-formatting evaluates before logger call, even if logging is disabled
   229	logger.info(f"User {user.id} completed {action}")
   230	logger.info("User {} completed {}".format(user.id, action))
   231	
   232	# RIGHT: Use logger's %-formatting for lazy evaluation
   233	logger.info("%s.user.action.complete", PREFIX)
   234	
   235	# ALSO RIGHT: Use structured logging with extra parameters only
   236	logger.info(
   237	    "user.action.complete", extra={"user_id": user.id}
   238	)
   239	
   240	# Analytics event

content.text: The audit surface is broad enough that I’m treating “production dependent” as any production model/service/UI/API boundary that either stores an issue reference, accepts/returns one as part of its contract, or reads core `Group` fields such as status, substatus, type, short ID, project, release, priority, times seen, first/last seen, assignee, URL, or share identity. I’m pulling exact line anchors for the high-risk and non-obvious subsystems now.

   146	    def has_permission(self, request: Request, view: APIView) -> bool:
   147	        if is_demo_user(request.user):
   148	            if not is_demo_mode_enabled() or request.method not in self.ALLOWED_METHODS:
   149	                return False
   150	
   151	            return True
   152	        return super().has_permission(request, view)
   153	
   154	    def has_object_permission(self, request: Request, view: APIView, group: Any) -> bool:
   155	        assert isinstance(group, Group)
   156	        if is_demo_user(request.user):
   157	            if not is_demo_mode_enabled() or request.method not in self.ALLOWED_METHODS:
   158	                return False
   159	
   160	            if not is_demo_org(group.project.organization):
   161	                return False
   162	
   163	            return True

   123	        model = get_issue_tsdb_group_model(group.issue_category)

   125	        hourly_stats = tsdb.backend.rollup(
   126	            tsdb.backend.get_range(
   127	                model=model,

   129	                end=now,
   130	                start=now - timedelta(days=1),

   132	                tenant_ids={"organization_id": group.project.organization_id},
   133	            ),
   134	            3600,
   135	        )[group.id]
   136	        daily_stats = tsdb.backend.rollup(
   137	            tsdb.backend.get_range(
   138	                model=model,

   140	                end=now,
   141	                start=now - timedelta(days=30),

   143	                tenant_ids={"organization_id": group.project.organization_id},
   144	            ),
   145	            3600 * 24,

   482	def send_issue_view_attribution(request: Request, response: Response, group: Any) -> None:
   483	    if request.method != "GET":
   484	        return
   485	
   486	    if response.status_code < 200 or response.status_code >= 300:
   487	        return
   488	
   489	    if not isinstance(group, Group):
   490	        return
   491	
   492	    if is_mcp_request(request):
   493	        client_family = request.headers.get("x-sentry-mcp-client-family") or "unknown"
   494	        analytics.record(
   495	            IssueViewedEvent(
   496	                organization_id=group.project.organization_id,
   497	                project_id=group.project.id,
   498	                group_id=group.id,
   499	                client=f"mcp - {client_family}",
   500	                user_id=request.user.id,
   501	            )
   502	        )
   503	        if features.has("organizations:mcp-issue-view-attribution", group.project.organization):
   504	            cache.set(
   505	                cache_key_for_issue_view(group.id, "mcp"), client_family, ISSUE_VIEW_CACHE_KEY_TTL
   506	            )

    77	def inbox_search(
    78	    projects: Sequence[Project],
    79	    environments: Sequence[Environment] | None = None,
    80	    limit: int = 100,
    81	    cursor: Cursor | None = None,
    82	    count_hits: bool = False,
    83	    search_filters: Sequence[SearchFilter] | None = None,
    84	    date_from: datetime | None = None,
    85	    date_to: datetime | None = None,
    86	    max_hits: int | None = None,
    87	    actor: Any | None = None,
    88	) -> CursorResult[Group]:
    89	    now: datetime = timezone.now()
    90	    end: datetime | None = None
    91	    end_params: list[datetime] = [
    92	        _f for _f in [date_to, get_search_filter(search_filters, "date", "<")] if _f
    93	    ]
    94	    if end_params:
    95	        end = min(end_params)
    96	
    97	    end = end if end else now + ALLOWED_FUTURE_DELTA
    98	
    99	    # We only want to search back a week at most, since that's the oldest inbox rows
   100	    # can be.
   101	    earliest_date = now - timedelta(days=7)
   102	    start_params = [date_from, earliest_date, get_search_filter(search_filters, "date", ">")]
   103	    start = max(_f for _f in start_params if _f)
   104	    end = max([earliest_date, end])
   105	
   106	    if start >= end:
   107	        return Paginator(Group.objects.none()).get_result()
   108	
   109	    # Make sure search terms are valid
   110	    invalid_search_terms = (
   111	        [str(sf) for sf in search_filters if sf.key.name not in allowed_inbox_search_terms]
   112	        if search_filters
   113	        else []
   114	    )
   115	    if invalid_search_terms:
   116	        raise InvalidSearchQuery(f"Invalid search terms for 'inbox' search: {invalid_search_terms}")
   117	
   118	    # Make sure this is an inbox search
   119	    if not get_search_filter(search_filters, "for_review", "="):
   120	        raise InvalidSearchQuery("Sort key 'inbox' only supported for inbox search")
   121	
   122	    if get_search_filter(
   123	        search_filters, "status", "="
   124	    ) != GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED and get_search_filter(search_filters, "status", "IN") != [
   125	        GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED
   126	    ]:
   127	        raise InvalidSearchQuery("Inbox search only works for 'unresolved' status")
   128	
   129	    # We just filter on `GroupInbox.date_added` here, and don't filter by date
   130	    # on the group. This keeps the query simpler and faster in some edge cases,
   131	    # and date_added is a good enough proxy when we're using this sort.
   132	    qs = GroupInbox.objects.filter(
   133	        date_added__gte=start,
   134	        date_added__lte=end,
   135	        project__in=projects,
   136	    ).using_replica()
   137	
   138	    if environments is not None:
   139	        environment_ids: list[int] = [environment.id for environment in environments]
   140	        qs = qs.filter(
   141	            group_id__in=GroupEnvironment.objects.filter(environment_id__in=environment_ids)
   142	            .values_list("group_id", flat=True)
   143	            .distinct()
   144	        )
   145	
   146	    owner_search = get_search_filter(search_filters, "assigned_or_suggested", "IN")
   147	    if owner_search:
   148	        qs = qs.filter(
   149	            assigned_or_suggested_filter(owner_search, projects, field_filter="group_id")
   150	        )
   151	
   152	    paginator = DateTimePaginator(qs.order_by("date_added"), "-date_added")
   153	    results = paginator.get_result(limit, cursor, count_hits=count_hits, max_hits=max_hits)
   154	
   155	    # We want to return groups from the endpoint, but have the cursor be related to the
   156	    # GroupInbox rows. So we paginate on the GroupInbox results queryset, then fetch
   157	    # the group_ids out and use them to get the actual groups.
   158	    group_qs = Group.objects.filter(
   159	        id__in=[r.group_id for r in results.results],
   160	        project__in=projects,
   161	        status=GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED,
   162	    )
   163	    groups: Mapping[int, Group] = {g.id: g for g in group_qs}
   164	    results.results = [groups[r.group_id] for r in results.results if r.group_id in groups]
   165	    return results
   166

   169	    request: Request,
   170	    organization: Organization,

   172	    environments: Sequence[Environment],

   175	    with start_span(op="_search"):
   176	        query_kwargs = build_query_params_from_request(

   179	        if extra_query_kwargs is not None:
   180	            assert "environment" not in extra_query_kwargs
   181	            query_kwargs.update(extra_query_kwargs)
   182	
   183	        query_kwargs["environments"] = environments if environments else None
   184	
   185	        query_kwargs["actor"] = request.user
   186	        if query_kwargs["sort_by"] == "progress" and not features.has(

   193	            result = inbox_search(**query_kwargs)
   194	        else:
   195	            result = search.backend.query(**query_kwargs)
   196	        return result, query_kwargs

    91	def handle_discard(
    92	    request: Request,
    93	    group_list: Sequence[Group],
    94	    projects: Sequence[Project],
    95	    acting_user: RpcUser | User | None,
    96	) -> Response:
    97	    for project in projects:
    98	        if not features.has("projects:discard-groups", project, actor=acting_user):
    99	            return Response({"detail": ["You do not have that feature enabled"]}, status=400)
   100	
   101	    if any(group.issue_category != GroupCategory.ERROR for group in group_list):
   102	        raise rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError(
   103	            detail="Only error issues can be discarded."
   104	        )
   105	    # grouped by project_id
   106	    groups_to_delete = defaultdict(list)
   107	
   108	    for group in group_list:
   109	        with transaction.atomic(router.db_for_write(GroupTombstone)):
   110	            try:
   111	                tombstone = GroupTombstone.objects.create(
   112	                    previous_group_id=group.id,
   113	                    actor_id=coerce_id_from(acting_user),
   114	                    **{name: getattr(group, name) for name in TOMBSTONE_FIELDS_FROM_GROUP},
   115	                )
   116	            except IntegrityError:
   117	                # in this case, a tombstone has already been created
   118	                # for a group, so no hash updates are necessary
   119	                pass
   120	            else:
   121	                groups_to_delete[group.project_id].append(group)
   122	
   123	                GroupHash.objects.filter(group=group).update(
   124	                    group=None, group_tombstone_id=tombstone.id
   125	                )
   126	
   127	    for project in projects:
   128	        delete_group_list(
   129	            request, project, groups_to_delete.get(project.id, []), delete_type="discard"
   130	        )
   131	
   132	    return Response(status=204)

   135	def self_subscribe_and_assign_issue(
   136	    acting_user: RpcUser | User | None, group: Group, self_assign_issue: str
   137	) -> Actor | None:
   138	    """
   139	    Used during issue resolution to assign to acting user
   140	    returns None if the user didn't elect to self assign on resolution
   141	    or the group is assigned already, otherwise returns Actor
   142	    representation of current user
   143	    """
   144	    if acting_user:
   145	        GroupSubscription.objects.subscribe(
   146	            subscriber=acting_user, group=group, reason=GroupSubscriptionReason.status_change
   147	        )
   148	
   149	        if self_assign_issue == "1" and not group.assignee_set.exists():
   150	            return Actor(id=acting_user.id, actor_type=ActorType.USER)
   151	    return None

   154	def get_current_release_version_of_group(group: Group, follows_semver: bool = False) -> str | None:
   155	    """
   156	    Function that returns the latest release version associated with a Group, and by latest we
   157	    mean either most recent (date) or latest in semver versioning scheme
   158	    Inputs:
   159	        * group: Group of the issue
   160	        * follows_semver: flag that determines whether the project of the group follows semantic
   161	                          versioning or not.
   162	    Returns:
   163	        current_release_version
   164	    """
   165	    current_release_version = None
   166	    if follows_semver:
   167	        release = greatest_semver_release(group.project)
   168	        if release is not None:
   169	            current_release_version = release.version
   170	    else:
   171	        # This sets current_release_version to the most recent release associated with a group
   172	        # In order to be able to do that, `use_cache` has to be set to False. Otherwise,
   173	        # group.get_last_release might not return the actual latest release associated with a
   174	        # group but rather a cached version (which might or might not be the actual latest. It is
   175	        # the first latest observed by Sentry)
   176	        current_release_version = group.get_last_release(use_cache=False)
   177	    return current_release_version

   684	    referer: str,
   685	) -> MergedGroup:
   686	    issue_stream_regex = r"^(\/organizations\/[^/]+)?\/issues\/$"
   687	    similar_issues_tab_regex = r"^(\/organizations\/[^/]+)?\/issues\/\d+\/similar\/$"
   688	
   689	    metrics.incr(
   690	        "grouping.merge_issues",
   691	        sample_rate=1.0,
   692	        tags={
   693	            # We assume that if someone's merging groups, they're from the same platform
   694	            "platform": group_list[0].platform or "unknown",
   695	            "sdk": group_list[0].sdk or "unknown",
   696	            # TODO: It's probably cleaner to just send this value from the front end
   697	            "referer": (
   698	                "issue stream"
   699	                if re.search(issue_stream_regex, referer)
   700	                else (
   701	                    "similar issues tab"
   702	                    if re.search(similar_issues_tab_regex, referer)
   703	                    else "unknown"
   704	                )
   705	            ),
   706	        },
   707	    )
   708	    return handle_merge(group_list, project_lookup, acting_user)

   711	def handle_other_status_updates(
   712	    result: dict[str, Any],
   713	    group_list: Sequence[Group],
   714	    projects: Sequence[Project],
   715	    project_lookup: Mapping[int, Project],
   716	    status_details: dict[str, Any],
   717	    acting_user: RpcUser | User | None,
   718	) -> dict[str, Any]:
   719	    group_ids = [group.id for group in group_list]
   720	    queryset = Group.objects.filter(id__in=group_ids)
   721	    new_status = STATUS_UPDATE_CHOICES[result["status"]]
   722	    new_substatus = None
   723	    if result.get("substatus"):
   724	        new_substatus = SUBSTATUS_UPDATE_CHOICES[result["substatus"]]
   725	    new_substatus = infer_substatus(new_status, new_substatus, status_details, group_list)
   726	
   727	    with transaction.atomic(router.db_for_write(Group)):
   728	        status_updated = queryset.exclude(status=new_status).update_with_returning(
   729	            ["id"], status=new_status, substatus=new_substatus
   730	        )
   731	        changed_group_ids = {row[0] for row in status_updated}
   732	        GroupResolution.objects.filter(group__in=group_ids).delete()
   733	        # Also delete commit/PR resolution links when unresolving to prevent
   734	        # showing old "resolved by commit" after manual re-resolution
   735	        if new_status in (GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED, GroupStatus.IGNORED):
   736	            GroupLink.objects.filter(
   737	                group_id__in=group_ids,
   738	                linked_type=GroupLink.LinkedType.commit,
   739	                relationship=GroupLink.Relationship.resolves,
   740	            ).delete()
   741	        if new_status == GroupStatus.IGNORED:
   742	            if new_substatus == GroupSubStatus.UNTIL_ESCALATING:
   743	                result["statusDetails"] = handle_archived_until_escalating(
   744	                    group_list, acting_user, projects, sender=update_groups
   745	                )
   746	            else:
   747	                result["statusDetails"] = handle_ignored(group_list, status_details, acting_user)
   748	            result["inbox"] = None
   749	        else:
   750	            result["statusDetails"] = {}
   751	
   752	    changed_group_list = [group for group in group_list if group.id in changed_group_ids]
   753	    if changed_group_list:
   754	        handle_status_update(
   755	            group_list=changed_group_list,
   756	            projects=projects,
   757	            project_lookup=project_lookup,
   758	            new_status=new_status,
   759	            new_substatus=new_substatus,
   760	            is_bulk=len(group_ids) > 1,

   201	def _get_status_label(group: Group):
   202	    status = group.get_status()
   203	
   204	    if status == GroupStatus.RESOLVED:
   205	        status_label = "resolved"
   206	    elif status == GroupStatus.IGNORED:
   207	        status_label = "ignored"
   208	    elif status in [GroupStatus.PENDING_DELETION, GroupStatus.DELETION_IN_PROGRESS]:
   209	        status_label = "pending_deletion"
   210	    elif status == GroupStatus.PENDING_MERGE:
   211	        status_label = "pending_merge"
   212	    elif status == GroupStatus.REPROCESSING:
   213	        status_label = "reprocessing"
   214	    else:
   215	        status_label = "unresolved"
   216	
   217	    return status_label

   460	    def _get_status(self, attrs: Mapping[str, Any], obj: Group):
   461	        status = obj.status
   462	        status_details = {}
   463	        if attrs["ignore_until"]:
   464	            snooze = attrs["ignore_until"]
   465	            if snooze.is_valid(group=obj):
   466	                # counts return the delta remaining when window is not set
   467	                status_details.update(
   468	                    {
   469	                        "ignoreCount": (
   470	                            snooze.count - (obj.times_seen - snooze.state["times_seen"])
   471	                            if snooze.count and not snooze.window
   472	                            else snooze.count
   473	                        ),
   474	                        "ignoreUntil": snooze.until,
   475	                        "ignoreUserCount": (
   476	                            snooze.user_count - (attrs["user_count"] - snooze.state["users_seen"])
   477	                            if snooze.user_count
   478	                            and not snooze.user_window
   479	                            and not self._collapse("stats")
   480	                            else snooze.user_count
   481	                        ),
   482	                        "ignoreUserWindow": snooze.user_window,
   483	                        "ignoreWindow": snooze.window,
   484	                        "actor": attrs["ignore_actor"],
   485	                    }
   486	                )
   487	            else:
   488	                status = GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED
   489	        # If the issue is UNRESOLVED but has resolved_at set, it means the user manually
   490	        # unresolved it after it was resolved. We should respect that and not override
   491	        # the status back to RESOLVED.
   492	        if status == GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED and obj.is_over_resolve_age() and not obj.resolved_at:
   493	            # When an issue is over the auto-resolve age but the task has not yet run
   494	            # Only show as auto-resolved if this group type has auto-resolve enabled
   495	            if obj.issue_type.enable_auto_resolve:
   496	                status = GroupStatus.RESOLVED
   497	                status_details["autoResolved"] = True
   498	        if status == GroupStatus.RESOLVED:
   499	            status_label = "resolved"
   500	            if attrs["resolution_type"] == "release":
   501	                res_type, res_version, _ = attrs["resolution"]
   502	                if res_type in (GroupResolution.Type.in_next_release, None):
   503	                    status_details["inNextRelease"] = True
   504	                elif res_type == GroupResolution.Type.in_release:
   505	                    status_details["inRelease"] = res_version
   506	                status_details["actor"] = attrs["resolution_actor"]
   507	            elif attrs["resolution_type"] == "commit":
   508	                status_details["inCommit"] = attrs["resolution"]
   509	        elif status == GroupStatus.IGNORED:
   510	            status_label = "ignored"
   511	        elif status in [GroupStatus.PENDING_DELETION, GroupStatus.DELETION_IN_PROGRESS]:
   512	            status_label = "pending_deletion"
   513	        elif status == GroupStatus.PENDING_MERGE:
   514	            status_label = "pending_merge"
   515	        elif status == GroupStatus.REPROCESSING:
   516	            status_label = "reprocessing"
   517	            status_details["pendingEvents"], status_details["info"] = get_progress(
   518	                attrs["id"], obj.project.id
   519	            )
   520	        else:
   521	            status_label = "unresolved"
   522	        return status_details, status_label

   524	    def _get_seen_stats(self, item_list: Sequence[Group], user) -> Mapping[Group, SeenStats] | None:
   525	        """
   526	        Returns a dictionary keyed by item that includes:
   527	            - times_seen
   528	            - first_seen
   529	            - last_seen
   530	            - user_count
   531	        """
   532	        if self._collapse("stats"):
   533	            return None
   534	
   535	        if not item_list:
   536	            return None
   537	
   538	        # partition the item_list by type
   539	        error_issues = [group for group in item_list if GroupCategory.ERROR == group.issue_category]
   540	        generic_issues = [
   541	            group for group in item_list if group.issue_category != GroupCategory.ERROR
   542	        ]
   543	
   544	        # bulk query for the seen_stats by type
   545	        error_stats = (self._seen_stats_error(error_issues, user) if error_issues else {}) or {}
   546	        generic_stats = (
   547	            self._seen_stats_generic(generic_issues, user) if generic_issues else {}
   548	        ) or {}
   549	        agg_stats = {**error_stats, **generic_stats}
   550	        # combine results back
   551	        return {group: agg_stats[group] for group in item_list if group in agg_stats}

   726	    @staticmethod
   727	    def _resolve_integration_annotations(
   728	        org_id: int, groups: Sequence[Group]
   729	    ) -> Sequence[Mapping[int, Sequence[Any]]]:
   730	        from sentry.integrations.base import IntegrationFeatures
   731	
   732	        integration_annotations = []
   733	        # find all the integration installs that have issue tracking
   734	        integrations = integration_service.get_integrations(organization_id=org_id)
   735	        for integration in integrations:
   736	            if not (
   737	                integration.has_feature(feature=IntegrationFeatures.ISSUE_BASIC)
   738	                or integration.has_feature(feature=IntegrationFeatures.ISSUE_SYNC)
   739	            ):
   740	                continue
   741	
   742	            install = integration.get_installation(organization_id=org_id)
   743	            assert isinstance(install, IssueBasicIntegration), install
   744	            local_annotations_by_group_id = (
   745	                safe_execute(install.get_annotations_for_group_list, group_list=groups) or {}
   746	            )
   747	            integration_annotations.append(local_annotations_by_group_id)
   748	
   749	        return integration_annotations

    51	class GroupStatsQueryArgs(NamedTuple):
    52	    stats_period: str | None
    53	    stats_period_start: datetime | None = None
    54	    stats_period_end: datetime | None = None

    57	class GroupStatsMixin:
    58	    STATS_PERIOD_CHOICES = {
    59	        "14d": StatsPeriod(14, timedelta(hours=24)),
    60	        "24h": StatsPeriod(24, timedelta(hours=1)),
    61	    }
    62	
    63	    CUSTOM_ROLLUP_CHOICES = {
    64	        "1h": timedelta(hours=1).total_seconds(),
    65	        "2h": timedelta(hours=2).total_seconds(),
    66	        "3h": timedelta(hours=3).total_seconds(),
    67	        "6h": timedelta(hours=6).total_seconds(),
    68	        "12h": timedelta(hours=12).total_seconds(),
    69	        "24h": timedelta(hours=24).total_seconds(),
    70	    }
    71	
    72	    CUSTOM_SEGMENTS = 29  # for 30 segments use 1/29th intervals
    73	    CUSTOM_SEGMENTS_12H = 35  # for 12h 36 segments, otherwise 15-16-17 bars is too few
    74	
    75	    @abstractmethod
    76	    def query_tsdb(
    77	        self,
    78	        groups: Sequence[Group],
    79	        query_params,
    80	        conditions=None,
    81	        environment_ids=None,
    82	        user=None,
    83	        **kwargs,
    84	    ):
    85	        pass
    86	
    87	    def get_stats(
    88	        self,
    89	        item_list: Sequence[Group],
    90	        user,
    91	        stats_query_args: GroupStatsQueryArgs,
    92	        **kwargs,
    93	    ):
    94	        if stats_query_args and stats_query_args.stats_period:
    95	            # we need to compute stats at 1d (1h resolution), and 14d or a custom given period
    96	            if stats_query_args.stats_period == "auto":
    97	                assert stats_query_args.stats_period_end is not None
    98	                assert stats_query_args.stats_period_start is not None
    99	                total_period = (
   100	                    stats_query_args.stats_period_end - stats_query_args.stats_period_start
   101	                ).total_seconds()
   102	                if total_period < timedelta(hours=24).total_seconds():
   103	                    rollup = total_period / self.CUSTOM_SEGMENTS
   104	                elif total_period < self.CUSTOM_SEGMENTS * self.CUSTOM_ROLLUP_CHOICES["1h"]:
   105	                    rollup = self.CUSTOM_ROLLUP_CHOICES["1h"]
   106	                elif total_period < self.CUSTOM_SEGMENTS * self.CUSTOM_ROLLUP_CHOICES["2h"]:
   107	                    rollup = self.CUSTOM_ROLLUP_CHOICES["2h"]
   108	                elif total_period < self.CUSTOM_SEGMENTS * self.CUSTOM_ROLLUP_CHOICES["3h"]:
   109	                    rollup = self.CUSTOM_ROLLUP_CHOICES["3h"]
   110	                elif total_period < self.CUSTOM_SEGMENTS * self.CUSTOM_ROLLUP_CHOICES["6h"]:
   111	                    rollup = self.CUSTOM_ROLLUP_CHOICES["6h"]
   112	                elif (
   113	                    total_period < self.CUSTOM_SEGMENTS_12H * self.CUSTOM_ROLLUP_CHOICES["12h"]
   114	                ):  # 36 segments is ok
   115	                    rollup = self.CUSTOM_ROLLUP_CHOICES["12h"]
   116	                elif total_period < self.CUSTOM_SEGMENTS * self.CUSTOM_ROLLUP_CHOICES["24h"]:
   117	                    rollup = self.CUSTOM_ROLLUP_CHOICES["24h"]
   118	                else:
   119	                    delta_day = self.CUSTOM_ROLLUP_CHOICES["24h"]
   120	                    rollup = round(total_period / (self.CUSTOM_SEGMENTS * delta_day)) * delta_day
   121	
   122	                query_params = {
   123	                    "start": stats_query_args.stats_period_start,
   124	                    "end": stats_query_args.stats_period_end,
   125	                    "rollup": max(int(rollup), 1),  # Zero is a bad thing to divide by
   126	                }
   127	            else:
   128	                segments, interval = self.STATS_PERIOD_CHOICES[stats_query_args.stats_period]
   129	                now = timezone.now()
   130	                query_params = {
   131	                    "start": now - ((segments - 1) * interval),
   132	                    "end": now,
   133	                    "rollup": int(interval.total_seconds()),
   134	                }
   135	
   136	            return self.query_tsdb(
   137	                item_list,
   138	                query_params,
   139	                user=user,
   140	                **kwargs,
   141	            )

   152	class StreamGroupSerializer(GroupSerializer, GroupStatsMixin):
   153	    def __init__(self, environment_func=None, stats_period=None):
   154	        super().__init__(environment_func=environment_func)
   155	
   156	        assert (
   157	            stats_period is None
   158	            or stats_period in self.STATS_PERIOD_CHOICES
   159	            or stats_period == "auto"
   160	        ), stats_period
   161	
   162	        self.stats_period = stats_period

   164	    def get_attrs(
   165	        self,
   166	        item_list: Sequence[Group],
   167	        user: User | RpcUser | AnonymousUser,
   168	        **kwargs: Any,
   169	    ) -> dict[Group, dict[str, Any]]:
   170	        attrs = super().get_attrs(item_list, user)
   171	
   172	        if self.stats_period:
   173	            stats = self.get_stats(
   174	                item_list,
   175	                user,
   176	                GroupStatsQueryArgs(self.stats_period, None, None),
   177	            )
   178	            for item in item_list:
   179	                attrs[item]["stats"] = stats[item.id]
   180	
   181	        return attrs

   183	    def serialize(
   184	        self,
   185	        obj: Group,
   186	        attrs: Mapping[str, Any],
   187	        user: User | RpcUser | AnonymousUser,
   188	        **kwargs: Any,
   189	    ) -> StreamGroupSerializerResponse:
   190	        base = super().serialize(obj, attrs, user)
   191	
   192	        if self.stats_period:
   193	            extra: _MaybeStats = {"stats": {self.stats_period: attrs["stats"]}}
   194	        else:
   195	            extra = {}
   196	
   197	        return {**base, **extra}

   199	    def query_tsdb(
   200	        self,
   201	        groups: Sequence[Group],
   202	        query_params,
   203	        conditions=None,
   204	        environment_ids=None,
   205	        user=None,
   206	        **kwargs,
   207	    ):
   208	        try:
   209	            environment = self.environment_func()

   212	        else:
   213	            org_id = groups[0].project.organization_id if groups else None
   214	            stats = tsdb.backend.get_range(
   215	                model=TSDBModel.group,

   217	                environment_ids=[environment.id] if environment is not None else None,
   218	                **query_params,

   440	        if self._expand("sentryAppIssues"):

   443	                sentry_app_issues = serialize(

   445	                )

   447	
   448	        if (
   449	            self._expand("latestEventHasAttachments")

   452	        ):
   453	            for item in item_list:
   454	                latest_event = item.get_latest_event()
   455	                if latest_event is not None:
   456	                    num_attachments = EventAttachment.objects.filter(

   458	                    ).count()
   459	                    attrs[item]["latestEventHasAttachments"] = num_attachments > 0
   460	
   461	        return attrs

   463	    def serialize(  # type: ignore[override]  # intentionally different shape
   464	        self,
   465	        obj: Group,
   466	        attrs: Mapping[str, Any],
   467	        user: User | RpcUser | AnonymousUser,
   468	        **kwargs: Any,
   469	    ) -> StreamGroupSerializerSnubaResponse:
   470	        if not self._collapse("base"):
   471	            result: StreamGroupSerializerSnubaResponse = {**super().serialize(obj, attrs, user)}

   478	
   479	        if not self._collapse("stats"):

   530	    def query_tsdb(

   441	        if normalize_stacktraces:
   442	            with sentry_sdk.start_span(op="grouping.normalize_stacktraces_for_grouping") as span:
   443	                span.set_tag("project", self.project_id)
   444	                span.set_tag("event_id", self.event_id)
   445	                self.normalize_stacktraces_for_grouping(loaded_grouping_config)
   446	
   447	        with sentry_sdk.start_span(op="grouping.get_grouping_variants") as span:
   448	            span.set_tag("project", self.project_id)
   449	            span.set_tag("event_id", self.event_id)
   450	
   451	            return get_grouping_variants_for_event(self, loaded_grouping_config)

   458	    ) -> SpanGroupingResults:

   471	        """Returns the internal raw event data dict."""
   472	        rv = dict(self.data.items())
   473	        # If we get raw data for snuba we remove some large keys that blow
   474	        # up the payload for no reason.

   485	        subject_template = self.project.get_option("mail:subject_template")
   486	        if subject_template:

   488	        elif self.group and self.group.issue_category in PERFORMANCE_ISSUE_CATEGORIES:
   489	            template = EventSubjectTemplate("$shortID - $issueType")
   490	        else:

   494	    def as_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   495	        """Returns the data in normalized form for external consumers."""
   496	        data: dict[str, Any] = {}
   497	        data["event_id"] = self.event_id
   498	        data["project"] = self.project_id
   499	        data["release"] = self.release
   500	        data["dist"] = self.dist
   501	        data["platform"] = self.platform
   502	        data["message"] = self.message
   503	        data["datetime"] = self.datetime
   504	        data["tags"] = [(k.split("sentry:", 1)[-1], v) for (k, v) in self.tags]
   505	        for k, v in sorted(self.data.items()):
   506	            if k in data:
   507	                continue
   508	            if k == "sdk":
   509	                v = {v_k: v_v for v_k, v_v in v.items() if v_k != "client_ip"}
   510	            data[k] = v
   511	
   512	        # for a long time culprit was not persisted.  In those cases put
   513	        # the culprit in from the group.
   514	        if data.get("culprit") is None and self.group_id and self.group:
   515	            data["culprit"] = self.group.culprit
   516	
   517	        # Override title and location with dynamically generated data
   518	        data["title"] = self.title
   519	        data["location"] = self.location
   520	
   521	        return data

   523	    @cached_property
   524	    def search_message(self) -> str:
   525	        """
   526	        The internal search_message attribute is only used for search purposes.
   527	        It adds a bunch of data from the metadata and the culprit.
   528	        """
   529	        data = self.data
   530	        culprit = self.culprit
   531	
   532	        event_metadata = self.get_event_metadata()
   533	
   534	        message = ""
   535	
   536	        if data.get("logentry"):
   537	            message += data["logentry"].get("formatted") or data["logentry"].get("message") or ""
   538	
   539	        if event_metadata:
   540	            for key, value in event_metadata.items():
   541	                if key in SEARCH_MESSAGE_SKIPPED_KEYS or isinstance(value, (bool, int, float)):
   542	                    continue
   543	
   544	                value_u = force_str(value, errors="replace")
   545	                if value_u not in message:
   546	                    message = f"{message} {value_u}"
   547	
   548	        if culprit and culprit not in message:
   549	            culprit_u = force_str(culprit, errors="replace")
   550	            message = f"{message} {culprit_u}"
   551	
   552	        return cast(str, trim(message.strip(), settings.SENTRY_MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH))

    21	class Filter:
    22	    """
    23	    A set of conditions, start/end times and project, group and event ID sets
    24	    used to restrict the results of a Snuba query.
    25	
    26	    start (DateTime): Start datetime - default None
    27	    end (DateTime): Start datetime - default None
    28	    conditions (Sequence[tuple[str, str, Any]]): List of conditions to fetch - default None
    29	    having (Sequence[str, str, Any]]): List of having conditions to filter by - default None
    30	    user_id (int): The user ID to fetch - default None
    31	    organization_id (int): The organization ID to fetch - default None
    32	    team_id (Sequence[int]): List of team IDs to fetch - default None
    33	    project_ids (Sequence[int]): List of project IDs to fetch - default None
    34	    group_ids (Sequence[int]): List of group IDs to fetch - default None
    35	    event_ids (Sequence[int]): List of event IDs to fetch - default None
    36	
    37	    selected_columns (Sequence[str]): List of columns to select
    38	    aggregations (Sequence[Any, str|None, str]): Aggregate functions to fetch.
    39	    groupby (Sequence[str]): List of columns to group results by
    40	
    41	    condition_aggregates (Sequence[str]): List of aggregates used in the condition
    42	    aliases (Dict[str, Alias]): Endpoint specific aliases
    43	    """
    44	
    45	    def __init__(
    46	        self,
    47	        start: datetime | None = None,
    48	        end: datetime | None = None,
    49	        conditions: list[Any] | None = None,
    50	        having: Sequence[Condition] | None = None,
    51	        user_id: int | None = None,
    52	        organization_id: int | None = None,
    53	        team_id: Sequence[int] | None = None,
    54	        project_ids: Sequence[int] | None = None,
    55	        group_ids: Sequence[int] | None = None,
    56	        event_ids: Sequence[str] | None = None,
    57	        selected_columns: Sequence[str] | None = None,
    58	        aggregations: Sequence[str] | None = None,
    59	        rollup: int | None = None,
    60	        groupby: Sequence[str] | None = None,
    61	        orderby: Sequence[str] | None = None,
    62	        condition_aggregates: Sequence[str] | None = None,
    63	        aliases: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
    64	    ) -> None:
    65	        self.start = start
    66	        self.end = end
    67	        self.conditions = conditions or []
    68	        self.having = having or []
    69	        self.user_id = user_id
    70	        self.organization_id = organization_id
    71	        self.team_id = team_id
    72	        self.project_ids = project_ids
    73	        self.group_ids = group_ids
    74	        self.event_ids = event_ids or []
    75	
    76	        self.rollup = rollup
    77	        self.selected_columns = selected_columns if selected_columns is not None else []
    78	        self.aggregations = aggregations if aggregations is not None else []
    79	        self.groupby = groupby
    80	        self.orderby = orderby
    81	        self.condition_aggregates = condition_aggregates
    82	        self.aliases = aliases
    83	
    84	    @property
    85	    def filter_keys(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
    86	        """
    87	        Get filter_keys value required for raw snuba query
    88	        """
    89	        filter_keys: dict[str, Any] = {}
    90	
    91	        if self.project_ids:
    92	            filter_keys["project_id"] = self.project_ids
    93	
    94	        if self.group_ids:
    95	            filter_keys["group_id"] = self.group_ids
    96	
    97	        if self.event_ids:
    98	            filter_keys["event_id"] = self.event_ids
    99	
   100	        return filter_keys
   101	
   102	    @property
   103	    def params(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   104	        """
   105	        Get the datetime parameters as a dictionary
   106	        """
   107	        return {
   108	            "start": self.start,
   109	            "end": self.end,
   110	            "user_id": self.user_id,
   111	            "organization_id": self.organization_id,
   112	            # needed for the team key transaction column
   113	            "team_id": self.team_id,
   114	            "project_id": self.project_ids,
   115	        }
   116	
   117	    def update_with(self, updates: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   118	        keys = ("selected_columns", "aggregations", "conditions", "orderby", "groupby", "rollup")
   119	        for key in keys:
   120	            if key in updates:
   121	                setattr(self, key, updates[key])
   122	
   123	    def clone(self) -> Self:
   124	        return deepcopy(self)

    73	class SnubaEventStorage(EventStorage):
    74	    """
    75	    Eventstore backend backed by Snuba
    76	    """
    77	
    78	    def get_events_snql(
    79	        self,
    80	        organization_id: int,
    81	        group_id: int,
    82	        start: datetime | None,
    83	        end: datetime | None,
    84	        conditions: Sequence[Condition],
    85	        orderby: Sequence[str],
    86	        limit: int = DEFAULT_LIMIT,
    87	        inner_limit: int | None = None,
    88	        offset: int = DEFAULT_OFFSET,
    89	        referrer: str = "eventstore.get_events_snql",
    90	        dataset: Dataset = Dataset.Events,
    91	        tenant_ids: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
    92	    ) -> list[Event]:
    93	        cols = self.__get_columns(dataset)
    94	
    95	        resolved_order_by = []
    96	        order_by_col_names: set[str] = set()
    97	        for order_field_alias in orderby:
    98	            if order_field_alias.startswith("-"):
    99	                direction = Direction.DESC
   100	                order_field_alias = order_field_alias[1:]
   101	            else:
   102	                direction = Direction.ASC
   103	            resolved_column_or_none = DATASETS[dataset].get(order_field_alias)
   104	            if resolved_column_or_none:
   105	                order_by_col_names.add(resolved_column_or_none)
   106	                # special-case handling for nullable column values and proper ordering based on direction
   107	                # null values are always last in the sort order regardless of Desc or Asc ordering
   108	                if order_field_alias == Columns.NUM_PROCESSING_ERRORS.value.alias:
   109	                    resolved_order_by.append(
   110	                        OrderBy(
   111	                            Function("coalesce", [Column(resolved_column_or_none), 99999999]),
   112	                            direction=direction,
   113	                        )
   114	                    )
   115	                elif order_field_alias == Columns.TRACE_SAMPLED.value.alias:
   116	                    resolved_order_by.append(
   117	                        OrderBy(
   118	                            Function("coalesce", [Column(resolved_column_or_none), -1]),
   119	                            direction=direction,
   120	                        )
   121	                    )
   122	                elif order_field_alias in (
   123	                    Columns.PROFILE_ID.value.alias,
   124	                    Columns.REPLAY_ID.value.alias,
   125	                ):
   126	                    resolved_order_by.append(
   127	                        OrderBy(
   128	                            Function(
   129	                                "if",
   130	                                [
   131	                                    Function("isNull", [Column(resolved_column_or_none)]),
   132	                                    0,
   133	                                    1,
   134	                                ],
   135	                            ),
   136	                            direction=direction,
   137	                        )
   138	                    )
   139	                elif order_field_alias == Columns.TIMESTAMP.value.alias:
   140	                    resolved_order_by.extend(
   141	                        [
   142	                            OrderBy(
   143	                                Function("toStartOfDay", [Column("timestamp")]),
   144	                                direction=direction,
   145	                            ),
   146	                            OrderBy(
   147	                                Column("timestamp"),
   148	                                direction=direction,
   149	                            ),
   150	                        ]
   151	                    )
   152	                else:
   153	                    resolved_order_by.append(
   154	                        OrderBy(Column(resolved_column_or_none), direction=direction)
   155	                    )
   156	
   157	        start, end = _prepare_start_end(
   158	            start,
   159	            end,
   160	            organization_id,
   161	            [group_id],
   162	        )
   163	
   164	        match_entity = Entity(dataset.value)
   165	        event_filters = [
   166	            Condition(Column(DATASETS[dataset][Columns.TIMESTAMP.value.alias]), Op.GTE, start),

   466	        """
   467	        Get an event given a project ID and event ID

   471	        event into a `GroupEvent`. Used as part of `post_process_group`.
   472	        """

   475	
   476	        if not event_id:
   477	            return None
   478	
   479	        event = Event(project_id=project_id, event_id=event_id)
   480	
   481	        # Return None if there was no data in nodestore
   482	        if len(event.data) == 0:
   483	            return None
   484

   487	            sentry_sdk.set_attribute("nodestore.event_type", event.get_event_type())

   490	            event.get_event_type() == "error"
   491	            or (event.get_event_type() == "transaction" and skip_transaction_groupevent)

   494	        elif occurrence_id is not None and group_id is not None:
   495	            event.group_id = group_id

   497	            event._snuba_data = {
   498	                "event_id": event_id,
   499	                "group_id": group_id,
   500	                "project_id": project_id,
   501	                "timestamp": event.timestamp,
   502	                "occurrence_id": occurrence_id,
   503	            }
   504	
   505	        elif event.get_event_type() != "transaction" or group_id:
   506	            # Load group_id from Snuba if not a transaction
   507	            raw_query_kwargs = {}
   508	            if event.datetime > timezone.now() - timedelta(hours=1):
   509	                # XXX: This is a hack to bust the snuba cache. We want to avoid the case where
   510	                # we cache an empty result, since this can result in us failing to fetch new events
   511	                # in some cases.
   512	                raw_query_kwargs["conditions"] = [
   513	                    [
   514	                        "timestamp",
   515	                        ">",
   516	                        datetime.fromtimestamp(random.randint(0, 1000000000)),
   517	                    ]
   518	                ]
   519	            dataset = (

   526	                filter_keys = {"project_id": [project_id], "event_id": [event_id]}
   527	                if group_id:

   529	                result = snuba.raw_query(
   530	                    dataset=dataset,
   531	                    selected_columns=self.__get_columns(dataset),
   532	                    start=event.datetime,
   533	                    end=event.datetime + timedelta(seconds=1),
   534	                    filter_keys=filter_keys,
   535	                    limit=1,
   536	                    referrer=Referrer.EVENTSTORE_GET_EVENT_BY_ID_NODESTORE.value,
   537	                    tenant_ids=tenant_ids,
   538	                    **raw_query_kwargs,
   539	                )

   541	                # this can happen due to races.  We silently want to hide
   542	                # this from callers.

   545	            # Return None if the event from Nodestore was not yet written to Snuba
   546	            if len(result["data"]) != 1:
   547	                logger.warning(
   548	                    "eventstore.missing-snuba-event",
   549	                    extra={
   550	                        "project_id": project_id,
   551	                        "event_id": event_id,
   552	                        "group_id": group_id,
   553	                        "event_datetime": event.datetime,
   554	                        "event_timestamp": event.timestamp,
   555	                        "nodestore_insert": event.data.get("nodestore_insert"),
   556	                        "received": event.data.get("received"),
   557	                        "len_data": len(result["data"]),

   559	                )
   560	                return None
   680	    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]] | None:
   681	        try:
   682	            try:
   683	                organization = Organization.objects.get_from_cache(id=organization_id)

   686	
   687	            projects = list(

   752	        if getattr(event, "occurrence", None) or event.get_event_type() == "generic":

   187	def pop_tag(data: dict[str, Any], key: str) -> None:
   188	    if "tags" not in data:
   189	        return
   190	
   191	    data["tags"] = [kv for kv in data["tags"] if kv is None or kv[0] != key]
   192	
   193	
   194	def set_tag(data: dict[str, Any], key: str, value: Any) -> None:
   195	    pop_tag(data, key)
   196	    if value is not None:
   197	        data.setdefault("tags", []).append((key, trim(value, MAX_TAG_VALUE_LENGTH)))
   198	
   199	
   200	def get_tag(data: dict[str, Any], key: str) -> Any | None:
   201	    for k, v in get_path(data, "tags", filter=True) or ():
   202	        if k == key:
   203	            return v
   204	    return None

   207	def sdk_metadata_from_event(event: Event) -> Mapping[str, Any]:
   208	    """
   209	    Returns a metadata dictionary with "sdk" field populated, including a normalized name
   210	    Returns {} when event type of event is known to not be SDK generated.
   211	    """
   212	
   213	    if event.get_event_type() in SECURITY_REPORT_INTERFACES:
   214	        return {}
   215	
   216	    if not (sdk_metadata := event.data.get("sdk")):
   217	        return {}
   218	
   219	    try:
   220	        return {
   440	        """
   441	        After normalizing and processing an event, save adjacent models such as
   442	        releases and environments to postgres and write the event into
   443	        eventstream. From there it will be picked up by Snuba and
   444	        post-processing.
   445	
   446	        We re-insert events with duplicate IDs into Snuba, which is responsible
   447	        for deduplicating events. Since deduplication in Snuba is on the primary
   448	        key (based on event ID, project ID and day), events with same IDs are only
   449	        deduplicated if their timestamps fall on the same day. The latest event
   450	        always wins and overwrites the value of events received earlier in that day.
   451	
   452	        Since we increment counters and frequencies here before events get inserted
   453	        to eventstream these numbers may be larger than the total number of
   454	        events if we receive duplicate event IDs that fall on the same day
   455	        (that do not hit cache first).
   456	        """
   457	
   458	        if project is None:
   459	            assert project_id is not None
   460	            project = resolve_project(project_id)
   461	        projects = {project.id: project}
   462	
   463	        # Normalize if needed
   464	        if not self._normalized:
   465	            if not assume_normalized:
   466	                self.normalize(project_id=project.id)
   467	            self._normalized = True
   468	
   469	        job: dict[str, Any] = {
   470	            "data": self._data,
   471	            "project_id": project.id,
   472	            "raw": raw,
   473	            "start_time": start_time,
   474	        }
   475	
   476	        # After calling _pull_out_data we get some keys in the job like the platform
   477	        _pull_out_data([job], projects)
   478	
   479	        # Sometimes projects get created without a platform (e.g. through the API), in which case we
   480	        # attempt to set it based on the first event
   481	        _set_project_platform_if_needed(project, job["event"])
   482	
   483	        event_type = self._data.get("type")
   484	        if event_type == "transaction":
   485	            job["data"]["project"] = project.id
   486	            jobs = save_transaction_events([job], projects, skip_send_first_transaction)
   487	            return jobs[0]["event"]
   488	        elif event_type == "generic":
   489	            job["data"]["project"] = project.id
   490	            jobs = save_generic_events([job], projects)
   491	            return jobs[0]["event"]
   492	        else:
   493	            project = job["event"].project
   494	            job["in_grouping_transition"] = is_in_transition(project)
   495	            metric_tags = {
   496	                "platform": job["event"].platform or "unknown",
   497	                "sdk": normalized_sdk_tag_from_event(job["event"].data),
   498	                "in_transition": job["in_grouping_transition"],
   499	                "split_enhancements": get_enhancements_version(project) == 3,
   500	            }
   501	            # This metric allows differentiating from all calls to the `event_manager.save` metric
   502	            # and adds support for differentiating based on platforms
   503	            with metrics.timer("event_manager.save_error_events", tags=metric_tags):
   504	                return self.save_error_events(
   505	                    project, job, projects, metric_tags, attachments or [], raw, cache_key
   506	                )
