   509	    def save_error_events(
   510	        self,
   511	        project: Project,
   512	        job: Job,
   513	        projects: ProjectsMapping,
   514	        metric_tags: MutableTags,
   515	        attachments: list[CachedAttachment],
   516	        raw: bool = False,
   517	        cache_key: str | None = None,
   518	    ) -> Event:
   519	        jobs = [job]
   520	
   521	        is_reprocessed = is_reprocessed_event(job["data"])
   522	
   523	        _get_or_create_release_many(jobs, projects)
   524	        _get_event_user_many(jobs, projects)
   525

   725	    for job in jobs:

   727	        if not data.get("release"):

   729	
   730	        project = projects[job["project_id"]]
   731	        date = job["event"].datetime
   732	
   733	        try:
   734	            release = Release.get_or_create(
   735	                project=project,
   736	                version=data["release"],
   737	                date_added=date,
   738	            )

   740	            logger.exception(
   741	                "Failed creating Release due to ValidationError",
   742	                extra={"project": project, "version": data["release"]},
   743	            )
   744	            release = None
   745	
   746	        job["release"] = release
   747	        if not release:
   748	            return
   749	
   750	        # Don't allow a conflicting 'release' tag
   751	        pop_tag(data, "release")
   752	        set_tag(data, "sentry:release", release.version)

   754	        if data.get("dist"):
   755	            job["dist"] = release.add_dist(data["dist"], date)
   756	
   757	            # don't allow a conflicting 'dist' tag

  1694	def _handle_regression(
  1695	    group: Group,
  1696	    event: BaseEvent,
  1697	    release: Release | None,
  1698	    incoming_group_values: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
  1699	) -> bool | None:
  1700	    if not group.is_resolved():
  1701	        return None
  1702	
  1703	    # we only mark it as a regression if the event's release is newer than
  1704	    # the release which we originally marked this as resolved
  1705	    elif GroupResolution.has_resolution(group, release):
  1706	        return None
  1707	
  1708	    elif has_pending_commit_resolution(group):
  1709	        return None
  1710	
  1711	    # we now think its a regression, rely on the database to validate that
  1712	    # no one beat us to this
  1713	    date = max(event.datetime, group.last_seen)
  1714	    is_regression = bool(
  1715	        Group.objects.filter(
  1716	            id=group.id,
  1717	            # ensure we can't update things if the status has been set to
  1718	            # ignored
  1719	            status__in=[GroupStatus.RESOLVED, GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED],
  1720	        )
  1721	        .exclude(
  1722	            # add to the regression window to account for races here
  1723	            active_at__gte=date - timedelta(seconds=5)
  1724	        )
  1725	        .update(
  1726	            active_at=date,
  1727	            # explicitly set last_seen here as ``is_resolved()`` looks
  1728	            # at the value
  1729	            last_seen=date,
  1730	            status=GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED,
  1731	            substatus=GroupSubStatus.REGRESSED,
  1732	        )
  1733	    )
  1734	
  1735	    group.active_at = date
  1736	    group.status = GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED
  1737	    group.substatus = GroupSubStatus.REGRESSED
  1738	    # groups may have been updated already from a separate event that groups to the same group
  1739	    # only fire these signals the first time the row was actually updated
  1740	    if is_regression:
  1741	        issue_unresolved.send_robust(
  1742	            project=group.project,
  1743	            user=None,
  1744	            group=group,
  1745	            transition_type="automatic",
  1746	            sender="handle_regression",
  1747	        )
  1748	        if not options.get("groups.enable-post-update-signal"):
  1749	            post_save.send_robust(
  1750	                sender=Group,
  2380	        filtered.append(attachment)
  2381	
  2382	    # Check if we have exceeded the stored crash reports count. If so, we
  2383	    # persist the current maximum (not the actual number!) into the cache. Next
  2384	    # time when loading from the cache, we will validate that this number has
  2385	    # not changed, or otherwise re-fetch from the database.
  2386	    if crashreports_exceeded(stored_reports, max_crashreports) and stored_reports > cached_reports:
  2387	        cache.set(crashreports_key, max_crashreports, CRASH_REPORT_TIMEOUT)
  2388	
  2389	    if refund_quantity:
  2390	        quotas.backend.refund(
  2391	            project,
  2392	            key=job["project_key"],
  2393	            timestamp=job["start_time"],
  2394	            category=DataCategory.ATTACHMENT,
  2395	            quantity=refund_quantity,
  2396	        )
  2397	
  2398	    return filtered

    75	def _should_send_error_created_hooks(project: Project) -> bool:
    76	    from sentry.models.organization import Organization
    77	    from sentry.sentry_apps.models.servicehook import ServiceHook
    78	
    79	    cache_key = f"servicehooks-error-created:1:{project.id}"
    80	    result = cache.get(cache_key)
    81	
    82	    if result is None:
    83	        org = Organization.objects.get_from_cache(id=project.organization_id)
    84	        if not features.has("organizations:integrations-event-hooks", organization=org):
    85	            cache.set(cache_key, 0, 60)
    86	            return False
    87	
    88	        result = (
    89	            ServiceHook.objects.filter(organization_id=org.id)
    90	            .extra(where=["events @> '{error.created}'"])
    91	            .exists()
    92	        )
    93	
    94	        cache_value = 1 if result else 0
    95	        cache.set(cache_key, cache_value, 60)
    96	
    97	    # We cache either 0 or 1; cast to bool is purely for typing.
    98	    return bool(result)

   101	def should_write_event_stats(event: Event | GroupEvent) -> bool:
   102	    # For now, we only want to write these stats for error events. If we start writing them for
   103	    # other event types we'll throw off existing stats and potentially cause various alerts to fire.
   104	    # We might decide to write these stats for other event types later, either under different keys
   105	    # or with differentiating tags.
   106	    return bool(
   107	        event.group
   108	        and event.group.issue_category == GroupCategory.ERROR
   109	        and event.group.platform is not None
   110	    )
   111	
   112	
   113	def format_event_platform(event: Event | GroupEvent) -> str | None:
   114	    if not event.group:
   115	        logger.error(
   116	            "Group not found on event during formatting", extra={"event_id": event.event_id}
   117	        )
   118	        return None
   119	    if not event.group.platform:
   120	        logger.error(
   121	            "Platform not found on group during formatting",
   122	            extra={"event_id": event.event_id, "group_id": event.group.id},
   123	        )
   124	        return None
   125	    platform = event.group.platform
   126	    return platform.split("-", 1)[0].split("_", 1)[0]
   127	
   128	
   129	def _capture_event_stats(event: Event) -> None:
   130	    if not should_write_event_stats(event):
   131	        return
   132	
   133	    platform = format_event_platform(event)
   134	    tags = {"platform": platform}
   135	    metrics.incr("events.processed", tags={"platform": platform}, skip_internal=False)
   136	    metrics.distribution("events.size.data", event.size, tags=tags, unit="byte")
   137	
   138	
   139	def _capture_group_stats(job: PostProcessJob) -> None:
   140	    event = job["event"]
   141	    if not job["group_state"]["is_new"] or not should_write_event_stats(event):
   142	        return
   143	
   144	    if not event.group:
   145	        logger.error(
   146	            "Group not found on event while capturing group stats",
   147	            extra={"event_id": event.event_id},
   148	        )
   149	        return
   150	
   151	    platform = format_event_platform(event)
   152	    metrics.incr("events.unique", tags={"platform": platform}, skip_internal=False)

   155	@sentry_sdk.trace
   156	def should_issue_owners_ratelimit(
   157	    project_id: int, group_id: int, organization_id: int | None
   158	) -> bool:
   159	    """
   160	    Make sure that we do not accept more groups than the enforced_limit at the project level.
   161	    """
   162	    from sentry.models.organization import Organization
   163	
   164	    enforced_limit = ISSUE_OWNERS_PER_PROJECT_PER_MIN_RATELIMIT
   165	    organization = Organization.objects.get_from_cache(id=organization_id)
   166	    if features.has("organizations:increased-issue-owners-rate-limit", organization=organization):
   167	        enforced_limit = HIGHER_ISSUE_OWNERS_PER_PROJECT_PER_MIN_RATELIMIT
   168	
   169	    cache_key = f"issue_owner_assignment_ratelimiter:{project_id}"
   170	    data = cache.get(cache_key)
   171	
   172	    if data is None:
   173	        groups = {group_id}
   174	        window_start = datetime.now()
   175	        cache.set(cache_key, (groups, window_start), 60)
   176	    else:
   177	        groups = set(data[0])
   178	        groups.add(group_id)
   179	        window_start = data[1]
   180	        timeout = max(60 - (datetime.now() - window_start).total_seconds(), 0)
   181	        cache.set(cache_key, (groups, window_start), timeout)
   182	
   183	    return len(groups) > enforced_limit

   188	def handle_owner_assignment(job: PostProcessJob) -> None:
   189	    """
   190	    The handle_owner_assignment task attempts to find issue owners for a group.
   191	    We call `ProjectOwnership.get_issue_owners` to find issue owners, and then
   192	    `handle_group_owners` to store them.
   193	
   194	    Before doing any work, we first do a few checks:
   195	
   196	    - If the issue has an assignee, we skip the task, as
   197	    if the issue is assigned, we do not need to do this logic.
   198	    - We check if we've attempted to find an issue owner in the last day.
   199	      - We cache the result of this check so we're not checking every issue event.
   200	    - We then check that the project has not gone over its rate limit for ownership evaluation.
   201	    - We also have a killswitch to disable this logic for a project, in case for whatever reason a
   202	      project is causing problems with the queue.
   203	
   204	    These checks are to protect the queue from being overwhelmed by one project, and also to prevent
   205	    one project to spam our Source code manager's (github, etc.) APIs.
   206	    """
   207	    if job["is_reprocessed"]:
   208	        return
   209	
   210	    from sentry.models.groupowner import (
   211	        ASSIGNEE_DOES_NOT_EXIST_DURATION,
   212	        ASSIGNEE_EXISTS_DURATION,
   213	        ASSIGNEE_EXISTS_KEY,
   214	        ISSUE_OWNERS_DEBOUNCE_DURATION,
   215	        ISSUE_OWNERS_DEBOUNCE_KEY,
   216	        GroupOwner,
   217	    )
   218	    from sentry.models.projectownership import ProjectOwnership
   219	
   220	    event = job["event"]

   457	def fetch_buffered_group_stats(group: Group) -> None:
   458	    """
   459	    Populates `times_seen_pending` with the number of buffered increments to `times_seen`
   460	    for this group. `times_seen_with_pending` can subsequently be used as the total times seen,
   461	    including the pending buffer updates.
   462	    """
   463	    from sentry import buffer
   464	    from sentry.models.group import Group
   465	
   466	    result = buffer.backend.get(Group, ["times_seen"], {"id": group.id})
   467	    group.times_seen_pending = result["times_seen"]

   473	def should_retry_fetch(attempt: int, e: Exception) -> bool:
   474	    from sentry.issues.occurrence_consumer import EventLookupError
   475	
   476	    return not attempt > MAX_FETCH_ATTEMPTS and (
   477	        isinstance(e, ServiceUnavailable) or isinstance(e, EventLookupError)

   484	@instrumented_task(
   485	    name="sentry.issues.tasks.post_process.post_process_group",

   490	def post_process_group(
   491	    is_new: bool,
   492	    is_regression: bool | None,
   493	    is_new_group_environment: bool,
   494	    cache_key: str | None,
   495	    group_id: int | None = None,
   496	    occurrence_id: str | None = None,
   497	    *,
   498	    project_id: int,
   499	    eventstream_type: str | None = None,
   500	    **kwargs: Any,
   501	) -> None:
   502	    """
   503	    Fires post processing hooks for a group.
   504	    """
   505	    from sentry.utils import snuba
   506	
   507	    with snuba.options_override({"consistent": True}):
   508	        from sentry.issues.occurrence_consumer import EventLookupError
   509	        from sentry.models.organization import Organization
   510	        from sentry.models.project import Project
   511	        from sentry.reprocessing2 import is_reprocessed_event
   512	        from sentry.services import eventstore
   513	        from sentry.services.eventstore.processing import event_processing_store
   514	
   515	        if occurrence_id is None:
   516	            # We use the data being present/missing in the processing store
   517	            # to ensure that we don't duplicate work should the forwarding consumers
   518	            # need to rewind history.
   519	            assert cache_key is not None
   520	            data = event_processing_store.get(cache_key)
   521	            if not data:
   522	                logger.info(
   523	                    "post_process.skipped",
   524	                    extra={"cache_key": cache_key, "reason": "missing_cache"},
   525	                )
   526	                return
   527	            with metrics.timer("tasks.post_process.delete_event_cache"):
   528	                event_processing_store.delete_by_key(cache_key)
   529	            occurrence = None
   530	            event = process_event(data, group_id)
   531	        else:
   532	            # Note: We attempt to acquire the lock here, but we don't release it and instead just
   533	            # rely on the ttl. The goal here is to make sure we only ever run post process group
   534	            # at most once per occurrence. Even though we don't use retries on the task, this is
   535	            # still necessary since the consumer that sends these might reprocess a batch.
   536	            # TODO: It might be better to instead set a value that we delete here, similar to what
   537	            # we do with `event_processing_store`. If we could do this *before* the occurrence ends
   538	            # up in Kafka (IE via the api that will sit in front of it), then we could guarantee at
   539	            # most once running of post process group.
   540	            lock = locks.get(
   541	                f"ppg:{occurrence_id}-once",
   542	                duration=600,

### src/sentry/integrations/utils/external_issues.py
     1	from __future__ import annotations
     2	
     3	import logging
     4	from typing import Any, TypedDict
     5	
     6	from sentry import features
     7	from sentry.models.group import Group
     8	from sentry.seer.signed_seer_api import (
     9	    LlmGenerateRequest,
    10	    SeerViewerContext,
    11	    make_llm_generate_request,
    12	)
    13	from sentry.services.eventstore.models import GroupEvent
    14	from sentry.users.models.user import User
    15	from sentry.users.services.user import RpcUser
    16	from sentry.utils import json
    17	from sentry.utils.safe import safe_execute
    18	
    19	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    20	
    21	SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are a helpful assistant that generates concise titles and descriptions for issue tickets in external project management tools like Jira, GitHub Issues, and Linear.
    22	
    23	Given information about a Sentry error (title and stack trace / error details), generate:
    24	1. A short, actionable title (3-8 words) suitable for a ticket. Describe the problem clearly.
    25	2. A brief description (1-3 sentences) summarizing the error, its likely cause, and potential impact.
    26	
    27	Do not include Sentry-specific formatting, links, or markdown. Keep the description in plain text.
    28	Return a JSON object with "title" and "description" keys. Return only the JSON, nothing else."""
    29	
    30	MAX_CONTEXT_LENGTH = 2000
    31	
    32	
    33	def _build_event_context(group: Group, event: Any | None = None) -> str:
    34	    if event is None:
    35	        event = group.get_latest_event()
    36	    title = group.title or ""
    37	    culprit = group.culprit or ""
    38	
    39	    body_parts: list[str] = []
    40	    if event:
    41	        for interface in event.interfaces.values():
    42	            output = safe_execute(interface.to_string, event)
    43	            if output:
    44	                body_parts.append(output)
    45	
    46	    event_body = "\n\n".join(body_parts)
    47	
    48	    context = f"Error Title: {title}"
    49	    if culprit:
    50	        context += f"\nCulprit: {culprit}"
    51	    if event_body:
    52	        context += f"\nDetails:\n{event_body}"
    53	
    54	    if len(context) > MAX_CONTEXT_LENGTH:
    55	        context = context[:MAX_CONTEXT_LENGTH]
    56	
    57	    return context
    58	
    59	
    60	class GeneratedExternalIssueDetails(TypedDict):
    61	    title: str | None
    62	    description: str | None
    63	
    64	
    65	def _make_generate_external_issue_details_request(
    66	    group: Group, event: Any | None = None, viewer_context: SeerViewerContext | None = None
    67	) -> GeneratedExternalIssueDetails | None:
    68	    logging_ctx: dict[str, Any] = {"group_id": group.id, "viewer_context": viewer_context}
    69	    context = _build_event_context(group, event=event)
    70	
    71	    body = LlmGenerateRequest(
    72	        provider="gemini",
    73	        model="flash",
    74	        referrer="sentry.external-issue.details-generate",
    75	        prompt=f"Generate a title and description for this Sentry error:\n\n{context}",
    76	        system_prompt=SYSTEM_PROMPT,
    77	        temperature=0.3,
    78	        max_tokens=750,
    79	        response_schema={
    80	            "type": "object",
    81	            "properties": {
    82	                "title": {"type": "string"},
    83	                "description": {"type": "string"},
    84	            },
    85	            "required": ["title", "description"],
    86	        },
    87	    )
    88	    response = make_llm_generate_request(body, timeout=10, viewer_context=viewer_context)
    89	    logging_ctx["status_code"] = response.status
    90	    if response.status >= 400:
    91	        logger.warning("external_issues.seer_request_failed", extra=logging_ctx)
    92	        return None
    93	
    94	    try:
    95	        data = response.json()
    96	    except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
    97	        logger.warning(
    98	            "external_issues.seer_response_json_failed", extra=logging_ctx, exc_info=True
    99	        )
   100	        return None
   101	    content = data.get("content")
   102	    try:
   103	        content = json.loads(content)
   104	    except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError, ValueError):
   105	        logger.warning(
   106	            "external_issues.seer_response_parse_failed", extra=logging_ctx, exc_info=True
   107	        )
   108	        return None
   109	
   110	    title = content.get("title")
   111	    description = content.get("description")
   112	    if title and description:
   113	        return {"title": title.strip(), "description": description.strip()}
   114	
   115	    logging_ctx["title"] = title
   116	    logging_ctx["description"] = description
   117	    logger.warning("external_issues.invalid_shape", extra=logging_ctx)
   118	    return None
   119	
   120	
   121	def maybe_generate_external_issue_details(
   122	    *, group: Group, user: User | RpcUser, event: GroupEvent | None = None
   123	) -> GeneratedExternalIssueDetails:
   124	    organization = group.organization
   125	    empty_result = GeneratedExternalIssueDetails(title=None, description=None)
   126	    if not features.has("organizations:gen-ai-features", organization, actor=user):
   127	        return empty_result
   128	    if organization.get_option("sentry:hide_ai_features", False):
   129	        return empty_result
   130	    if not features.has("organizations:external-issues-ai-generate", organization, actor=user):
   131	        return empty_result
   132	
   133	    try:
   134	        viewer_context = SeerViewerContext(organization_id=organization.id, user_id=user.id)
   135	        result = _make_generate_external_issue_details_request(
   136	            group, event=event, viewer_context=viewer_context
   137	        )
   138	    except Exception:
   139	        logger.error("external_issues.generate_issue_details_failed", exc_info=True)
   140	        return empty_result
   141	
   142	    if not result:
   143	        return empty_result
   144	
   145	    return result

    30	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    31	
    32	
    33	def get_resolutions_and_activity_data_for_groups(
    34	    affected_groups: Iterable[Group],
    35	    resolution_strategy: str | None,
    36	    activity_data: dict,
    37	    organization_id: int,
    38	):
    39	    activity_data = activity_data.copy()
    40	    resolutions_by_group_id = {}
    41	    activity_type = ActivityType.SET_RESOLVED
    42	    # If the resolution strategy is set to resolve in the next release or current release
    43	    if resolution_strategy in [
    44	        "resolve_next_release",
    45	        "resolve_current_release",
    46	    ]:
    47	        all_project_ids = list({group.project_id for group in affected_groups})
    48	        has_releases_for_each_project = all(
    49	            Release.objects.filter(
    50	                projects=project_id, organization_id=organization_id, status=ReleaseStatus.OPEN
    51	            ).exists()
    52	            for project_id in all_project_ids
    53	        )
    54	        logging_params = {
    55	            "has_releases_for_each_project": has_releases_for_each_project,
    56	            "resolution_strategy": resolution_strategy,
    57	            "organization_id": organization_id,
    58	        }
    59	        logger.info(
    60	            "get_resolutions_and_activity_data_for_groups.has_releases_for_each_project",
    61	            extra=logging_params,
    62	        )
    63	        if has_releases_for_each_project:
    64	            # found a release, we can proceed with non-dfeault resolutions
    65	            if resolution_strategy == "resolve_next_release":
    66	                activity_type = ActivityType.SET_RESOLVED_IN_RELEASE
    67	                activity_data["inNextRelease"] = True
    68	            elif resolution_strategy == "resolve_current_release":
    69	                activity_type = ActivityType.SET_RESOLVED_IN_RELEASE
    70	
    71	            for group in affected_groups:
    72	                local_logging_params = logging_params.copy()
    73	                # update the resolutions
    74	                # probably should be done within a single transaction with the status but this is fine for now
    75	                # note this logic is ported from src/sentry/api/helpers/group_index/update.py
    76	                # find the latest release by date for the project
    77	                last_release_by_date = (
    78	                    Release.objects.filter(
    79	                        projects=group.project,
    80	                        organization_id=organization_id,
    81	                        status=ReleaseStatus.OPEN,
    82	                    )
    83	                    .extra(select={"sort": "COALESCE(date_released, date_added)"})
    84	                    .order_by("-sort")
    85	                    .first()
    86	                )
    87	                # Check if semver versioning scheme is followed
    88	                follows_semver = follows_semver_versioning_scheme(
    89	                    org_id=organization_id,
    90	                    project_id=group.project.id,
    91	                    release_version=last_release_by_date.version if last_release_by_date else None,
    92	                )
    93	
    94	                local_logging_params.update(
    95	                    {
    96	                        "group_id": group.id,
    97	                        "last_release_by_date": (
    98	                            last_release_by_date.version if last_release_by_date else None
    99	                        ),
   100	                        "follows_semver": follows_semver,
   101	                    }
   102	                )
   103	                logger.info(
   104	                    "get_resolutions_and_activity_data_for_groups.last_release_by_date",
   105	                    extra=local_logging_params,
   106	                )
   107	
   108	                resolution_params = {
   109	                    "status": GroupStatus.RESOLVED,
   110	                    "release": last_release_by_date,  # Is this the right release?
   111	                    "type": (
   112	                        GroupResolution.Type.in_next_release
   113	                        if resolution_strategy == "resolve_next_release"
   114	                        else GroupResolution.Type.in_release
   115	                    ),
   116	                }
   117	
   118	                if resolution_strategy == "resolve_next_release":
   119	                    # get the current release version of the group if we are resolving in the next release
   120	                    current_release_version = get_current_release_version_of_group(
   121	                        group=group, follows_semver=follows_semver
   122	                    )
   123	
   124	                    resolution_params["current_release_version"] = current_release_version
   125	                    # if semver, set current_release_version in activity_data
   126	                    if follows_semver:
   127	                        activity_data.update({"current_release_version": current_release_version})
   128	                    else:
   129	                        try:
   130	                            current_release_obj = Release.objects.get(
   131	                                version=current_release_version,
   132	                                organization_id=organization_id,
   133	                            )
   134	
   135	                            # If we already know the `next` release in date based ordering
   136	                            # when clicking on `resolvedInNextRelease` because it is already
   137	                            # been released, there is no point in setting GroupResolution to
   138	                            # be of type in_next_release but rather in_release would suffice
   139	
   140	                            date_order_q = Q(date_added__gt=current_release_obj.date_added) | Q(
   141	                                date_added=current_release_obj.date_added,
   142	                                id__gt=current_release_obj.id,
   143	                            )
   144	                            # Find the next release after the current_release_version
   145	                            # i.e. the release that resolves the issue
   146	                            resolved_in_release = (
   147	                                Release.objects.filter(
   148	                                    date_order_q,
   149	                                    projects=group.project,
   150	                                    organization_id=organization_id,
   151	                                )
   152	                                .extra(select={"sort": "COALESCE(date_released, date_added)"})
   153	                                .order_by("sort", "id")[:1]
   154	                                .get()
   155	                            )
   156	                            resolution_params.update({"release": resolved_in_release})
   157	                            activity_data.update({"version": resolved_in_release.version})
   158	                            local_logging_params.update(
   159	                                {"resolved_in_release": resolved_in_release.version}
   160	                            )
   161	                            logger.info(
   162	                                "get_resolutions_and_activity_data_for_groups.resolved_in_release",
   163	                                extra=local_logging_params,
   164	                            )
   165	                        except Release.DoesNotExist:
   166	                            # If it gets here, it means we don't know the upcoming
   167	                            # release yet because it does not exist, and so we should
   168	                            # fall back to our current model
   169	                            logger.info(
   170	                                "get_resolutions_and_activity_data_for_groups.next_release_not_found",
   171	                                extra=local_logging_params,
   172	                            )
   173	
   174	                resolutions_by_group_id[group.id] = resolution_params
   175	
   176	    return resolutions_by_group_id, activity_type, activity_data
   177

   179	def group_was_recently_resolved(group: Group) -> bool:
   180	    """
   181	    Check if the group was resolved in the last 3 minutes
   182	    """
   183	    if group.status != GroupStatus.RESOLVED:
   184	        return False

   188	        return group_resolution.datetime > django_timezone.now() - timedelta(minutes=3)

   240	        "provider_key": provider.key,

   243

   311	                    )
   312	                )
   313	            except Exception as e:
   314	                sentry_sdk.capture_exception(e)
   315	
   316	    elif action == ResolveSyncAction.UNRESOLVE:
   317	        with action_context_scope(source=provider.key, actor=SYSTEM_ACTOR):
   318	            Group.objects.update_group_status(
   319	                groups=affected_groups,
   320	                status=GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED,
   321	                substatus=GroupSubStatus.ONGOING,
   322	                activity_type=ActivityType.SET_UNRESOLVED,
   323	                activity_data=activity_data,
   324	            )
   325	
   326	        for group in affected_groups:
   327	            issue_unresolved.send_robust(
   328	                project=group.project,
   329	                user=None,
   330	                group=group,
   331	                transition_type=provider.key,
   332	                sender=f"unresolved_with_{provider.key}",
   333	            )

     1	from taskbroker_client.retry import Retry
     2	
     3	from sentry import analytics, features
     4	from sentry.constants import ObjectStatus
     5	from sentry.exceptions import InvalidIdentity
     6	from sentry.integrations.analytics import IntegrationIssueStatusSyncedEvent
     7	from sentry.integrations.base import IntegrationInstallation
     8	from sentry.integrations.errors import OrganizationIntegrationNotFound
     9	from sentry.integrations.models.external_issue import ExternalIssue
    10	from sentry.integrations.models.integration import Integration
    11	from sentry.integrations.project_management.metrics import (
    12	    ProjectManagementActionType,
    13	    ProjectManagementEvent,
    14	)
    15	from sentry.integrations.services.integration import integration_service
    16	from sentry.models.group import Group, GroupStatus
    17	from sentry.shared_integrations.exceptions import ApiUnauthorized, IntegrationFormError
    18	from sentry.silo.base import SiloMode
    19	from sentry.tasks.base import instrumented_task, track_group_async_operation
    20	from sentry.taskworker.namespaces import integrations_tasks
    21	
    22	
    23	@instrumented_task(
    24	    name="sentry.integrations.tasks.sync_status_outbound",
    25	    namespace=integrations_tasks,
    26	    retry=Retry(times=5, delay=60 * 5, on=(Exception,), ignore=(Integration.DoesNotExist,)),
    27	    silo_mode=SiloMode.CELL,
    28	    silenced_exceptions=(Integration.DoesNotExist,),
    29	)
    30	@track_group_async_operation
    31	def sync_status_outbound(group_id: int, external_issue_id: int) -> bool | None:
    32	    groups = Group.objects.filter(
    33	        id=group_id, status__in=[GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED, GroupStatus.RESOLVED]
    34	    )
    35	    if not groups:
    36	        return False
    37	
    38	    group = groups[0]
    39	    has_issue_sync = features.has("organizations:integrations-issue-sync", group.organization)
    40	    if not has_issue_sync:
    41	        return False
    42	
    43	    try:
    44	        external_issue = ExternalIssue.objects.get(id=external_issue_id)
    45	    except ExternalIssue.DoesNotExist:
    46	        # Issue link could have been deleted while sync job was in the queue.
    47	        return None
    48	
    49	    integration = integration_service.get_integration(
    50	        integration_id=external_issue.integration_id, status=ObjectStatus.ACTIVE
    51	    )
    52	    if not integration:
    53	        return None
    54	    installation: IntegrationInstallation = integration.get_installation(
    55	        organization_id=external_issue.organization_id
    56	    )
    57	    if not (hasattr(installation, "should_sync") and hasattr(installation, "sync_status_outbound")):
    58	        return None
    59	
    60	    with ProjectManagementEvent(
    61	        action_type=ProjectManagementActionType.OUTBOUND_STATUS_SYNC, integration=integration
    62	    ).capture() as lifecycle:
    63	        lifecycle.add_extra("sync_task", "sync_status_outbound")
    64	        try:
    65	            if installation.should_sync("outbound_status"):
    66	                lifecycle.add_extras(
    67	                    {
    68	                        "organization_id": external_issue.organization_id,
    69	                        "integration_id": integration.id,
    70	                        "external_issue": external_issue_id,
    71	                        "status": group.status,
    72	                    }
    73	                )
    74	
    75	                installation.sync_status_outbound(
    76	                    external_issue, group.status == GroupStatus.RESOLVED, group.project_id
    77	                )
    78	
    79	                analytics.record(
    80	                    IntegrationIssueStatusSyncedEvent(
    81	                        provider=integration.provider,
    82	                        id=integration.id,
    83	                        organization_id=external_issue.organization_id,
    84	                    )
    85	                )
    86	        except (
    87	            IntegrationFormError,
    88	            ApiUnauthorized,
    89	            OrganizationIntegrationNotFound,
    90	            InvalidIdentity,
    91	        ) as e:
    92	            lifecycle.record_halt(halt_reason=e)
    93	            return None
    94	    return None

   573	    def handle_seer_autofix_start(

   575	        *,
   576	        slack_request: SlackActionRequest,
   577	        action: BlockKitMessageAction,

   582	            slack_request=slack_request,
   583	            action=action,
   584	            group=group,
   585	            organization_id=group.project.organization_id,
   586	        )
   587	        stopping_point = entrypoint.autofix_stopping_point
   588	        is_continuation = entrypoint.autofix_run_id is not None
   589	        logging_ctx = {
   590	            "group_id": group.id,
   591	            "organization_id": group.project.organization_id,
   592	            "stopping_point": str(stopping_point),
   593	            "is_continuation": is_continuation,
   594	            "user_id": user.id,
   595	        }
   596	        _logger.info("seer.slack.trigger_autofix.start", extra=logging_ctx)
   597	        lock_key = SlackAutofixEntrypoint.get_autofix_lock_key(
   598	            group_id=group.id,
   599	            stopping_point=stopping_point,
   600	        )
   601	        lock = locks.get(lock_key, duration=10, name="autofix_entrypoint_slack")
   602	        try:
   603	            with lock.acquire():
   604	                SeerAutofixOperator(entrypoint=entrypoint).trigger_autofix(
   605	                    group=group,
   606	                    user=user,
   607	                    stopping_point=stopping_point,
   608	                    run_id=entrypoint.autofix_run_id,
   609	                )
   610	        except UnableToAcquireLock:
   611	            # Might be a double click, or Seer is taking it's time confirming the run start.
   612	            # The entrypoint will handle removing the button once it starts the run anyway.
   613	            _logger.info("seer.slack.trigger_autofix.lock_contention", extra=logging_ctx)
   614	            return
   615	
   616	        _logger.info("seer.slack.trigger_autofix.complete", extra=logging_ctx)
   617	        metrics.incr(
   618	            "seer.slack.trigger_autofix",
   619	            tags={"stopping_point": str(stopping_point), "is_continuation": str(is_continuation)},
   620	        )

   647	    @classmethod
   648	    def get_action_option(cls, slack_request: SlackActionRequest) -> tuple[str | None, str | None]:
   649	        action_option, action_id = None, None
   650	        for action_data in slack_request.data.get("actions", []):
   651	            # Get the _first_ value in the action list.
   652	            value = action_data.get("value")
   653	
   654	            routing_data = decode_action_id(action_data.get("action_id", ""))
   655	            action_id = routing_data.action or None
   656	            if value:
   657	                action_option = value
   658	                break
   659	
   660	        return action_option, action_id
   661	
   662	    @classmethod
   663	    def get_action_list(cls, slack_request: SlackActionRequest) -> list[BlockKitMessageAction]:
   664	        action_data = slack_request.data.get("actions")
   665	        if not action_data or not isinstance(action_data, list):
   666	            return []
   667	
   668	        action_list = []
   669	        for action_data in action_data:
   670	            routing_data = decode_action_id(action_data.get("action_id", ""))
   671	            action_name = routing_data.action
   672	
   673	            if not action_name:
   674	                continue
   675	
   676	            if action_data.get("type") in ("static_select", "external_select"):
   677	                action = BlockKitMessageAction(
   678	                    name=action_name,
   679	                    label=action_data["selected_option"]["text"]["text"],
   680	                    type=action_data["type"],
   681	                    value=action_data["selected_option"]["value"],
   682	                    action_id=action_data["action_id"],
   683	                    block_id=action_data["block_id"],
   684	                    selected_options=[
   685	                        {"value": action_data.get("selected_option", {}).get("value")}
   686	                    ],
   687	                )
   688	                # TODO: selected_options is kinda ridiculous, I think this is built to handle multi-select?
   689	            else:
   690	                action = BlockKitMessageAction(
   691	                    name=action_name,
   692	                    label=action_data["text"]["text"],
   693	                    type=action_data["type"],
   694	                    value=action_data.get("value", ""),
   695	                    action_id=action_data["action_id"],
   696	                    block_id=action_data["block_id"],
   697	                )
   698	            action_list.append(action)
   699	
   700	        return action_list
   900	        return self.respond()

   903	class _ModalDialog(ABC):
   904	    @property
   905	    @abstractmethod
   906	    def dialog_type(self) -> str:
   907	        raise NotImplementedError
   908	
   909	    def _build_format_options(self, options: dict[str, str]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   910	        return [
   911	            {
   912	                "text": {
   913	                    "type": "plain_text",
   914	                    "text": text,
   915	                    "emoji": True,
   916	                },
   917	                "value": value,
   918	            }
   919	            for text, value in options.items()
   920	        ]
   921	
   922	    def build_modal_payload(
   923	        self,
   924	        title: str,
   925	        action_text: str,
   926	        options: dict[str, str],
   927	        initial_option_text: str,
   928	        initial_option_value: str,
   929	        callback_id: str,
   930	        metadata: str,
   931	    ) -> View:
   932	        formatted_options = self._build_format_options(options)
   933	
   934	        return View(
   935	            type="modal",
   936	            title={"type": "plain_text", "text": f"{title} Issue"},
   937	            blocks=[
   938	                {
   939	                    "type": "section",
   940	                    "text": {"type": "mrkdwn", "text": action_text},
   941	                    "accessory": {
   942	                        "type": "static_select",
   943	                        "initial_option": {
   944	                            "text": {
   945	                                "type": "plain_text",
   946	                                "text": initial_option_text,
   947	                                "emoji": True,
   948	                            },
   949	                            "value": initial_option_value,
   950	                        },
   951	                        "options": formatted_options,
   952	                        "action_id": "static_select-action",
   953	                    },
   954	                }
   955	            ],
   956	            close={"type": "plain_text", "text": "Cancel"},
   957	            submit={"type": "plain_text", "text": title},
   958	            private_metadata=metadata,
   959	            callback_id=callback_id,
   960	        )
   961	
   962	    @abstractmethod
   963	    def get_modal_payload(self, callback_id: str, metadata: str) -> View:
   964	        raise NotImplementedError
   965	
   966	    def _update_modal(
   967	        self,
   968	        slack_client: SlackSdkClient,
   969	        external_id: str,
   970	        modal_payload: View,
   971	        slack_request: SlackActionRequest,
   972	    ) -> None:
   973	        try:
   974	            slack_client.views_update(
   975	                external_id=external_id,
   976	                view=modal_payload,
   977	            )
   978	        except SlackApiError as e:
   979	            # If the external_id is not found, Slack we send `not_found` error
   980	            # https://api.slack.com/methods/views.update
   981	            if unpack_slack_api_error(e) == MODAL_NOT_FOUND:
   982	                logging_data = slack_request.get_logging_data()
   983	                _logger.info(
   984	                    "slack.action.update-modal-not-found",
   985	                    extra={
   986	                        **logging_data,
   987	                        "dialog": self.dialog_type,
   988	                    },
   989	                )
   990	                # The modal was not found, so we need to open a new one
   991	                self._open_modal(slack_client, modal_payload, slack_request)
   992	            else:
   993	                raise
   994	
   995	    def _open_modal(
   996	        self, slack_client: SlackSdkClient, modal_payload: View, slack_request: SlackActionRequest
   997	    ) -> None:
   998	        # Error handling is done in the calling function
   999	        slack_client.views_open(
  1000	            trigger_id=slack_request.data["trigger_id"],
  1001	            view=modal_payload,
  1002	        )
  1003	
  1004	    def open_dialog(self, slack_request: SlackActionRequest, group: Group) -> None:
  1005	        # XXX(epurkhiser): In order to update the original message we have to
  1006	        # keep track of the response_url in the callback_id. Definitely hacky,
  1007	        # but seems like there's no other solutions [1]:
  1008	        #
  1009	        # [1]: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/46629852/update-a-bot-message-after-responding-to-a-slack-dialog#comment80795670_46629852
  1010	        org = group.project.organization
  1011	        callback_id_dict = {
  1012	            "issue": group.id,
  1013	            "orig_response_url": slack_request.data["response_url"],
  1014	            "is_message": _is_message(slack_request.data),
  1015	            "rule": slack_request.callback_data.get("rule"),
  1016	        }
  1017	
  1018	        if slack_request.data.get("channel"):
  1019	            callback_id_dict["channel_id"] = slack_request.data["channel"]["id"]
  1020	        callback_id = orjson.dumps(callback_id_dict).decode()
  1021	
  1022	        # only add tags to metadata
  1023	        metadata_dict = callback_id_dict.copy()
  1024	        metadata_dict["tags"] = list(slack_request.get_tags())
  1025	        metadata = orjson.dumps(metadata_dict).decode()
  1026	
  1027	        # XXX(CEO): the second you make a selection (without hitting Submit) it sends a slightly different request
  1028	        modal_payload = self.get_modal_payload(callback_id, metadata=metadata)
  1029	        slack_client = SlackSdkClient(integration_id=slack_request.integration.id)
  1030	        try:
  1031	            # We need to use the action_ts as the external_id to update the modal
  1032	            # We passed this in control when we sent the loading modal to beat the 3 second timeout
  1033	            external_id = slack_request.get_action_ts()
  1034	
  1035	            if not external_id:
  1036	                # If we don't have an external_id or option is disabled we need to open a new modal
  1037	                self._open_modal(slack_client, modal_payload, slack_request)
  1038	            else:
  1039	                self._update_modal(slack_client, external_id, modal_payload, slack_request)
  1040

   107	def build_assigned_text(identity: RpcIdentity, assignee: str) -> str | None:

   109	
   110	    try:
   111	        assigned_actor = actor.resolve()
   112	    except ObjectDoesNotExist:
   113	        return None
   114	
   115	    if isinstance(assigned_actor, Team):
   116	        assignee_text = f"#{assigned_actor.slug}"
   117	    elif isinstance(assigned_actor, RpcUser):
   118	        assignee_identity = identity_service.get_identity(
   119	            filter={
   120	                "provider_id": identity.idp_id,
   121	                "user_id": assigned_actor.id,
   122	            }
   123	        )
   124	        assignee_text = (
   125	            assigned_actor.get_display_name()
   126	            if assignee_identity is None
   127	            else f"<@{assignee_identity.external_id}>"

   135	def build_action_text(
   136	    identity: RpcIdentity, action: MessageAction | BlockKitMessageAction
   137	) -> str | None:
   138	    if action.name == "assign":
   139	        selected_options = action.selected_options or []
   140	        if not len(selected_options):
   141	            return None
   142	        assignee = selected_options[0]["value"]
   143	        return build_assigned_text(identity, assignee)
   144	
   145	    # Resolve actions have additional 'parameters' after ':'
   146	    status = STATUSES.get((action.value or "").split(":", 1)[0])
   147	    status = "archived" if status == "ignored" else status
   148	    # Action has no valid action text, ignore
   149	    if not status:
   150	        return None
   151	
   152	    return f"*Issue {status} by <@{identity.external_id}>*"

   155	def format_release_tag(value: str, event: Event | GroupEvent | None) -> str:
   156	    """Format the release tag using the short version and make it a link"""
   157	    if not event:
   158	        return ""
   159	
   160	    path = f"/releases/{value}/"
   161	    url = event.project.organization.absolute_url(path)
   162	    release_description = parse_release(value, json_loads=orjson.loads).get("description")
   163	    return f"<{url}|{release_description}>"

   166	def get_tags(
   167	    event_for_tags: Event | GroupEvent | None,
   168	    tags: set[str] | list[tuple[str]] | None = None,
   169	) -> Sequence[Mapping[str, str | bool]]:
   170	    """Get tag keys and values for block kit"""
   171	    fields = []
   172	    if not tags:
   173	        tags = set()
   174	
   175	    # XXX(CEO): context is passing tags as a list of tuples from self.event.tags
   176	    # we should standardize but it might break other notifications
   177	    if tags and isinstance(tags, list):
   178	        tags = set(tags[0])
   179	
   180	    if tags:
   181	        event_tags = event_for_tags.tags if event_for_tags else []
   182	        for key, value in event_tags:
   183	            std_key = tagstore.backend.get_standardized_key(key)
   184	            if std_key not in tags:

   186	            labeled_value = tagstore.backend.get_tag_value_label(key, value)
   187	            if std_key == "release":

   189	            fields.append(
   190	                {
   191	                    "title": std_key,
   192	                    "value": labeled_value,
   193	                }
   194	            )
   195	    return fields

   201	    context = group.issue_type.notification_config.context.copy()
   202	
   203	    # for errors, non-regression performance, and rage click issues

   206	
   207	    state = None
   208	    event_count = None
   209	    if NotificationContextField.STATE in context:

   211	    if NotificationContextField.EVENTS in context:
   212	        event_count = SUPPORTED_CONTEXT_DATA[NotificationContextField.EVENTS](group, rules)
   213	
   214	    if (state and state == "New") or (event_count and int(event_count) <= 1):
   215	        if NotificationContextField.EVENTS in context:
   216	            context.remove(NotificationContextField.EVENTS)
   217	
   218	        # avoid hitting Snuba for user count if we don't need it
   219	        if NotificationContextField.USERS_AFFECTED in context:

   247	        option_groups.append(team_option_group)

   258	def get_suggested_assignees(
   259	    project: Project, event: Event | GroupEvent, current_assignee: RpcUser | Team | None
   260	) -> list[str]:
   261	    """Get suggested assignees as a list of formatted strings"""
   262	    suggested_assignees, _ = ProjectOwnership.get_owners(project.id, event.data)
   263	    try:
   264	        suspect_commit_users = Actor.many_from_object(get_suspect_commit_users(project, event))
   265	        suggested_assignees.extend(suspect_commit_users)
   266	    except (Release.DoesNotExist, Commit.DoesNotExist):
   267	        logger.info("Skipping suspect committers because release does not exist.")
   268	    except Exception:
   269	        logger.warning("Could not get suspect committers. Continuing execution.")
   270	
   271	    if suggested_assignees:
   272	        suggested_assignees = dedupe_suggested_assignees(suggested_assignees)
   273	        assignee_texts = []
   274	
   275	        for assignee in suggested_assignees:
   276	            # skip over any suggested assignees that are the current assignee of the issue, if there is any
   277	            if assignee.is_team and not (
   278	                isinstance(current_assignee, Team) and assignee.id == current_assignee.id
   279	            ):
   280	                assignee_texts.append(f"#{assignee.slug}")
   281	            elif assignee.is_user and not (
   282	                isinstance(current_assignee, RpcUser) and assignee.id == current_assignee.id
   283	            ):
   284	                assignee_as_user = assignee.resolve()
   285	                if isinstance(assignee_as_user, RpcUser):
   286	                    assignee_texts.append(assignee_as_user.get_display_name())
   287	        return assignee_texts
   288	    return []

   524	            }
   525	            footer_text = ""
   526	            for k, v in footer_data.items():
   527	                footer_text += f"{k}: {v}    "
   528	
   529	            if replay_link:
   530	                footer_text += replay_link
   531	            else:
   532	                footer_text = footer_text[:-4]  # chop off the empty space
   533	
   534	            return self.get_context_block(text=footer_text)
   535	        else:
   536	            return self.get_context_block(text=footer, timestamp=timestamp)
   537	
   538	    def get_slack_app_update_nudge_block(self) -> SlackBlock:
   539	        org = self.group.organization
   540	
   541	        # app_mentions:read is mandatory for every new Slack app installation, so its
   542	        # presence tells us the app is up to date.
   543	        # groups/channels:history scopes are optional, so they may not be present in
   544	        # new Slack app installations.
   545	        if self.has_mentions_read_scope:
   546	            nudge_text = "Mention or tag Sentry to investigate issues more deeply."
   547	            nudge_type = "mention_reminder"
   548	        else:
   549	            reinstall_url = org.absolute_url(
   550	                f"/settings/{org.slug}/integrations/slack/",
   551	                query="showInstallModal=1",
   552	            )
   553	            nudge_text = f"Ask Sentry questions and debug faster, <{reinstall_url}|reinstall Sentry Slack app>."
   554	            nudge_type = "reinstall"
   555	        record_nudge_metric("sent", nudge_type=nudge_type)
   556	        return self.get_context_block(text=nudge_text)
   557	
   558	    def build_description_block(self, description_text: str) -> SlackBlock | None:
   559	        text = description_text.lstrip(" ")
   560	        # XXX(CEO): sometimes text is " " and slack will error if we pass an empty string (now "")
   561	        return self.get_text_block(text) if text else None
   562	
   563	    def build_group_context_block(self, suggested_assignees: list[str]) -> SlackBlock | None:
   564	        """Combine stats (events, users, state, first seen) with suggested assignees in one context block."""
   565	        context_text = get_context(self.group, self.rules)
   566	
   567	        if suggested_assignees:
   568	            truncated_assignees = suggested_assignees[:MAX_SUGGESTED_ASSIGNEES]
   569	            suggested_text = ", ".join(truncated_assignees)
   570	            context_text += f"   Suggested: {suggested_text}"
   571	
   572	        context_text = context_text.strip()
   573	        if not context_text:
   574	            return None
   575	
   576	        return self.get_context_block(context_text)
   577	
   578	    def build(self, notification_uuid: str | None = None) -> SlackBlock:
   579	        # XXX(dcramer): options are limited to 100 choices, even when nested
   580	        text = build_attachment_text(self.group, self.event) or ""
   581	        text = text.strip(" \n")
   582	
   583	        text = escape_slack_markdown_text(text)
   584	
   585	        project = Project.objects.get_from_cache(id=self.group.project_id)
   586	
   587	        # If an event is unspecified, use the tags of the latest event (if one exists).
   588	        event_for_tags = self.event or self.group.get_latest_event()
   589	
   590	        event_or_group: Group | Event | GroupEvent = (
   591	            self.event if self.event is not None else self.group
   592	        )
   593	
   594	        action_text = ""
   595	
   596	        if not self.issue_details or (self.recipient and self.recipient.is_team):
   597	            payload_actions, action_text, has_action = build_actions(
   598	                self.group, project, text, self.actions, self.identity
   599	            )
   600	        else:
   601	            payload_actions = []
   602	            has_action = False
   603	
   604	        rule_id = None
   605	        rule_environment_id = None
   606	        key = "legacy_rule_id"
   607	        if self.rules:
   608	            key, value = get_rule_or_workflow_id(self.rules[0])
   609	            rule_id = int(value)
   610	
   611	            match key:
   612	                case "workflow_id":
   613	                    workflow = Workflow.objects.filter(id=rule_id).first()
   614	                    rule_environment_id = workflow.environment_id if workflow else None
   615	                case "legacy_rule_id":
   616	                    rule = Rule.objects.filter(id=rule_id).first()
   617	                    rule_environment_id = rule.environment_id if rule else None
   618	
   619	        # build up actions text
   620	        if self.actions and self.identity and not action_text:
   621	            # this means somebody is interacting with the message
   622	            action_text = get_action_text(self.actions, self.identity)
   623	            has_action = True
   624	
   625	        title_link = None
   626	        match key:
   627	            case "workflow_id":
   628	                title_link = get_title_link_workflow_engine_ui(
   629	                    self.group,
   630	                    self.event,
   631	                    self.link_to_event,
   632	                    self.issue_details,
   633	                    self.notification,
   634	                    ExternalProviders.SLACK,
   635	                    rule_id,
   636	                    rule_environment_id,
   637	                    notification_uuid=notification_uuid,
   638	                )
   639	            case "legacy_rule_id":
   640	                title_link = get_title_link(
   641	                    self.group,
   642	                    self.event,
   643	                    self.link_to_event,
   644	                    self.issue_details,
   645	                    self.notification,
   646	                    ExternalProviders.SLACK,
   647	                    rule_id,
   648	                    rule_environment_id,
   649	                    notification_uuid=notification_uuid,
   650	                )
   651	
   652	        blocks = [self.get_title_block(event_or_group, has_action, title_link)]
   653	
   654	        if culprit_block := self.get_culprit_block(event_or_group):
   655	            blocks.append(culprit_block)
   656	
   657	        if description_block := self.build_description_block(text):
   658	            blocks.append(description_block)
   659	
   660	        # set up block id
   661	        block_id = {"issue": self.group.id}
   662	        if rule_id:
   663	            block_id["rule"] = rule_id
   664	
   665	        # build tags block
   666	        tags = get_tags(event_for_tags=event_for_tags, tags=self.tags)
   667	        if tags:
   668	            blocks.append(self.get_tags_block(tags, block_id))
   669	
   670	        try:
   671	            assignee = self.group.get_assignee()
   672	        except Actor.InvalidActor:
   673	            assignee = None
   674	
   675	        suggested_assignees = []
   676	        if event_for_tags:
   677	            suggested_assignees = get_suggested_assignees(
   678	                self.group.project, event_for_tags, assignee
   679	            )
   680	
   681	        if group_context_block := self.build_group_context_block(
   682	            suggested_assignees=suggested_assignees
   683	        ):
   684	            blocks.append(group_context_block)
   685	
   686	        # If an action has been taken, add the text for it (e.g. "Issue resolved by <@U0234567890>")
   687	        if self.actions:
   688	            blocks.append(self.get_markdown_block(action_text))
   689	
   690	        # build actions
   691	        actions = []
   692	        for action in payload_actions:
   693	            if action.label in (
   694	                "Archive",
   695	                "Ignore",
   696	                "Mark as Ongoing",
   697	                "Stop Ignoring",
   698	                "Resolve",
   699	                "Unresolve",
   700	                "Resolve...",

   122	def _webhook_event_data(
   123	    event: Event | GroupEvent, group_id: int, project_id: int
   124	) -> dict[str, Any]:
   125	    from sentry.api.serializers.rest_framework import convert_dict_key_case, snake_to_camel_case
   126	
   127	    project = Project.objects.get_from_cache(id=project_id)
   128	    organization = Organization.objects.get_from_cache(id=project.organization_id)
   129	
   130	    event_context = event.as_dict()
   131	    event_context["url"] = absolute_uri(
   132	        reverse(
   133	            "sentry-api-0-project-event-details",
   134	            args=[project.organization.slug, project.slug, event.event_id],
   135	        )
   136	    )
   137	
   138	    event_context["web_url"] = absolute_uri(
   139	        reverse(
   140	            "sentry-organization-event-detail", args=[organization.slug, group_id, event.event_id]
   141	        )
   142	    )
   143	    if hasattr(event, "occurrence") and event.occurrence is not None:
   144	        event_context["occurrence"] = convert_dict_key_case(
   145	            event.occurrence.to_dict(), snake_to_camel_case
   146	        )
   147	
   148	    # The URL has a regex OR in it ("|") which means `reverse` cannot generate
   149	    # a valid URL (it can't know which option to pick). We have to manually
   150	    # create this URL for, that reason.
   151	    event_context["issue_url"] = absolute_uri(
   152	        f"/api/0/organizations/{organization.slug}/issues/{group_id}/"
   153	    )
   154	    event_context["issue_id"] = str(group_id)
   155	    return event_context

   173	        **serialized_group,
   174	    }
   175	    return cast(WebhookGroupResponse, webhook_payload)

   178	@instrumented_task(

   191	def send_alert_webhook_v2(
   192	    rule_label: str,
   193	    sentry_app_id: int,
   194	    instance_id: str,
   195	    group_id: int,
   196	    occurrence_id: str | None = None,
   197	    additional_payload_key: str | None = None,
   198	    additional_payload: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
   199	    **kwargs: Any,
   200	):
   201	    with SentryAppInteractionEvent(
   202	        operation_type=SentryAppInteractionType.PREPARE_WEBHOOK,
   203	        event_type=SentryAppEventType.EVENT_ALERT_TRIGGERED,
   204	    ).capture() as lifecycle:
   205	        group = Group.objects.get_from_cache(id=group_id)
   206	        assert group, "Group must exist to get related attributes"
   207	        project = Project.objects.get_from_cache(id=group.project_id)
   208	        organization = Organization.objects.get_from_cache(id=project.organization_id)
   209	        extra: dict[str, int | str] = {
   210	            "sentry_app_id": sentry_app_id,
   211	            "project_id": project.id,
   212	            "organization_slug": organization.slug,
   213	            "rule": rule_label,
   214	        }
   215	        lifecycle.add_extras(extra)
   216	
   217	        sentry_app = app_service.get_sentry_app_by_id(id=sentry_app_id)
   218	        if sentry_app is None:
   219	            raise SentryAppSentryError(message=SentryAppWebhookFailureReason.MISSING_SENTRY_APP)
   220	
   240	            BaseEvent.generate_node_id(project_id=project.id, event_id=instance_id)
   241	        )
   242	
   243	        if not nodedata:
   244	            raise SentryAppSentryError(message=SentryAppWebhookFailureReason.MISSING_EVENT)
   245	
   246	        occurrence = None
   247	        if occurrence_id:
   248	            occurrence = IssueOccurrence.fetch(occurrence_id, project_id=project.id)
   249	
   250	            if not occurrence:
   251	                raise SentryAppSentryError(
   252	                    message=SentryAppWebhookFailureReason.MISSING_ISSUE_OCCURRENCE
   253	                )
   254	
   255	        group_event = GroupEvent(
   256	            project_id=project.id,
   257	            event_id=instance_id,
   258	            group=group,
   259	            data=nodedata,
   260	            occurrence=occurrence,
   261	        )
   262	
   263	        event_context = _webhook_event_data(group_event, group.id, project.id)
   264	
   265	        data = {"event": event_context, "triggered_rule": rule_label}
   266	
   267	        # Attach extra payload to the webhook
   268	        if additional_payload_key and additional_payload:
   269	            data[additional_payload_key] = additional_payload
   270	
   271	        request_data = AppPlatformEvent(
   272	            resource=SentryAppResourceType.EVENT_ALERT,
   273	            action=IssueAlertActionType.TRIGGERED,
   274	            install=install,
   275	            data=data,
   276	        )
   277	
   278	    send_and_save_webhook_request(sentry_app, request_data)
   279	
   280	    # On success, record analytic event for Alert Rule UI Component
   281	    if request_data.data.get("issue_alert"):
   282	        try:
   283	            analytics.record(
   284	                AlertRuleUiComponentWebhookSentEvent(
   285	                    organization_id=organization.id,
   286	                    sentry_app_id=sentry_app_id,
   287	                    event=SentryAppEventType.EVENT_ALERT_TRIGGERED,
   288	                )
   289	            )
   290	        except Exception as e:
   291	            sentry_sdk.capture_exception(e)

   294	def _process_resource_change(
   295	    *,
   296	    action: str,
   297	    sender: str,
   298	    instance_id: str,
   299	    **kwargs: Any,
   300	) -> None:
   301	    # The class is serialized as a string when enqueueing the class.
   302	    model: type[Event] | type[Model] = TYPES[sender]
   303	    instance: Event | Model | None = None
   304	    # Make the event name first so we can add to metric tag
   305	    if sender == "Error":
   306	        name = sender.lower()
   307	    else:
   308	        # Some resources are named differently than their model. eg. Group vs Issue.
   309	        # Looks up the human name for the model. Defaults to the model name.
   310	        name = RESOURCE_RENAMES.get(model.__name__, model.__name__.lower())
   311	
   312	    event = SentryAppEventType(f"{name}.{action}")
   313	    with SentryAppInteractionEvent(
   314	        operation_type=SentryAppInteractionType.PREPARE_WEBHOOK,
   315	        event_type=event,
   316	    ).capture():
   317	        project_id: int | None = kwargs.get("project_id", None)
   318	        group_id: int | None = kwargs.get("group_id", None)
   319	
   320	        if sender == "Error" and project_id and group_id:
   321	            # Read event from nodestore as Events are heavy in task messages.
   322	            nodedata = nodestore.backend.get(Event.generate_node_id(project_id, instance_id))
   323	            if not nodedata:
   324	                raise SentryAppSentryError(
   325	                    message=f"{SentryAppWebhookFailureReason.MISSING_EVENT}",
   326	                )
   327	            instance = Event(
   328	                project_id=project_id, group_id=group_id, event_id=instance_id, data=nodedata
   329	            )
   330	
   331	        # We may run into a race condition where this task executes before the
   332	        # transaction that creates the Group has committed.
   333	        if not issubclass(model, Event):
   334	            try:
   335	                instance = model.objects.get(id=instance_id)
   336	            except model.DoesNotExist as e:
   337	                # Explicitly requeue the task, so we don't report this to Sentry until
   338	                # we hit the max number of retries.
   339	                retry_task(e)
   340	
   500	    try:
   501	        sentry_app = SentryApp.objects.get(id=sentry_app_id)
   502	    except SentryApp.DoesNotExist:
   503	        return
   504	
   505	    # When a sentry app's definition changes purge cache for all the installations.
   506	    # This could get slow for large applications, but generally big applications don't change often.
   507	    install_query = SentryAppInstallation.objects.filter(
   508	        sentry_app=sentry_app,
   509	    ).values("id", "organization_id")
   510	
   511	    # There isn't a constraint on org : sentryapp so we have to handle lists
   512	    install_map: dict[int, list[int]] = defaultdict(list)
   513	    for install_row in install_query:
   514	        install_map[install_row["organization_id"]].append(install_row["id"])
   515	
   516	    # Clear application_id cache
   517	    cell_caching_service.clear_key(
   518	        key=get_by_application_id.key_from(sentry_app.application_id), cell_name=cell_name
   519	    )
   520	
   521	    # Limit our operations to the cell this outbox is for.
   522	    # This could be a single query if we use raw_sql.
   523	    cell_query = OrganizationMapping.objects.filter(
   524	        organization_id__in=list(install_map.keys()), cell_name=cell_name
   525	    ).values("organization_id")
   526	    for cell_row in cell_query:
   527	        cell_caching_service.clear_key(
   528	            key=get_installations_for_organization.key_from(cell_row["organization_id"]),
   529	            cell_name=cell_name,
   530	        )
   531	        installs = install_map[cell_row["organization_id"]]
   532	        for install_id in installs:
   533	            cell_caching_service.clear_key(
   534	                key=get_installation.key_from(install_id), cell_name=cell_name
   535	            )
   536	
   537	
   538	@instrumented_task(
   539	    name="sentry.sentry_apps.tasks.sentry_apps.workflow_notification",
   540	    namespace=sentryapp_tasks,
   541	    retry=Retry(
   542	        times=3,
   543	        delay=60 * 5,
   544	        on=_SENTRY_APP_WEBHOOK_RETRY_ON,

   551	def workflow_notification(
   552	    installation_id: int, issue_id: int, type: str, user_id: int | None, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any
   553	) -> None:
   554	    event = SentryAppEventType(f"issue.{type}")
   555	    with SentryAppInteractionEvent(
   556	        operation_type=SentryAppInteractionType.PREPARE_WEBHOOK,
   557	        event_type=event,
   558	    ).capture():
   559	        webhook_data = get_webhook_data(installation_id, issue_id, user_id)
   560	
   561	        install, issue, user = webhook_data
   562	        data = kwargs.get("data", {})
   563	        data.update({"issue": serialize(issue)})
   564	
   565	    send_webhooks(installation=install, event=event, data=data, actor=user)
   566	
   567	    analytics_event: analytics.Event | None = None
   568	    if event == SentryAppEventType.ISSUE_ASSIGNED:
   569	        analytics_event = SentryAppIssueAssigned(
   570	            user_id=user_id,
   571	            group_id=issue_id,
   572	            installation_id=installation_id,
   573	        )
   574	    elif event == SentryAppEventType.ISSUE_CREATED:
   575	        analytics_event = SentryAppIssueCreated(
   576	            user_id=user_id,
   577	            group_id=issue_id,
   578	            installation_id=installation_id,
   579	        )
   580	    elif event == SentryAppEventType.ISSUE_IGNORED:
   581	        analytics_event = SentryAppIssueIgnored(
   582	            user_id=user_id,
   583	            group_id=issue_id,
   584	            installation_id=installation_id,
   585	        )
   586	    elif event == SentryAppEventType.ISSUE_RESOLVED:
   587	        analytics_event = SentryAppIssueResolved(
   588	            user_id=user_id,
   589	            group_id=issue_id,
   590	            installation_id=installation_id,
   591	        )
   592	    elif event == SentryAppEventType.ISSUE_UNRESOLVED:
   593	        analytics_event = SentryAppIssueUnresolved(
   594	            user_id=user_id,
   595	            group_id=issue_id,
   596	            installation_id=installation_id,
   597	        )
   598	
   599	    if analytics_event is not None:
   600	        analytics.record(analytics_event)

   696	                message=f"workflow_notification.{SentryAppWebhookFailureReason.MISSING_USER}",
   697	            )

   699	    return (install, issue, user)

   975	@instrumented_task(
   976	    name="sentry.sentry_apps.tasks.sentry_apps.send_metric_alert_webhook",
   977	    namespace=sentryapp_tasks,
   978	    retry=Retry(
   979	        times=3,
   980	        delay=60 * 5,
   981	        on=_SENTRY_APP_WEBHOOK_RETRY_ON,
   982	        ignore=_SENTRY_APP_WEBHOOK_RETRY_IGNORE,
   983	    ),
   984	    silo_mode=SiloMode.CELL,
   985	    silenced_exceptions=_SENTRY_APP_WEBHOOK_SILENCED,
   986	)
   987	def send_metric_alert_webhook(
   988	    sentry_app_id: int,
   989	    new_status: int,
   990	    incident_attachment_json: str,
   991	    organization_id: int,
   992	    project_id: int,
   993	    alert_id: int,
   994	    notification_uuid: str | None = None,
   995	    **kwargs: Any,
   996	) -> None:
   997	    try:
   998	        new_status_str = INCIDENT_STATUS[IncidentStatus(new_status)].lower()
   999	        event = SentryAppEventType(
  1000	            f"{SentryAppResourceType.METRIC_ALERT}.{MetricAlertActionType(new_status_str)}"
  1001	        )
  1002	    except ValueError as e:
  1003	        sentry_sdk.capture_exception(e)
  1004	        return
  1005	
  1006	    with SentryAppInteractionEvent(
  1007	        operation_type=SentryAppInteractionType.PREPARE_WEBHOOK,
  1008	        event_type=event,
  1009	    ).capture() as lifecycle:
  1010	        sentry_app = app_service.get_sentry_app_by_id(id=sentry_app_id)
  1011	        if sentry_app is None:
  1012	            lifecycle.record_failure(SentryAppWebhookFailureReason.MISSING_SENTRY_APP)
  1013	            return
  1014	
  1015	        if _is_sentry_app_disabled_for_webhooks(sentry_app):
  1016	            lifecycle.record_halt(halt_reason=SentryAppWebhookHaltReason.APP_DISABLED)
  1017	            return
  1018	
  1019	        installations = app_service.get_many(
  1020	            filter=dict(
  1021	                organization_id=organization_id,
  1022	                app_ids=[sentry_app.id],
  1023	                status=SentryAppInstallationStatus.INSTALLED,
  1024	            )
  1025	        )
  1026	        if not installations:
  1027	            lifecycle.record_failure(SentryAppWebhookFailureReason.MISSING_INSTALLATION)
  1028	            return
  1029	
  1030	        if len(installations) > 1:
  1031	            lifecycle.record_failure(SentryAppWebhookFailureReason.MULTIPLE_INSTALLATIONS)
  1032	            return
  1033	
  1034	        app_platform_event = AppPlatformEvent(
  1035	            resource=SentryAppResourceType.METRIC_ALERT,
  1036	            action=MetricAlertActionType(new_status_str),
  1037	            install=installations[0],
  1038	            data=json.loads(incident_attachment_json),
  1039	        )

   174	    def record_notification_sent(self, recipient: Actor, provider: ExternalProviders) -> None:
   175	        from sentry.integrations.discord.analytics import DiscordIntegrationNotificationSent
   176	        from sentry.integrations.msteams.analytics import MSTeamsIntegrationNotificationSent
   177	        from sentry.integrations.opsgenie.analytics import OpsgenieIntegrationNotificationSent
   178	        from sentry.integrations.pagerduty.analytics import PagerdutyIntegrationNotificationSent
   179	        from sentry.integrations.slack.analytics import SlackIntegrationNotificationSent
   180	
   181	        with sentry_sdk.start_span(op="notification.send", name="record_notification_sent"):
   182	            project: Project | None = getattr(self, "project", None)
   183	            group: Group | None = getattr(self, "group", None)
   184	
   185	            PROVIDER_TO_EVENT_CLASS = {
   186	                ExternalProviders.EMAIL: EmailNotificationSent,
   187	                ExternalProviders.SLACK: SlackIntegrationNotificationSent,
   188	                ExternalProviders.MSTEAMS: MSTeamsIntegrationNotificationSent,
   189	                ExternalProviders.PAGERDUTY: PagerdutyIntegrationNotificationSent,
   190	                ExternalProviders.OPSGENIE: OpsgenieIntegrationNotificationSent,
   191	                ExternalProviders.DISCORD: DiscordIntegrationNotificationSent,
   192	            }
   193	
   194	            try:
   195	                if event_class := PROVIDER_TO_EVENT_CLASS.get(provider):
   196	                    analytics.record(
   197	                        event_class(
   198	                            organization_id=self.organization.id,
   199	                            project_id=project.id if project else None,
   200	                            category=self.metrics_key,
   201	                            actor_id=recipient.id if recipient.is_user else None,
   202	                            user_id=recipient.id if recipient.is_user else None,
   203	                            group_id=group.id if group else None,
   204	                            id=recipient.id,
   205	                            actor_type=recipient.actor_type,
   206	                            notification_uuid=self.notification_uuid,
   207	                            alert_id=self.alert_id if self.alert_id else None,
   208	                        )
   209	                    )
   210	            except Exception as e:
   211	                sentry_sdk.capture_exception(e)
   212	
   213	            # record an optional second event
   214	            if notification_event := self.get_specific_analytics_event(provider):
   215	                try:
   216	                    analytics.record(notification_event)
   217	                except Exception as e:
   218	                    sentry_sdk.capture_exception(e)
