   266	            self.organization.absolute_url(
   267	                url_str,
   268	                query=self.get_sentry_query_params(provider, recipient, set_organization_id),
   269	            )
   270	        )
   271	
   272	    def determine_recipients(self) -> list[Actor]:
   273	        raise NotImplementedError
   274	
   275	    def get_notification_providers(self) -> Iterable[ExternalProviders]:
   276	        # subclass this method to limit notifications to specific providers
   277	        from sentry.notifications.notify import notification_providers
   278	
   279	        return notification_providers()
   280	
   281	    def filter_to_accepting_recipients(
   282	        self, recipients: Iterable[Actor]
   283	    ) -> Mapping[ExternalProviders, Iterable[Actor]]:
   284	        from sentry.notifications.utils.participants import get_notification_recipients
   285	
   286	        setting_type = (
   287	            self.notification_setting_type_enum
   288	            if self.notification_setting_type_enum
   289	            else NotificationSettingEnum.ISSUE_ALERTS
   290	        )
   291	        return get_notification_recipients(
   292	            recipients=recipients,
   293	            type=setting_type,
   294	            organization_id=self.organization.id,
   295	        )
   296	
   297	    def get_participants(self) -> Mapping[ExternalProviders, Iterable[Actor]]:
   298	        # need a notification_setting_type_enum to call this function
   299	        if not self.notification_setting_type_enum:
   300	            raise NotImplementedError
   301	
   302	        available_providers = self.get_notification_providers()
   303	        recipients = list(self.determine_recipients())
   304	        recipients_by_provider = self.filter_to_accepting_recipients(recipients)
   305	
   306	        return {
   307	            provider: recipients_of_provider
   308	            for provider, recipients_of_provider in recipients_by_provider.items()
   309	            if provider in available_providers
   310	        }
   311	
   312	    def send(self) -> None:
   313	        """The default way to send notifications that respects Notification Settings."""
   314	        from sentry.notifications.notify import notify
   315	
   316	        with sentry_sdk.start_span(op="notification.send", name="get_participants"):
   317	            participants_by_provider = self.get_participants()
   318	            if not participants_by_provider:
   319	                return
   320	
   321	        context = self.get_context()
   322	        for provider, recipients in participants_by_provider.items():
   323	            with sentry_sdk.start_span(op="notification.send", name=f"send_for_{provider}"):
   324	                safe_execute(notify, provider, self, recipients, context)
   325

   181	    def get_context(self) -> MutableMapping[str, Any]:
   182	        environment = self.event.get_tag("environment")

   184	        rule_details = get_rules(self.rules, self.organization, self.project, self.group.type)
   185	        sentry_query_params = self.get_sentry_query_params(ExternalProviders.EMAIL)
   186	        for rule in rule_details:

   196	        fallback_params: MutableMapping[str, str] = {}
   197	        group_header = get_group_substatus_text(self.group)
   198	
   199	        notification_uuid = self.notification_uuid if hasattr(self, "notification_uuid") else None
   200	        context = {

   202	            "group": self.group,
   203	            "group_header": group_header,
   204	            "event": self.event,
   205	            "link": get_group_settings_link(
   206	                self.group,
   207	                environment,
   208	                rule_details,
   209	                None,
   210	                notification_uuid=notification_uuid,
   211	                **fallback_params,
   212	            ),
   213	            "rules": rule_details,

   240	                {
   241	                    "issue_replays_url": get_issue_replay_link(self.group, sentry_query_params),
   242	                }
   243	            )
   244	
   245	        template_name = (
   246	            self.event.occurrence.evidence_data.get("template_name")
   247	            if isinstance(self.event, GroupEvent) and self.event.occurrence
   248	            else None
   249	        )
   250	
   251	        if self.group.issue_category in PERFORMANCE_ISSUE_CATEGORIES and template_name != "profile":
   252	            # This can't use data from the occurrence at the moment, so we'll keep fetching the event
   253	            # and gathering span evidence.
   254	
   255	            # Regression issues don't have span evidence
   256	            if self.group.issue_type not in [
   257	                PerformanceP95EndpointRegressionGroupType,
   258	                ProfileFunctionRegressionType,
   259	            ]:
   260	                context.update(
   261	                    {
   262	                        "transaction_data": [

   264	                        ],
   265	                    }
   266	                )
   267	            context.update(
   268	                {
   269	                    "subtitle": get_performance_issue_alert_subtitle(self.event),
   270	                },
   271	            )
   272	
   273	        # We don't show the snooze alert if the organization has not enabled the workflow engine UI because in the new UI/system a user can't individually disable a workflow
   274	        if not features.has("organizations:workflow-engine-ui", self.organization):
   275	            if len(self.rules) > 0:

   284	        else:

   287

   291	            context["culprit"] = self.event.occurrence.culprit

   315	                case "workflow_id":

   317	                case "legacy_rule_id":
   318	                    rule_url = build_rule_url(self.rules[0], self.group, self.project)
   319	
   320	            title_str += (

   322	            )
   323	
   324	            if len(self.rules) > 1:
   325	                title_str += f" (+{len(self.rules) - 1} other)"
   326	
   327	        return title_str

   101	            yield provider, participants_with_reasons.keys(), extra_context

   104	def get_providers_from_which_to_remove_user(
   105	    user_id: int,
   106	    participants_by_provider: ParticipantMap,
   107	) -> set[ExternalProviders]:
   108	    """
   109	    Given a mapping of provider to mappings of users to why they should receive
   110	    notifications for an activity, return the set of providers where the user
   111	    has opted out of receiving notifications.
   112	    """
   113	
   114	    providers = {
   115	        provider
   116	        for provider, participants in participants_by_provider.get_participant_sets()
   117	        if user_id in map(lambda p: int(p.id), participants)
   118	    }
   119	
   120	    if (
   121	        get_option_from_list(
   122	            user_option_service.get_many(
   123	                filter={"user_ids": [user_id], "keys": ["self_notifications"]}
   124	            ),
   125	            key="self_notifications",
   126	            default="0",
   127	        )
   128	        == "0"
   129	    ):
   130	        return providers
   131	    return set()

   134	def get_participants_for_group(group: Group, user_id: int | None = None) -> ParticipantMap:
   135	    participants_by_provider: ParticipantMap = GroupSubscription.objects.get_participants(group)
   136	    if user_id:
   137	        # Optionally remove the actor that created the activity from the recipients list.
   138	        providers = get_providers_from_which_to_remove_user(user_id, participants_by_provider)
   139	        for provider in providers:
   140	            participants_by_provider.delete_participant_by_id(provider, ActorType.USER, user_id)
   141	
   142	    return participants_by_provider

   145	def get_reason(
   146	    user: User | Actor,
   147	    value: NotificationSettingsOptionEnum,
   148	    user_ids: set[int],
   149	) -> int | None:
   150	    # Members who opt into all deploy emails.
   151	    if value == NotificationSettingsOptionEnum.ALWAYS:
   152	        return GroupSubscriptionReason.deploy_setting
   153	
   154	    # Members which have been seen in the commit log.
   155	    elif value == NotificationSettingsOptionEnum.COMMITTED_ONLY and user.id in user_ids:
   156	        return GroupSubscriptionReason.committed
   157	    return None

   160	def get_participants_for_release(
   161	    projects: Iterable[Project], organization: Organization, commited_user_ids: set[int]
   162	) -> ParticipantMap:
   163	    # Collect all users with verified emails on a team in the related projects.
   164	    user_ids = [
   165	        user_id
   166	        for user_id in OrganizationMember.objects.filter(
   167	            teams__projectteam__project__in=projects,
   168	            user_is_active=True,
   169	            user_id__isnull=False,
   170	        )
   171	        .distinct()
   172	        .values_list("user_id", flat=True)
   173	        if user_id is not None
   174	    ]
   175	
   176	    # filter those user ids by verified emails
   177	    user_ids = user_service.get_many_ids(filter=dict(user_ids=user_ids, email_verified=True))
   178	
   179	    actors = Actor.many_from_object(RpcUser(id=user_id) for user_id in user_ids)
   180	    # don't pass in projects since the settings are scoped to the organization only for now
   181	    providers_by_recipient = notifications_service.get_participants(
   182	        type=NotificationSettingEnum.DEPLOY,
   183	        recipients=actors,
   184	        organization_id=organization.id,
   185	    )
   186	
   187	    users_to_reasons_by_provider = ParticipantMap()
   188	    for actor in actors:
   189	        settings = providers_by_recipient.get(actor.id, {})
   190	        for provider_str, val_str in settings.items():
   191	            provider = ExternalProviders(provider_str)
   192	            val = NotificationSettingsOptionEnum(val_str)
   193	            reason = get_reason(actor, val, commited_user_ids)
   194	            if reason:
   195	                users_to_reasons_by_provider.add(provider, actor, reason)
   196	    return users_to_reasons_by_provider

   199	def get_owners(
   200	    project: Project,
   201	    event: Event | GroupEvent | None = None,
   202	    fallthrough_choice: FallthroughChoiceType | None = None,
   203	) -> tuple[list[Actor], str]:
   204	    """
   205	    Given a project and an event, decide which users and teams are the owners.
   206	
   207	    If when checking owners, there is a rule match we only notify the last owner
   208	    (would-be auto-assignee)
   209	    """
   210	
   211	    if event:
   212	        owners, _ = ProjectOwnership.get_owners(project.id, event.data)
   213	        if not owners:
   214	            outcome = "empty"
   215	            recipients = []
   216	        else:
   217	            outcome = "match"
   218	            recipients = owners[-1:]
   219	    else:
   220	        outcome = "everyone"
   240	        return None
   241	
   242	    # Not an issue alert
   243	    if event is None:
   244	        return None
   245	
   246	    # Describe why an issue owner was notified
   247	    if fallthrough_choice == FallthroughChoiceType.ALL_MEMBERS:
   248	        return f"We notified all members in the {project.get_full_name()} project of this issue"
   249	    if fallthrough_choice == FallthroughChoiceType.ACTIVE_MEMBERS:
   250	        return f"We notified recently active members in the {project.get_full_name()} project of this issue"
   251	
   252	    return None
   253

   255	def get_suspect_commit_users(project: Project, event: Event | GroupEvent) -> list[RpcUser]:
   256	    """
   257	    Returns a list of users that are suspect committers for the given event.
   258	
   259	    `project`: The project that the event is associated to
   260	    `event`: The event that suspect committers are wanted for
   261	    """
   262	
   263	    committers: Sequence[AuthorCommitsSerialized] = get_serialized_event_file_committers(
   264	        project, event
   265	    )
   266	    user_emails = [committer["author"]["email"] for committer in committers]  # type: ignore[index]
   267	    suspect_committers = user_service.get_many_by_email(emails=user_emails, is_verified=True)
   268	    in_project_user_ids = set(
   269	        OrganizationMember.objects.filter(
   270	            teams__projectteam__project__in=[project],
   271	            user_id__in=[owner.id for owner in suspect_committers],
   272	        ).values_list("user_id", flat=True)
   273	    )
   274	
   275	    return [committer for committer in suspect_committers if committer.id in in_project_user_ids]

   278	def dedupe_suggested_assignees(suggested_assignees: Iterable[Actor]) -> list[Actor]:
   279	    return list({assignee.id: assignee for assignee in suggested_assignees}.values())

   282	def determine_eligible_recipients(
   283	    project: Project,
   284	    target_type: ActionTargetType,
   285	    target_identifier: int | None = None,
   286	    event: Event | GroupEvent | None = None,
   287	    fallthrough_choice: FallthroughChoiceType | None = None,
   288	) -> Iterable[Actor]:
   289	    """
   290	    Either get the individual recipient from the target type/id or the
   291	    owners as determined by rules for this project and event.
   292	    """
   293	    if not (project and project.teams.exists()):
   294	        logger.debug("Tried to send notification to invalid project: %s", project)
   295	
   296	    elif target_type == ActionTargetType.MEMBER:
   297	        user = get_user_from_identifier(project, target_identifier)
   298	        if user:
   299	            return [Actor.from_object(user)]
   300	
   301	    elif target_type == ActionTargetType.TEAM:
   302	        team = get_team_from_identifier(project, target_identifier)
   303	        if team:
   304	            return [Actor.from_orm_team(team)]
   305	
   306	    elif target_type == ActionTargetType.ISSUE_OWNERS:
   307	        if not event:
   308	            return []
   309	
   310	        suggested_assignees, outcome = get_owners(project, event, fallthrough_choice)
   311	
   312	        # We're adding the current assignee to the list of suggested assignees because
   313	        # a new issue could have multiple codeowners and one of them got auto-assigned.
   314	        group_assignee = (
   315	            GroupAssignee.objects.filter(group_id=event.group_id).first()
   316	            if event.group_id is not None
   317	            else None
   318	        )
   319	        if group_assignee:
   320	            outcome = "match"
   321	            assignee_actor = group_assignee.assigned_actor()
   322	            suggested_assignees.append(assignee_actor)
   323	
   324	        suspect_commit_users = None
   325	
   326	        try:
   327	            suspect_commit_users = Actor.many_from_object(get_suspect_commit_users(project, event))
   328	            suggested_assignees.extend(suspect_commit_users)
   329	        except (Release.DoesNotExist, Commit.DoesNotExist):
   330	            logger.info("Skipping suspect committers because release does not exist.")
   331	        except Exception:
   332	            logger.exception("Could not get suspect committers. Continuing execution.")
   333	
   334	        metrics.incr(
   335	            "features.owners.send_to",
   336	            tags={
   337	                "outcome": (
   338	                    outcome
   339	                    if outcome == "match" or fallthrough_choice is None
   340	                    else fallthrough_choice.value

   502	def get_notification_recipients(
   503	    recipients: Iterable[Actor],
   504	    type: NotificationSettingEnum,
   505	    organization_id: int | None = None,
   506	    project_ids: list[int] | None = None,
   507	    actor_type: ActorType | None = None,
   508	) -> Mapping[ExternalProviders, set[Actor]]:
   509	    recipients_by_provider = notifications_service.get_notification_recipients(
   510	        recipients=list(recipients),
   511	        type=type,
   512	        organization_id=organization_id,
   513	        project_ids=project_ids,
   514	        actor_type=actor_type,
   515	    )
   516	    # ensure we use a defaultdict here in case someone tries to access a provider that has no recipients
   517	    out = defaultdict(set)
   518	    for provider, actors in recipients_by_provider.items():
   519	        key = get_provider_enum_from_string(provider)
   520	        out[key] = actors
   521	    return out

   523	
   524	def _get_recipients_by_provider(
   525	    project: Project,
   526	    recipients: Iterable[Actor],
   527	    notification_type_enum: NotificationSettingEnum = NotificationSettingEnum.ISSUE_ALERTS,
   528	    target_type: ActionTargetType | None = None,
   529	    target_identifier: int | None = None,
   530	    notification_uuid: str | None = None,
   531	) -> Mapping[ExternalProviders, set[Actor]]:
   532	    """Get the lists of recipients that should receive an Issue Alert by ExternalProvider."""
   533	    recipients_by_type = _partition_recipients(recipients)
   534	    teams = recipients_by_type[ActorType.TEAM]
   535	    users = recipients_by_type[ActorType.USER]
   536	
   537	    # First evaluate the teams.
   538	    setting_type = notification_type_enum
   539	    teams_by_provider: Mapping[ExternalProviders, Iterable[Actor]] = {}
   540	
   541	    # get by team
   542	    teams_by_provider = get_notification_recipients(
   543	        recipients=teams,
   544	        type=setting_type,
   545	        organization_id=project.organization_id,
   546	        project_ids=[project.id],
   547	        actor_type=ActorType.TEAM,
   548	    )
   549	
   550	    # Teams cannot receive emails so omit EMAIL settings.
   551	    teams_by_provider = {
   552	        provider: teams
   553	        for provider, teams in teams_by_provider.items()
   554	        if provider != ExternalProviders.EMAIL
   555	    }
   556	
   557	    # If there are any teams that didn't get added, fall back and add all users.
   558	    users |= set(Actor.many_from_object(_get_users_from_team_fall_back(teams, teams_by_provider)))
   559	
   560	    # Repeat for users.
   561	    users_by_provider: Mapping[ExternalProviders, Iterable[Actor]] = {}
   562	    # convert from string to enum
   563	    users_by_provider = get_notification_recipients(
   564	        recipients=users,
   565	        type=setting_type,
   566	        organization_id=project.organization_id,
   567	        project_ids=[project.id],
   568	        actor_type=ActorType.USER,
   569	    )
   570	
   571	    # TODO(jangjodi): Remove the try-except once INC-564 prevention steps are completed
   572	    try:
   573	        teams_by_provider_dict = {
   574	            provider.value: [team.id for team in teams_by_provider[provider]]
   575	            for provider in teams_by_provider.keys()
   576	        }
   577	        users_by_provider_dict = {
   578	            provider.value: [user.id for user in users_by_provider[provider]]
   579	            for provider in users_by_provider.keys()
   580	        }
   581	        extra = {
   582	            "organization_id": project.organization.id,
   583	            "project_id": project.id,
   584	            "target_type": target_type,
   585	            "target_identifier": target_identifier,
   586	            "notification_uuid": notification_uuid,
   587	            "teams": json.dumps([team.id for team in teams]),
   588	            "teams_by_provider": json.dumps(teams_by_provider_dict),
   589	            "users": json.dumps([user.id for user in users]),
   590	            "users_by_provider": json.dumps(users_by_provider_dict),
   591	        }
   592	        logger.info("sentry.notifications.recipients_by_provider", extra=extra)
   593	    except Exception as e:
   594	        logger.exception("Unable to log recipients_by_provider: %s", e)
   595	
   596	    return combine_recipients_by_provider(teams_by_provider, users_by_provider)

    35	@dataclass(frozen=True)
    36	class EventDetectors:
    37	    issue_stream_detector: Detector | None = None
    38	    event_detector: Detector | None = None
    39	
    40	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
    41	        if not self.has_detectors:
    42	            raise ValueError("At least one detector must be provided")
    43	
    44	    @property
    45	    def has_detectors(self) -> bool:
    46	        """
    47	        Returns True if at least one detector exists.
    48	        """
    49	        return self.issue_stream_detector is not None or self.event_detector is not None
    50	
    51	    @property
    52	    def preferred_detector(self) -> Detector:
    53	        """
    54	        The preferred detector is the one that should be used for the event,
    55	        if we need to use a singular detector (for example, in logging).
    56	        The class will not initialize if no detectors are found.
    57	        """
    58	        detector = self.event_detector or self.issue_stream_detector
    59	        assert detector is not None, "At least one detector must exist"
    60	        return detector
    61	
    62	    @property
    63	    def detectors(self) -> set[Detector]:
    64	        return {d for d in [self.issue_stream_detector, self.event_detector] if d is not None}

    67	# TODO - Delete this once the issue stream is fully rolled out.
    68	def _is_issue_stream_detector_enabled(event_data: WorkflowEventData) -> bool:
    69	    """
    70	    Check if the issue stream detector should be enabled for this event's group type.
    71	    """
    72	    group_type_id = event_data.group.type
    73	    disabled_type_ids = options.get("workflow_engine.group.type_id.disable_issue_stream_detector")
    74	    if group_type_id not in disabled_type_ids:
    75	        return True
    76	
    77	    # Metric isssues are a special case currently.
    78	    # In order to give users time to adjust to the new behavior, we allow them to disable the
    79	    # issue stream detector for metric issues via a feature flag.
    80	    if group_type_id != MetricIssue.type_id:
    81	        return False
    82	
    83	    organization = event_data.event.project.organization
    84	
    85	    disable_issue_stream_detector_for_metric_issues = features.has(
    86	        "organizations:workflow-engine-metric-issue-disable-issue-detector-notifications",
    87	        organization,
    88	    )
    89	    return not disable_issue_stream_detector_for_metric_issues

    92	def get_detectors_for_event_data(
    93	    event_data: WorkflowEventData,
    94	    detector: Detector | None = None,
    95	) -> EventDetectors | None:
    96	    """
    97	    Returns a list of detectors for the event to process workflows for.
    98	
    99	    We always return at least the issue stream detector, unless excluded via option or feature flag.
   100	    If the event has an associated detector, we return it too.
   101	
   102	    We expect a detector to be passed in for Activity updates.
   103	    """
   104	    issue_stream_detector: Detector | None = None
   105	
   106	    try:
   107	        if _is_issue_stream_detector_enabled(event_data):
   108	            issue_stream_detector = Detector.get_issue_stream_detector_for_project(
   109	                event_data.group.project_id
   110	            )
   111	    except Detector.DoesNotExist:
   112	        metrics.incr("workflow_engine.detectors.error")
   113	        logger.exception(
   114	            "Issue stream detector not found for event",
   115	            extra={
   116	                "project_id": event_data.group.project_id,
   117	                "group_id": event_data.group.id,
   118	            },
   119	        )
   120	
   121	    if detector is None and isinstance(event_data.event, GroupEvent):
   122	        detector = _get_detector_for_event(event_data.event)
   123	    try:
   124	        return EventDetectors(issue_stream_detector=issue_stream_detector, event_detector=detector)
   125	    except ValueError:
   126	        return None

   129	def _get_detector_for_event(event: GroupEvent) -> Detector | None:
   130	    """
   131	    Returns the detector from the GroupEvent in event_data, or None if no detector is found.
   132	    """
   133	    issue_occurrence = event.occurrence
   134	    try:
   135	        if issue_occurrence is not None:
   136	            detector_id = issue_occurrence.evidence_data.get("detector_id")
   137	            if detector_id is None:
   138	                return None
   139	            return Detector.objects.get(id=detector_id)
   140	        else:
   141	            return Detector.get_error_detector_for_project(event.group.project_id)
   142	    except Detector.DoesNotExist:
   143	        return None
   144	
   145	
   146	def _get_detector_for_group(group: Group) -> Detector:
   147	    """
   148	    Returns Detector associated with this group, either based on DetectorGroup,
   149	    (project, type), or if those fail, returns the Issue Stream detector.
   150	    """
   151	    try:
   152	        detector = DetectorGroup.objects.get(group=group).detector
   153	        if detector is not None:
   154	            return detector
   155	    except DetectorGroup.DoesNotExist:
   156	        logger.exception(
   157	            "DetectorGroup not found for group",
   158	            extra={"group_id": group.id},
   159	        )
   160	        pass
   161	
   162	    try:
   163	        return Detector.objects.get(project_id=group.project_id, type=group.issue_type.slug)
   164	    except (Detector.DoesNotExist, Detector.MultipleObjectsReturned):
   165	        # return issue stream detector
   166	        return Detector.objects.get(project_id=group.project_id, type=IssueStreamGroupType.slug)

   169	def get_preferred_detector(event_data: WorkflowEventData) -> Detector:
   170	    """
   171	    Attempts to fetch the specific detector based on the GroupEvent or Activity in event_data
   172	    """
   173	    try:
   174	        if isinstance(event_data.event, GroupEvent):
   175	            event_detectors = get_detectors_for_event_data(event_data)
   176	            if event_detectors is None:
   177	                raise Detector.DoesNotExist("No detectors found for event")
   178	            return event_detectors.preferred_detector
   179	        elif isinstance(event_data.event, Activity):
   180	            return _get_detector_for_group(event_data.group)
   181	        else:
   182	            raise TypeError(f"Cannot determine the detector from {type(event_data.event)}.")
   183	    except Detector.DoesNotExist:
   184	        logger.exception(
   185	            "Detector not found for event data",
   186	            extra={
   187	                "type": type(event_data.event),
   188	                "id": (
   189	                    event_data.event.event_id
   190	                    if isinstance(event_data.event, GroupEvent)
   191	                    else event_data.event.id
   192	                ),
   193	                "group_id": event_data.group.id,
   194	            },
   195	        )
   196	        raise

   286	    """
   287	    Associate a new Group with it's Detector in the database.
   288	    If the Group is an error, it can be associated without a detector ID.
   289	
   290	    Return whether the group was associated.
   291	    """
   292	    if detector_id is None:

   294	        # The detector may be missing due to concurrent project deletion.
   295	        if group.type == ErrorGroupType.type_id:

   300	            except Detector.DoesNotExist:
   301	                # If the project is mid-deletion, the detector will be missing, so infrequently
   302	                # hitting this case is fine, but we add a metric to make sure it stays infrequent.

   311	                        "group_type": group.type,
   312	                        "project_id": group.project.id,
   313	                    },
   314	                )
   315	                return False
   316	        else:

   318	                "workflow_engine.associate_new_group_with_detector",
   319	                tags={"group_type": group.type, "result": "failure"},
   320	            )
   321	            logger.warning(

   323	                extra={
   324	                    "group_id": group.id,
   325	                    "group_type": group.type,
   326	                },
   327	            )
   328	            return False
   329	
   330	    # Check if the detector exists. If not, create DetectorGroup with null detector_id
   331	    # to make it clear that we were associated with a detector that no longer exists.
   332	    if not Detector.objects.filter(id=detector_id).exists():
   333	        metrics.incr(
   334	            "workflow_engine.associate_new_group_with_detector",
   335	            tags={"group_type": group.type, "result": "detector_missing"},
   336	        )
   337	        logger.warning(
   338	            "associate_new_group_with_detector_detector_missing",
   339	            extra={
   340	                "group_id": group.id,

    44	def get_workflow_action_group_statuses(
    45	    action_to_workflows_ids: dict[int, set[int]], group: Group, workflow_ids: set[int]
    46	) -> dict[int, list[WorkflowActionGroupStatus]]:
    47	    """
    48	    Returns a mapping of action IDs to their corresponding WorkflowActionGroupStatus objects
    49	    given the provided action_to_workflows_ids and group.
    50	    """
    51	
    52	    all_statuses = WorkflowActionGroupStatus.objects.filter(
    53	        group=group, action_id__in=action_to_workflows_ids.keys(), workflow_id__in=workflow_ids
    54	    )
    55	
    56	    actions_with_statuses: dict[int, list[WorkflowActionGroupStatus]] = defaultdict(list)
    57	
    58	    for status in all_statuses:
    59	        workflow_id = status.workflow_id
    60	        action_id = status.action_id
    61	        if workflow_id not in action_to_workflows_ids[action_id]:
    62	            # if the (workflow, action) combination shouldn't be processed, skip it
    63	            # more difficult to query than to iterate
    64	            continue
    65	
    66	        actions_with_statuses[action_id].append(status)
    67	
    68	    return actions_with_statuses

    70	
    71	def process_workflow_action_group_statuses(
    72	    action_to_workflows_ids: dict[int, set[int]],
    73	    action_to_statuses: dict[int, list[WorkflowActionGroupStatus]],
    74	    workflows: BaseQuerySet[Workflow],
    75	    group: Group,
    76	    now: datetime,
    77	) -> tuple[dict[int, set[int]], set[int], list[WorkflowActionGroupStatus]]:
    78	    """
    79	    Determine which workflow actions should be fired based on their statuses.
    80	    Prepare the statuses to update and create.
    81	    """
    82	
    83	    updated_action_to_workflows_ids: dict[int, set[int]] = defaultdict(set)
    84	    workflow_frequencies: dict[int, timedelta] = {
    85	        workflow.id: workflow.config.get("frequency", 0) * timedelta(minutes=1)
    86	        for workflow in workflows
    87	    }
    88	    statuses_to_update: set[int] = set()
    89	
    90	    zero_timedelta = timedelta(minutes=0)
    91	    for action_id, statuses in action_to_statuses.items():
    92	        for status in statuses:
    93	            if (now - status.date_updated) > workflow_frequencies.get(
    94	                status.workflow_id, zero_timedelta
    95	            ):
    96	                # we should fire the workflow for this action
    97	                updated_action_to_workflows_ids[action_id].add(status.workflow_id)
    98	                statuses_to_update.add(status.id)
    99	
   100	    missing_statuses: list[WorkflowActionGroupStatus] = []
   101	    for action_id, expected_workflows in action_to_workflows_ids.items():
   102	        wags = action_to_statuses.get(action_id, [])
   103	        actual_workflows = {status.workflow_id for status in wags}
   104	        missing_workflows = expected_workflows - actual_workflows
   105	
   106	        for workflow_id in missing_workflows:
   107	            # create a new status for the missing workflow
   108	            missing_statuses.append(
   109	                WorkflowActionGroupStatus(
   110	                    workflow_id=workflow_id, action_id=action_id, group=group, date_updated=now
   111	                )
   112	            )
   113	            updated_action_to_workflows_ids[action_id].add(workflow_id)
   114	
   115	    return updated_action_to_workflows_ids, statuses_to_update, missing_statuses
   116	
   117	
   118	def _bulk_insert_workflow_action_group_statuses(
   119	    missing_statuses: list[WorkflowActionGroupStatus],
   120	    now: datetime,
   121	    *,
   122	    exists_checks: bool,
   123	) -> set[tuple[int, int]]:
   124	    """Execute a batch INSERT and return the set of (workflow_id, action_id) rows created.
   125	
   126	    When exists_checks=True the INSERT filters out rows whose FK targets have been
   127	    deleted, avoiding IntegrityErrors at the cost of 3 correlated EXISTS subqueries
   128	    per row.  Use exists_checks=False for the fast path and only retry with
   129	    exists_checks=True after an IntegrityError.
   130	    """
   131	    # XXX: the query does not currently include batch size limit like bulk_create does
   132	    values_placeholders = []
   133	    values_data = []
   134	    for s in missing_statuses:
   135	        values_placeholders.append("(%s, %s, %s, %s, %s)")
   136	        values_data.extend([s.workflow_id, s.action_id, s.group_id, now, now])
   137	
   138	    values_clause = ", ".join(values_placeholders)
   139	
   140	    if exists_checks:
   141	        source = f"""
   142	            SELECT v.workflow_id, v.action_id, v.group_id, v.date_added, v.date_updated
   143	            FROM (VALUES {values_clause})
   144	                AS v(workflow_id, action_id, group_id, date_added, date_updated)
   145	            WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM workflow_engine_workflow w WHERE w.id = v.workflow_id)
   146	              AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM workflow_engine_action a WHERE a.id = v.action_id)
   147	              AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sentry_groupedmessage g WHERE g.id = v.group_id)
   148	        """
   149	    else:
   150	        source = f"VALUES {values_clause}"
   151	
   152	    sql = f"""
   153	        INSERT INTO workflow_engine_workflowactiongroupstatus
   154	        (workflow_id, action_id, group_id, date_added, date_updated)
   155	        {source}
   156	        ON CONFLICT (workflow_id, action_id, group_id) DO NOTHING
   157	        RETURNING workflow_id, action_id
   158	    """
   159	
   160	    with connections[router.db_for_write(WorkflowActionGroupStatus)].cursor() as cursor:
   161	        cursor.execute(sql, values_data)
   162	        return set(cursor.fetchall())
   163	
   164	
   165	def update_workflow_action_group_statuses(
   166	    now: datetime, statuses_to_update: set[int], missing_statuses: list[WorkflowActionGroupStatus]
   167	) -> StatusUpdateResult:
   168	    updated_count = WorkflowActionGroupStatus.objects.filter(
   169	        id__in=statuses_to_update, date_updated__lt=now
   170	    ).update(date_updated=now)
   171	
   172	    if not missing_statuses:
   173	        return StatusUpdateResult(updated=updated_count, created=0, not_created=[])
   174	
   175	    try:
   176	        with transaction.atomic(router.db_for_write(WorkflowActionGroupStatus)):
   177	            created_rows = _bulk_insert_workflow_action_group_statuses(
   178	                missing_statuses, now, exists_checks=False
   179	            )
   180	    except IntegrityError:
   181	        # A referenced workflow/action/group was deleted between our read and this
   182	        # insert.  Retry with EXISTS checks to silently skip the orphaned rows.
   183	        created_rows = _bulk_insert_workflow_action_group_statuses(
   184	            missing_statuses, now, exists_checks=True
   185	        )
   186	
   187	    # Rows not returned were either unique conflicts or FK-missing (dropped)
   188	    dropped_statuses: DroppedStatuses = [
   189	        (s.workflow_id, s.action_id)
   190	        for s in missing_statuses
   191	        if (s.workflow_id, s.action_id) not in created_rows
   192	    ]
   193	
   194	    # Log action_ids for debugging
   195	    attempted_action_ids = {s.action_id for s in missing_statuses}
   196	    created_action_ids = {action_id for _, action_id in created_rows}
   197	    logger.debug(
   198	        "workflow_action_group_status.creation",
   199	        extra={
   200	            "attempted_action_ids": list(attempted_action_ids),
   201	            "created_action_ids": list(created_action_ids),
   202	        },
   203	    )
   204	
   205	    return StatusUpdateResult(
   206	        updated=updated_count,
   207	        created=len(created_rows),
   208	        not_created=dropped_statuses,
   209	    )
   210

   212	def get_unique_active_actions(
   213	    actions_queryset: BaseQuerySet[Action],
   214	    group: Group,
   215	) -> BaseQuerySet[Action]:
   216	    """
   217	    Returns a queryset of unique active actions based on their handler's dedup_key method.
   218	    Group is used for logging only.
   219	    """
   220	    dedup_key_to_action_id: dict[str, int] = {}
   240	                "dedup_key": dedup_key,
   241	                "dropped_action_ids": sorted(action_ids),
   242	                "replacement_action_id": dedup_key_to_action_id[dedup_key],
   243	                "group_id": group.id,
   244	                "group_type": group_type,
   245	            },
   246	        )
   247	        metrics.incr(
   248	            "workflow_engine.action.dedup.dropped", len(action_ids), tags={"group_type": group_type}
   249	        )
   250	
   251	    return actions_queryset.filter(id__in=dedup_key_to_action_id.values())

   254	@scopedstats.timer()
   255	def fire_actions(
   256	    actions: BaseQuerySet[Action],
   257	    event_data: WorkflowEventData,
   258	    workflow_uuid_map: dict[WorkflowId, str],
   259	    action_to_workflow_id: dict[ActionId, WorkflowId],
   260	) -> None:
   261	    deduped_actions = get_unique_active_actions(actions, event_data.group)
   262	
   263	    for action in deduped_actions:
   264	        workflow_id = action_to_workflow_id[action.id]
   265	        task_params = build_trigger_action_task_params(
   266	            action, event_data, workflow_uuid_map, workflow_id=workflow_id
   267	        )
   268	        trigger_action.apply_async(kwargs=task_params, headers={"sentry-propagate-traces": False})
   269	
   270	
   271	def filter_recently_fired_workflow_actions(
   272	    filtered_action_groups: set[DataConditionGroup], event_data: WorkflowEventData
   273	) -> tuple[BaseQuerySet[Action], dict[ActionId, WorkflowId]]:
   274	    """
   275	    Returns actions associated with the provided DataConditionGroups, excluding
   276	    those that have been recently fired, along with a mapping of action_id to
   277	    the workflow_id each action is firing for. Also updates associated
   278	    WorkflowActionGroupStatus objects.
   279	    """
   280	
   281	    data_condition_group_actions = DataConditionGroupAction.objects.filter(
   282	        condition_group__in=filtered_action_groups
   283	    ).values_list("action_id", "condition_group__workflowdataconditiongroup__workflow_id")
   284	
   285	    action_to_workflows_ids: dict[int, set[int]] = defaultdict(set)
   286	    workflow_ids: set[int] = set()
   287	
   288	    for action_id, workflow_id in data_condition_group_actions:
   289	        # If we are mid-deletion, the workflow_id can be none.
   290	        if workflow_id is not None:
   291	            action_to_workflows_ids[action_id].add(workflow_id)
   292	            workflow_ids.add(workflow_id)
   293	
   294	    workflows = Workflow.objects.filter(id__in=workflow_ids)
   295	
   296	    action_to_statuses = get_workflow_action_group_statuses(
   297	        action_to_workflows_ids=action_to_workflows_ids,
   298	        group=event_data.group,
   299	        workflow_ids=workflow_ids,
   300	    )
   301	    now = timezone.now()
   302	    action_to_workflows_ids, statuses_to_update, missing_statuses = (
   303	        process_workflow_action_group_statuses(
   304	            action_to_workflows_ids=action_to_workflows_ids,
   305	            action_to_statuses=action_to_statuses,
   306	            workflows=workflows,
   307	            group=event_data.group,
   308	            now=now,
   309	        )
   310	    )
   311	    update_result = update_workflow_action_group_statuses(now, statuses_to_update, missing_statuses)
   312	
   313	    # if statuses were not created for some reason, we should not fire for them
   314	    for workflow_id, action_id in update_result.not_created:
   315	        action_to_workflows_ids[action_id].remove(workflow_id)
   316	        if not action_to_workflows_ids[action_id]:
   317	            action_to_workflows_ids.pop(action_id)
   318	
   319	    actions_queryset = Action.objects.filter(id__in=list(action_to_workflows_ids.keys()))
   320	
   321	    # Build a mapping of action_id -> workflow_id (pick one arbitrarily but deterministically)
   322	    action_to_workflow_id: dict[ActionId, WorkflowId] = {
   323	        action_id: min(wf_ids) for action_id, wf_ids in action_to_workflows_ids.items()
   324	    }
   325	
   326	    return actions_queryset, action_to_workflow_id
   327	
   328	
   329	def get_available_action_integrations_for_org(organization: Organization) -> list[RpcIntegration]:
   330	    providers = [
   331	        handler.provider_slug
   332	        for handler in action_handler_registry.registrations.values()
   333	        if hasattr(handler, "provider_slug")
   334	    ]
   335	    return integration_service.get_integrations(
   336	        status=ObjectStatus.ACTIVE,
   337	        org_integration_status=ObjectStatus.ACTIVE,
   338	        organization_id=organization.id,
   339	        providers=providers,
   340	    )

    43	def process_workflow_activity(activity_id: int, group_id: int, detector_id: DetectorId) -> None:
    44	    """
    45	    Process a workflow task identified by the given activity, group, and detector.
    46	
    47	    The task will get the Activity from the database, create a WorkflowEventData object,
    48	    and then process the data in `process_workflows`.
    49	    """
    50	    from sentry.workflow_engine.processors.workflow import process_workflows
    51	
    52	    with transaction.atomic(router.db_for_write(Detector)):
    53	        try:
    54	            activity = Activity.objects.get(id=activity_id)
    55	            group = Group.objects.get(id=group_id)
    56	            detector = Detector.objects.get(id=detector_id)
    57	        except (Activity.DoesNotExist, Group.DoesNotExist, Detector.DoesNotExist):
    58	            logger.exception(
    59	                "Unable to fetch data to process workflow activity",
    60	                extra={
    61	                    "activity_id": activity_id,
    62	                    "group_id": group_id,
    63	                    "detector_id": detector_id,
    64	                },
    65	            )
    66	            return  # Exit execution that we cannot recover from
    67	
    68	    event_data = WorkflowEventData(
    69	        event=activity,
    70	        group=group,
    71	    )
    72	    with quiet_redis_noise():
    73	        batch_client = DelayedWorkflowClient()
    74	        evaluation = process_workflows(
    75	            batch_client, event_data, event_start_time=activity.datetime, detector=detector
    76	        )
    77	
    78	    evaluation.log_to(logger)

    81	        "workflow_engine.tasks.process_workflows.activity_update.executed",
    82	        tags={"activity_type": activity.type, "detector_type": detector.type},
    83	        sample_rate=1.0,
    84	    )

   131	def _process_workflows_event(

   144	    with recorder.record():
   145	        try:
   146	            event_data = build_workflow_event_data_from_event(
   147	                event_id=event_id,
   148	                group_id=group_id,
   149	                occurrence_id=occurrence_id,
   150	                group_state=group_state,
   151	                has_escalated=has_escalated,
   152	            )
   153	        except (RetryError, OSError) as e:
   154	            # We want to quietly retry these.
   155	            # Both are expected transient errors from Bigtable interactions.
   156	            retry_task(e)
   157	
   158	        event_start_time = (
   159	            datetime.fromtimestamp(start_timestamp_seconds, tz=UTC)
   160	            if start_timestamp_seconds
   161	            else datetime.now(tz=UTC)
   162	        )
   163	        with quiet_redis_noise():
   164	            batch_client = DelayedWorkflowClient()
   165	            evaluation = process_workflows(
   166	                batch_client, event_data, event_start_time=event_start_time
   167	            )
   168	            if isinstance(event_data.event, GroupEvent):
   169	                kick_off_service_hooks(
   170	                    event_data.event,
   171	                    evaluation.data.triggered_actions is not None
   172	                    and len(evaluation.data.triggered_actions) > 0,
   173	                )
   174	
   175	    evaluation.log_to(logger)
   176	    duration = time.time() - start_time
   177	    is_slow = duration > 1.0
   178	    # We want full coverage for particularly slow cases, plus a random sampling.
   179	    if is_slow or random.random() < 0.0001:
   180	        stats = recorder.get_result()
   181	        logger.info(
   182	            "workflow_engine.tasks.process_workflows.scopedstats",
   183	            extra={
   184	                "is_slow": is_slow,
   185	                "stats": stats,
   186	                "duration": duration,
   187	            },
   188	        )
   189	
   190	    metrics.incr("workflow_engine.tasks.process_workflow_task_executed", sample_rate=1.0)
   191	
   192	
   193	@instrumented_task(
   194	    name="sentry.workflow_engine.tasks.workflows.schedule_delayed_workflows",
   195	    namespace=namespaces.workflow_engine_tasks,
   196	    processing_deadline_duration=40,
   197	)
   198	def schedule_delayed_workflows(**kwargs: Any) -> None:
   199	    """
   200	    Schedule delayed workflow buffers in a batch.
   201	    """
   202	    from sentry.workflow_engine.processors.schedule import process_buffered_workflows
   203	
   204	    lock_name = "schedule_delayed_workflows"
   205	    lock = locks.get(f"workflow_engine:{lock_name}", duration=60, name=lock_name)
   206	
   207	    try:
   208	        with lock.acquire():
   209	            with quiet_redis_noise():
   210	                process_buffered_workflows(DelayedWorkflowClient())
   211	    except UnableToAcquireLock as error:
   212	        logger.warning("schedule_delayed_workflows.fail", extra={"error": error})

    21	from sentry.workflow_engine.types import WorkflowEventData, WorkflowId
    22	from sentry.workflow_engine.utils import log_context
    23	
    24	logger = log_context.get_logger(__name__)
    25	
    26	
    27	def build_trigger_action_task_params(
    28	    action: Action,
    29	    event_data: WorkflowEventData,
    30	    workflow_uuid_map: dict[WorkflowId, str],
    31	    *,
    32	    workflow_id: WorkflowId,
    33	) -> dict[str, object]:
    34	    """
    35	    Build parameters for trigger_action task invocation.
    36	
    37	    Args:
    38	        action: The action to trigger.
    39	        event_data: The event data to use for the action.
    40	        workflow_uuid_map: Mapping of workflow_id to notification_uuid.
    41	        workflow_id: The workflow this action is firing for.
    42	    """
    43	    event_id = None
    44	    activity_id = None
    45	    occurrence_id = None
    46	
    47	    if isinstance(event_data.event, GroupEvent):
    48	        event_id = event_data.event.event_id
    49	        occurrence_id = event_data.event.occurrence_id
    50	    elif isinstance(event_data.event, Activity):
    51	        activity_id = event_data.event.id
    52	
    53	    task_params = {
    54	        "action_id": action.id,
    55	        "workflow_id": workflow_id,
    56	        "event_id": event_id,
    57	        "activity_id": activity_id,
    58	        "group_id": event_data.event.group_id,
    59	        "occurrence_id": occurrence_id,
    60	        "group_state": event_data.group_state,
    61	        "has_reappeared": False,  # TODO: remove when deployed trigger_action task doesn't expect it
    62	        "has_escalated": event_data.has_escalated,
    63	    }
    64	
    65	    if workflow_id in workflow_uuid_map:
    66	        task_params["notification_uuid"] = workflow_uuid_map[workflow_id]
    67	
    68	    return task_params
    69	
    70	
    71	@instrumented_task(
    72	    name="sentry.workflow_engine.tasks.trigger_action",
    73	    namespace=namespaces.workflow_engine_tasks,
    74	    processing_deadline_duration=30,
    75	    retry=Retry(
    76	        times=3,
    77	        delay=5,
    78	        on=(Exception, ProcessingDeadlineExceeded),
    79	        ignore=(
    80	            Action.DoesNotExist,
    81	            Group.DoesNotExist,
    82	            Project.DoesNotExist,
    83	            ProjectNotActiveError,
    84	            Workflow.DoesNotExist,
    85	        ),
    86	    ),
    87	    silo_mode=SiloMode.CELL,
    88	    silenced_exceptions=(
    89	        Project.DoesNotExist,
    90	        ProjectNotActiveError,
    91	        Workflow.DoesNotExist,
    92	    ),
    93	)
    94	def trigger_action(
    95	    action_id: int,
    96	    workflow_id: int,
    97	    event_id: str | None,
    98	    activity_id: int | None,
    99	    group_id: int,
   100	    occurrence_id: str | None,
   101	    group_state: GroupState,
   102	    has_escalated: bool,
   103	    notification_uuid: str | None = None,
   104	    **kwargs: dict[str, object],
   105	) -> None:
   106	    import uuid
   107	
   108	    from sentry.workflow_engine.processors.detector import get_preferred_detector
   109	
   110	    # Generate UUID if not provided (handles version skew at task boundary)
   111	    if notification_uuid is None:
   112	        notification_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())
   113	
   114	    # XOR check to ensure exactly one of event_id or activity_id is provided
   115	    if (event_id is not None) == (activity_id is not None):
   116	        logger.error(
   117	            "Exactly one of event_id or activity_id must be provided",
   118	            extra={"event_id": event_id, "activity_id": activity_id},
   119	        )
   120	        raise ValueError("Exactly one of event_id or activity_id must be provided")
   121	
   122	    action = Action.objects.get(id=action_id)
   123	
   124	    if event_id is not None:
   125	        event_data = build_workflow_event_data_from_event(
   126	            event_id=event_id,
   127	            group_id=group_id,
   128	            workflow_id=workflow_id,
   129	            occurrence_id=occurrence_id,
   130	            group_state=group_state,
   131	            has_escalated=has_escalated,
   132	        )
   133	    elif activity_id is not None:
   134	        event_data = build_workflow_event_data_from_activity(
   135	            activity_id=activity_id, group_id=group_id
   136	        )
   137	    else:
   138	        # This should never happen, and if it does, need to investigate
   139	        logger.error(
   140	            "Exactly one of event_id or activity_id must be provided",
   141	            extra={"event_id": event_id, "activity_id": activity_id},
   142	        )
   143	        raise ValueError("Exactly one of event_id or activity_id must be provided")
   144	
   145	    detector = get_preferred_detector(event_data)
   146	
   147	    metrics.incr(
   148	        "workflow_engine.tasks.trigger_action_task_started",
   149	        tags={"action_type": action.type, "detector_type": detector.type},
   150	        sample_rate=1.0,
   151	    )
   152	    # Set up a timeout grouping context because we want to make sure any Sentry timeout reporting
   153	    # in this scope is grouped properly.
   154	    with timeout_grouping_context(action.type):
   155	        action.trigger(event_data, notification_uuid=notification_uuid, workflow_id=workflow_id)

    10	from sentry.db.models.base import DefaultFieldsModel

    14	
    15	class GroupReactionType(Enum):
    16	    USER_SUSPECT_COMMIT_REACTION = 0
    17	
    18	
    19	@cell_silo_model
    20	class GroupReaction(DefaultFieldsModel):
    21	    """
    22	    This model has no affiliation with PullRequestComment.reactions.
    23	    This model represents feedback/evaluations related to a Group or an entity associated with a Group.
    24	
    25	    This model supports multiple patterns based on NULL combinations:
    26	    Suspect Commit Reactions:
    27	    - commit=commit_obj, group=group_obj: Reaction on specific commit for specific group
    28	    - commit=commit_obj, group=NULL: Commit excluded from suspicion on groups project-wide
    29	    - commit=NULL, group=group_obj: Group excluded from suspect commits entirely
    30	    """
    31	
    32	    __relocation_scope__ = RelocationScope.Excluded
    33	
    34	    reaction = models.BooleanField()
    35	    project = FlexibleForeignKey("sentry.Project", on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    36	    group = FlexibleForeignKey("sentry.Group", null=True, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    37	    commit = FlexibleForeignKey(
    38	        "sentry.Commit", null=True, on_delete=models.CASCADE, db_constraint=False
    39	    )
    40	    user_id = HybridCloudForeignKey(settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL, null=True, on_delete="SET_NULL")
    41	    source = models.PositiveSmallIntegerField(
    42	        choices=(
    43	            (
    44	                GroupReactionType.USER_SUSPECT_COMMIT_REACTION.value,
    45	                "User Submitted Suspect Commit Reaction",
    46	            ),
    47	        )
    48	    )
    49	
    50	    class Meta:
    51	        app_label = "sentry"
    52	        db_table = "sentry_groupreaction"
    53	        constraints = [
    54	            # Ensure at least one of commit or group is provided
    55	            models.CheckConstraint(
    56	                condition=~models.Q(commit__isnull=True, group__isnull=True),
    57	                name="commit_or_group_required",
    58	            ),
    59	            # User reaction constraints: one reaction per user per context
    60	            models.UniqueConstraint(
    61	                fields=["project", "commit", "group", "user_id"],
    62	                condition=models.Q(
    63	                    commit__isnull=False, group__isnull=False, user_id__isnull=False
    64	                ),
    65	                name="unique_user_commit_group_reaction",
    66	            ),
    67	            models.UniqueConstraint(
    68	                fields=["project", "commit", "user_id"],
    69	                condition=models.Q(commit__isnull=False, group__isnull=True, user_id__isnull=False),
    70	                name="unique_user_project_commit_reaction",
    71	            ),
    72	            models.UniqueConstraint(
    73	                fields=["project", "group", "user_id"],
    74	                condition=models.Q(commit__isnull=True, group__isnull=False, user_id__isnull=False),
    75	                name="unique_user_group_exclusion_reaction",
    76	            ),
    77	        ]

    19	@cell_silo_model

    21	    __relocation_scope__ = RelocationScope.Excluded
    22	
    23	    project_id = BoundedBigIntegerField(db_index=True)
    24	    group_id = BoundedBigIntegerField()
    25	    # TODO: Should be BoundedBigIntegerField
    26	    release_id = BoundedPositiveIntegerField(db_index=True)
    27	    environment = models.CharField(max_length=64, default="")
    28	    first_seen = models.DateTimeField(default=timezone.now)
    29	    last_seen = models.DateTimeField(default=timezone.now)
    30	
    31	    class Meta:
    32	        app_label = "sentry"
    33	        db_table = "sentry_grouprelease"
    34	        unique_together = (("group_id", "release_id", "environment"),)
    35	        indexes = (models.Index(fields=("group_id", "first_seen")),)
    36	
    37	    __repr__ = sane_repr("group_id", "release_id")
    38	
    39	    @classmethod
    40	    def get_cache_key(cls, group_id, release_id, environment):
    41	        return "grouprelease:1:{}:{}".format(
    42	            group_id, md5_text(f"{release_id}:{environment}").hexdigest()
    43	        )
    44	
    45	    @classmethod
    46	    def get_or_create(cls, group, release, environment, datetime, **kwargs):
    47	        cache_key = cls.get_cache_key(group.id, release.id, environment.name)
    48	
    49	        instance = cache.get(cache_key)
    50	        if instance is None:
    51	            instance, created = cls.objects.get_or_create(
    52	                release_id=release.id,
    53	                group_id=group.id,
    54	                environment=environment.name,
    55	                defaults={
    56	                    "first_seen": datetime,
    57	                    "last_seen": datetime,
    58	                    "project_id": group.project_id,
    59	                },
    60	            )
    61	        else:
    62	            created = False
    63	
    64	        if not created and instance.last_seen < datetime - timedelta(seconds=60):
    65	            buffer_incr(
    66	                model=cls,
    67	                columns={},
    68	                filters={"id": instance.id},
    69	                extra={"last_seen": datetime},
    70	            )
    71	            instance.last_seen = datetime
    72	
    73	        cache.set(cache_key, instance, 3600)
    74	        return instance

    54

    56	    user_id = HybridCloudForeignKey(settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL, null=True, on_delete="SET_NULL")
    57	    date_started = models.DateTimeField(default=timezone.now)

    70	                name="data__pend_inc_detector_id_idx",
    71	            ),

    76	                name="exclude_overlapping_date_start_end",
    77	                expressions=[

    79	                    (
    80	                        TsTzRange(
    81	                            "date_started",
    82	                            "date_ended",
    83	                            RangeBoundary(inclusive_lower=True, inclusive_upper=True),
    84	                        ),

    93	    def close_open_period(
    94	        self,
    95	        resolution_activity: Activity,
    96	        resolution_time: datetime,
    97	    ) -> None:
    98	        if self.date_ended is not None:
    99	            logger.warning("Open period is already closed", extra={"group_id": self.group.id})
   100	            return
   101	
   102	        self.update(
   103	            date_ended=resolution_time,
   104	            resolution_activity=resolution_activity,
   105	            user_id=resolution_activity.user_id,
   106	        )
   107	
   108	        if get_group_type_by_type_id(self.group.type).detector_settings is not None:
   109	            GroupOpenPeriodActivity.objects.create(
   110	                group_open_period=self,
   111	                type=OpenPeriodActivityType.CLOSED,
   112	            )
   113	
   114	    def reopen_open_period(self) -> None:
   115	        if self.date_ended is None:
   116	            logger.warning("Open period is not closed", extra={"group_id": self.group.id})
   117	            return
   118	
   119	        self.update(date_ended=None, resolution_activity=None, user_id=None)

   122	def get_last_checked_for_open_period(group: Group) -> datetime:
   123	    from sentry.incidents.grouptype import MetricIssue
   124	    from sentry.incidents.models.alert_rule import AlertRule
   125	
   126	    event = group.get_latest_event()
   127	    last_checked = group.last_seen
   128	    if event and group.type == MetricIssue.type_id:
   129	        alert_rule_id = event.data.get("contexts", {}).get("metric_alert", {}).get("alert_rule_id")
   130	        if alert_rule_id:
   131	            try:
   132	                alert_rule = AlertRule.objects.get(id=alert_rule_id)
   133	                now = timezone.now()
   134	                last_checked = now - timedelta(seconds=alert_rule.snuba_query.time_window)
   135	            except AlertRule.DoesNotExist:
   136	                pass
   137	
   138	    return last_checked
   139

    31	@cell_silo_model
    32	class GroupSnooze(Model):
    33	    """
    34	    A snooze marks an issue as ignored until a condition is hit.
    35	
    36	    - If ``until`` is set, the snooze is lifted at the given datetime.
    37	    - If ``count`` is set, the snooze is lifted when total occurrences match.
    38	    - If ``window`` is set (in addition to count), the snooze is lifted when
    39	      the rate of events matches.
    40	    - If ``user_count`` is set, the snooze is lfited when unique users match.
    41	    - If ``user_window`` is set (in addition to count), the snooze is lifted
    42	      when the rate unique users matches.
    43	
    44	    NOTE: `window` and `user_window` are specified in minutes
    45	    """
    46	
    47	    __relocation_scope__ = RelocationScope.Excluded
    48	
    49	    group = FlexibleForeignKey("sentry.Group", unique=True)
    50	    until = models.DateTimeField(null=True)
    51	    count = BoundedPositiveIntegerField(null=True)
    52	    window = BoundedPositiveIntegerField(null=True)
    53	    user_count = BoundedPositiveIntegerField(null=True)
    54	    user_window = BoundedPositiveIntegerField(null=True)
    55	    state: models.Field[dict[str, Any] | None, dict[str, Any] | None] = JSONField(null=True)
    56	    actor_id = BoundedPositiveIntegerField(null=True)
    57	
    58	    objects: ClassVar[BaseManager[Self]] = BaseManager(cache_fields=("group",))
    59	
    60	    class Meta:
    61	        db_table = "sentry_groupsnooze"
    62	        app_label = "sentry"
    63	
    64	    __repr__ = sane_repr("group_id")
    65	
    66	    @classmethod
    67	    def get_cache_key(cls, group_id: int) -> str:
    68	        return f"groupsnooze_group_id:1:{group_id}"
    69	
    70	    def is_valid(
    71	        self, group: Group | None = None, test_rates: bool = False, use_pending_data: bool = False
    72	    ) -> bool:
    73	        if group is None:
    74	            group = self.group
    75	        elif group.id != self.group_id:
    76	            raise ValueError
    77	
    78	        if self.until:
    79	            if self.until <= timezone.now():
    80	                return False
    81	
    82	        if self.count:
    83	            if self.window:
    84	                if test_rates:
    85	                    if not self.test_frequency_rates():
    86	                        return False
    87	            else:
    88	                times_seen = group.times_seen_with_pending if use_pending_data else group.times_seen
    89	                assert self.state is not None
    90	                if self.count <= times_seen - self.state["times_seen"]:
    91	                    return False
    92	
    93	        if self.user_count and test_rates:
    94	            if not self.test_user_rates_or_counts(group):
    95	                return False
    96	
    97	        return True
    98	
    99	    def test_frequency_rates(self) -> bool:
   100	        cache_key = f"groupsnooze:v1:{self.id}:test_frequency_rate:events_seen_counter"
   101	
   102	        cache_ttl = self.window * 60  # Redis TTL in seconds (window is in minutes)
   103	
   104	        cached_event_count: int | float = float("inf")  # using +inf as a sentinel value
   105	
   106	        try:
   107	            cached_event_count = cache.incr(cache_key)
   108	            cache.touch(cache_key, cache_ttl)
   109	        except ValueError:
   110	            # key doesn't exist, fall back on sentinel value
   111	            pass
   112	
   113	        if cached_event_count < self.count:
   114	            metrics.incr("groupsnooze.test_frequency_rates", tags={"cached": "true", "hit": "true"})
   115	            return True
   116	
   117	        metrics.incr("groupsnooze.test_frequency_rates", tags={"cached": "true", "hit": "false"})
   118	        metrics.incr("groupsnooze.test_frequency_rates.snuba_call")
   119	        end = timezone.now()
   120	        start = end - timedelta(minutes=self.window)
   121	
   122	        rate = tsdb.backend.get_timeseries_sums(
   123	            model=get_issue_tsdb_group_model(self.group.issue_category),
   124	            keys=[self.group_id],
   125	            start=start,
   126	            end=end,
   127	            tenant_ids={"organization_id": self.group.project.organization_id},
   128	            referrer_suffix="frequency_snoozes",
   129	        )[self.group_id]
   130	
   131	        # TTL is further into the future than it needs to be, but we'd rather over-estimate
   132	        # and call Snuba more often than under-estimate and not trigger
   133	        cache.set(cache_key, rate, cache_ttl)
   134	
   135	        if rate >= self.count:
   136	            return False
   137	
   138	        return True
   139	
   140	    def test_user_rates_or_counts(self, group: Group) -> bool:

    63	class GroupOwnerManager(BaseManager["GroupOwner"]):
    64	    def update_or_create_and_preserve_context(
    65	        self, lookup_kwargs: dict, defaults: dict, context_defaults: dict
    66	    ) -> tuple[GroupOwner, bool]:
    67	        """
    68	        update_or_create doesn't have great support for json fields like context.
    69	        To preserve the existing content and update only the keys we specify,
    70	        we have to handle the operation this way.
    71	
    72	        use lookup_kwargs to perform the .get()
    73	        if found: update the object with defaults and the context with context_defaults
    74	        if not found: create the object with the values in lookup_kwargs, defaults, and context_defaults
    75	
    76	        Note: lookup_kwargs is modified if the GroupOwner is created, do not reuse it for other purposes!
    77	        """
    78	        try:
    79	            group_owner = GroupOwner.objects.annotate(
    80	                context__asjsonb=Cast("context", models.JSONField())
    81	            ).get(**lookup_kwargs)
    82	
    83	            for k, v in defaults.items():
    84	                setattr(group_owner, k, v)
    85	
    86	            existing_context = group_owner.context or {}
    87	            existing_context.update(context_defaults)
    88	            group_owner.context = existing_context
    89	
    90	            group_owner.save()
    91	            return group_owner, False
    92	        except GroupOwner.DoesNotExist:
    93	            # modify lookup_kwargs so they can be used to create the GroupOwner
    94	            keys_to_delete = [k for k in lookup_kwargs.keys() if "__" in k]
    95	            for k in keys_to_delete:
    96	                del lookup_kwargs[k]
    97	
    98	            lookup_kwargs.update(defaults)
    99	            lookup_kwargs["context"] = context_defaults
   100	
   101	            return GroupOwner.objects.create(**lookup_kwargs), True
   102

    41	class GroupAssigneeManager(BaseManager["GroupAssignee"]):
    42	    def get_assigned_to_data(
    43	        self,
    44	        assigned_to: Team | RpcUser | User,
    45	        assignee_type: str,
    46	        extra: dict[str, str] | None = None,
    47	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
    48	        data = {

    50	            "assigneeEmail": getattr(assigned_to, "email", None),

    59	    def get_assignee_data(self, assigned_to: Team | RpcUser | User) -> tuple[str, str, str]:
    60	        from sentry.models.team import Team
    61	        from sentry.users.models.user import User
    62	        from sentry.users.services.user import RpcUser
    63	
    64	        if isinstance(assigned_to, (User, RpcUser)):
    65	            assignee_type = "user"
    66	            assignee_type_attr = "user_id"
    67	            other_type = "team"
    68	        elif isinstance(assigned_to, Team):
    69	            assignee_type = "team"
    70	            assignee_type_attr = "team_id"
    71	            other_type = "user_id"
    72	        else:
    73	            raise AssertionError(f"Invalid type to assign to: {type(assigned_to)}")
    74	
    75	        return (assignee_type, assignee_type_attr, other_type)
    76	
    77	    def remove_old_assignees(
    78	        self,
    79	        group: Group,
    80	        previous_assignee: GroupAssignee | None,
    81	        *,
    82	        new_assignee: NewAssignee | None = None,
    83	    ) -> None:
    84	        from sentry.models.team import Team
    85	
    86	        if not previous_assignee:
    87	            return
    88	
    89	        if previous_assignee.team:
    90	            team_members = list(previous_assignee.team.member_set.values_list("user_id", flat=True))
    91	            if (
    92	                new_assignee is not None
    93	                and new_assignee.type == "user"
    94	                and new_assignee.id in team_members
    95	            ):
    96	                team_members.remove(new_assignee.id)
    97	
    98	            GroupSubscription.objects.filter(
    99	                group=group,
   100	                project=group.project,
   101	                user_id__in=team_members,
   102	                reason=GroupSubscriptionReason.assigned,
   103	            ).delete()
   104	            logger.info(
   105	                "groupassignee.remove",
   106	                extra={"group_id": group.id, "team_id": previous_assignee.team.id},
   107	            )
   108	        else:
   109	            # if the new assignee is a team that the old assignee (a user) is in, don't remove them
   110	            if new_assignee is not None and new_assignee.type == "team":
   111	                team = Team.objects.get(id=new_assignee.id)
   112	                team_members = list(team.member_set.values_list("user_id", flat=True))
   113	                if previous_assignee.user_id in team_members:
   114	                    return
   115	
   116	            GroupSubscription.objects.filter(
   117	                group=group,
   118	                project=group.project,
   119	                user_id=previous_assignee.user_id,
   120	                reason=GroupSubscriptionReason.assigned,
   121	            ).delete()
   122	            logger.info(
   123	                "groupassignee.remove",
   124	                extra={"group_id": group.id, "user_id": previous_assignee.user_id},
   125	            )
   126

   134	        force_autoassign: bool = False,

   136	    ) -> dict[str, bool]:

   117	def bulk_remove_groups_from_inbox(

   119	    action: GroupInboxRemoveAction | None = None,
   120	    user: User | RpcUser | Team | None = None,
   121	) -> None:
   122	    with sentry_sdk.start_span(name="bulk_remove_groups_from_inbox"):
   123	        try:

   125	            group_inbox.delete()
   126

   128	                Activity.objects.bulk_create(
   129	                    [
   130	                        Activity(
   131	                            project_id=group_inbox_item.group.project_id,
   132	                            group_id=group_inbox_item.group.id,
   133	                            type=ActivityType.MARK_REVIEWED.value,
   134	                            user_id=user.id,
   135	                        )
   136	                        for group_inbox_item in group_inbox
   137	                    ]
   138	                )
   139	
   140	                bulk_record_group_history(list(groups), GroupHistoryStatus.REVIEWED, actor=user)

    11	from sentry import options
    12	from sentry.backup.scopes import RelocationScope
    13	from sentry.db.models import (
    14	    BoundedPositiveIntegerField,
    15	    FlexibleForeignKey,
    16	    Model,
    17	    cell_silo_model,
    18	)
    19	from sentry.db.models.base import sane_repr
    20	from sentry.db.models.manager.base import BaseManager
    21	from sentry.db.models.manager.base_query_set import BaseQuerySet
    22	from sentry.grouping.ingest.caching import (
    23	    get_grouphash_object_cache_key,
    24	    invalidate_grouphash_cache_on_save,
    25	    invalidate_grouphash_caches_on_delete,
    26	)
    27	from sentry.types.grouphash_metadata import has_fingerprint_data
    28	from sentry.utils import json
    29	from sentry.utils.json import JSONDecodeError
    30	
    31	if TYPE_CHECKING:
    32	    from sentry.models.grouphashmetadata import GroupHashMetadata
    33	
    34	
    35	# Use a custom queryset class so we can invalidate the relevant cache entries when data is updated.
    36	# Note that we don't need to write custom methods for saving or deleting because those send built-in
    37	# Django signals we can listen for (see end of file). We also don't have to override the model's
    38	# `update` method because our base model class already does that, with a method that calls the
    39	# queryset's `update`.
    40	class GroupHashQuerySet(BaseQuerySet):
    41	    def update(self, **kwargs: Any) -> int:
    42	        if not options.get("grouping.use_ingest_grouphash_caching"):
    43	            return super().update(**kwargs)
    44	
    45	        cache_keys = [
    46	            get_grouphash_object_cache_key(hash_value, project_id)
    47	            for hash_value, project_id in (
    48	                self.update_with_returning(  # This is where the actual update occurs
    49	                    ["hash", "project_id"], **kwargs
    50	                )
    51	            )
    52	        ]
    53	
    54	        try:
    55	            cache.delete_many(cache_keys)
    56	        except Exception:
    57	            # If we can't delete from the cache, likely we couldn't have put the grouphashes there
    58	            # in the first place. Regardless, we don't want this to block the `update` call.
    59	            pass
    60	
    61	        return len(cache_keys)
    62	
    63	
    64	class GroupHashModelManager(BaseManager["GroupHash"]):
    65	    def get_queryset(self) -> GroupHashQuerySet:
    66	        return GroupHashQuerySet(self.model, using=self._db)
    67	
    68	
    69	@cell_silo_model
    70	class GroupHash(Model):
    71	    __relocation_scope__ = RelocationScope.Excluded
    72	
    73	    class State:
    74	        UNLOCKED: None = None
    75	        LOCKED_IN_MIGRATION = 1
    76	
    77	    project = FlexibleForeignKey("sentry.Project", null=True)
    78	    hash = models.CharField(max_length=32)
    79	    group = FlexibleForeignKey("sentry.Group", null=True)
    80	
    81	    # not-null => the event should be discarded
    82	    group_tombstone_id = BoundedPositiveIntegerField(db_index=True, null=True)
    83	    state = BoundedPositiveIntegerField(
    84	        choices=[(State.LOCKED_IN_MIGRATION, _("Locked (Migration in Progress)"))], null=True
    85	    )
    86	    date_added = models.DateTimeField(default=timezone.now, null=True)
    87	
    88	    # Custom manager in order to override the queryset `update` method, so that we can invalidate
    89	    # the grouphash cache used during ingest when `GroupHash.objects.update` is called (such as when
    90	    # we merge groups).
    91	    objects: ClassVar[GroupHashModelManager] = GroupHashModelManager()
    92	
    93	    class Meta:
    94	        app_label = "sentry"
    95	        db_table = "sentry_grouphash"
    96	        unique_together = (("project", "hash"),)
    97	
    98	    @property
    99	    def metadata(self) -> GroupHashMetadata | None:
   100	        try:
   101	            return self._metadata
   102	        except AttributeError:
   103	            return None
   104	
   105	    __repr__ = sane_repr("group_id", "hash")
   106	    __str__ = __repr__
   107	
   108	    def get_associated_fingerprint(self) -> list[str] | None:
   109	        """
   110	        Pull a resolved fingerprint value from the grouphash's metadata, if possible.
   111	
   112	        This only returns results for hashes which come from either hybrid or custom fingerprints.
   113	        Even then, the hash may come from a time before we were storing such data, in which case
   114	        this returns None.
   115	        """
   116	        if (
   117	            not self.metadata
   118	            or not self.metadata.hashing_metadata
   119	            or not has_fingerprint_data(self.metadata.hashing_metadata)
   120	        ):
   121	            return None
   122	
   123	        try:
   124	            return json.loads(self.metadata.hashing_metadata["fingerprint"])
   125	
   126	        # Fingerprints used to be stored by stringifying them rather than jsonifying them, and the
   127	        # stringified ones can't be parsed and will cause this error
   128	        except JSONDecodeError:
   129	            return None
   130	
   131	
   132	# Calling `save` on the model uses the built-in method, which sends save signals. You can't call
   133	# `save` on queryset. This handler deletes the grouphash from the `GroupHash` object cache.
   134	pre_save.connect(invalidate_grouphash_cache_on_save, sender=GroupHash, weak=False)
   135	
   136	# Calling `delete` on the model or the queryset calls `Collector.delete` which sends delete signals
   137	# if you're acting on multiple rows and/or the model has dependencies (but not if not). Fortunately,
   138	# `GroupHashMetadata` depends on `GroupHash` so the latter doesn't qualify for fast deletes and the
   139	# signals are sent. This handler deletes the grouphash from both the `GroupHash` object cache and
   140	# the grouphash existence boolean cache.

    35	class GroupHistoryStatus:

    39	    # Prefer to use ONGOING instead of UNRESOLVED. We will be deprecating UNRESOLVED in the future.

    72	    def has_resolution(cls, group, release):

    78	
    79	        def compare_release_dates_for_in_next_release(res_release, res_release_datetime, release):
    80	            """
    81	            Helper function that compares release versions based on date for
    82	            `GroupResolution.Type.in_next_release`
    83	            """
    84	            return res_release == release.id or res_release_datetime > release.date_added

    89	                res_release,
    90	                res_release_version,
    91	                res_release_datetime,
    92	                current_release_version,

    95	                .select_related("release")
    96	                .values_list(
    97	                    "type",
    98	                    "release__id",
    99	                    "release__version",

   101	                    "current_release_version",
   102	                )[0]
   103	            )
   104	        except IndexError:
