   106	
   107	        # if no release is present, we assume we've gone from "no release" to "some release"
   108	        # in application configuration, and thus this must be older
   109	        if not release:

   112	        follows_semver = follows_semver_versioning_scheme(

   119	        # next release, which means a release will have a resolution if it is the same as
   120	        # `current_release_version` or was released before it according to either its semver version

   122	        # versioning or not

   128	                    # If current_release_version > release.version => 1
   129	                    current_release_raw = parse_release(

   133	                        "version_raw"
   134	                    )
   135	                    if compare_version_relay(current_release_raw, release_raw) >= 0:

    44	class ActivityManager(BaseManager["Activity"]):
    45	    def get_activities_for_group(self, group: Group, num: int) -> Sequence[Activity]:
    46	        activities = []
    47	        activity_qs = self.filter(group=group).order_by("-datetime")
    48	
    49	        initial_priority_value = group.get_event_metadata().get("initial_priority")
    50	        initial_priority = (
    51	            PriorityLevel(initial_priority_value).to_str() if initial_priority_value else None
    52	        )
    53	
    54	        prev_sig = None
    55	        sig = None
    56	        # we select excess so we can filter dupes
    57	        for item in activity_qs[: num * 2]:
    58	            prev_sig = sig
    59	            sig = (item.type, item.ident, item.user_id, item.data)
    60	
    61	            if item.type == ActivityType.NOTE.value:
    62	                activities.append(item)
    63	                continue
    64	
    65	            if sig != prev_sig:
    66	                activities.append(item)
    67	
    68	        activities.append(
    69	            Activity(
    70	                id=0,
    71	                project=group.project,
    72	                group=group,
    73	                type=ActivityType.FIRST_SEEN.value,
    74	                data={"priority": initial_priority},
    75	                datetime=group.first_seen,
    76	            )
    77	        )
    78	
    79	        return activities[:num]
    80	
    81	    def create_group_activity(
    82	        self,
    83	        group: Group,
    84	        type: ActivityType,
    85	        user: User | RpcUser | None = None,
    86	        user_id: int | None = None,
    87	        data: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
    88	        send_notification: bool = True,
    89	        datetime: datetime | None = None,
    90	        detector_id: DetectorId | None = None,
    91	    ) -> Activity:
    92	        if user:
    93	            user_id = user.id
    94	        activity_args = {
    95	            "project_id": group.project_id,
    96	            "group": group,
    97	            "type": type.value,
    98	            "data": data,
    99	        }
   100	        if user_id is not None:
   101	            activity_args["user_id"] = user_id
   102	        if datetime is not None:
   103	            activity_args["datetime"] = datetime
   104	        activity = self.create(**activity_args)
   105	
   106	        if send_notification:
   107	            activity.send_notification()
   108	
   109	        invoke_workflow_activity_handlers(group, activity, detector_id)
   110	
   111	        return activity
   112

    87	class ResolvedPullRequest(NamedTuple):
    88	    """Result of resolving an externally-reported PR to its canonical ``PullRequest``.
    89	
    90	    ``pull_request`` is None when the reported ``(repo_name, provider)`` doesn't map to
    91	    exactly one active ``Repository``; ``repo_resolution`` says why, and
    92	    ``provider_unmappable`` flags a *present* provider string we don't map (an empty or
    93	    ``"unknown"`` provider is treated as absent, not unmappable). Callers decide how (and
    94	    whether) to log these under their own namespace.
    95	    """
    96	
    97	    pull_request: PullRequest | None
    98	    repo_resolution: RepoResolution
    99	    provider_unmappable: bool
   100	
   101	
   102	def parse_pull_request_number(url: str) -> int | None:
   103	    """Extract the PR/MR number from a pull-request URL, or None if there isn't one.
   104	
   105	    Matches the number after a ``/pull/`` (GitHub) or ``/merge_requests/`` (GitLab)
   106	    segment specifically — a source can report a branch/``tree`` URL as its result, and
   107	    we must not mistake a trailing branch-name segment for a PR number.
   108	    """
   109	    match = re.search(r"/(?:pull|pulls|merge_requests)/(\d+)", url)
   110	    return int(match.group(1)) if match else None
   111	
   112	
   113	def _normalize_scm_provider(provider: str | None) -> str | None:
   114	    """Normalize a reported SCM provider to Sentry's unprefixed form, or None if unusable.
   115	
   116	    Returns None for the ``"unknown"`` sentinel (the source couldn't resolve the repo) and
   117	    for empty values — neither can scope a provider filter. Lowercases before the sentinel
   118	    check so any casing (e.g. ``UNKNOWN``) is treated as unknown.
   119	    """
   120	    if not provider:
   121	        return None
   122	    provider = provider.lower()
   123	    if provider.startswith("integrations:"):
   124	        provider = provider.split(":", 1)[1]
   125	    if provider == "unknown":
   126	        return None
   127	    return provider

    12	@cell_silo_model
    13	class NotificationMessage(Model):
    14	    """
    15	    Data model represents the aggregate for an entire notification message.
    16	    A notification message must always have the resulting identifier that was sent back if there was no error.
    17	    When there is an error, there will be no identifier to leverage or store.
    18	
    19	    If a notification message has an error, the resulting error code and details will be saved.
    20	    This information will then be leveraged to show the user which notifications are consistently failing.
    21	    An example of this would be if a Slack channel no longer exists.
    22	
    23	    The data model hold the singular gateway for both Metric and Issue Alerts.
    24	    Following the specific data relationship, you should be able to find the specific rule, organization, project, and
    25	    incident or group event that this notification message was enacted.
    26	
    27	    If any notification message is in relation to another notification message, you should be able to find the original
    28	    notification message through the parent notification message relation.
    29	    """
    30	
    31	    __relocation_scope__ = RelocationScope.Excluded
    32	
    33	    # Related information regarding failed notifications.
    34	    # Leveraged to help give the user visibility into notifications that are consistently failing.
    35	    error_details = LegacyTextJSONField(null=True)
    36	    error_code = IntegerField(null=True, db_index=True)
    37	
    38	    # Resulting identifier from the vendor that can be leveraged for future interaction with the notification.
    39	    message_identifier = CharField(null=True)
    40	    # Reference to another notification if we choose to modify the original message or reply to it (like start a thread)
    41	    parent_notification_message = FlexibleForeignKey("self", null=True)
    42	
    43	    date_added = DateTimeField(default=timezone.now)
    44	
    45	    # Related information regarding Action (Workflow Engine)
    46	    action = FlexibleForeignKey("workflow_engine.Action")
    47	    group = FlexibleForeignKey("sentry.Group")
    48	    # Key for a start of a specific open period of the group (e.g. metric/uptime issues)
    49	    # This doesn't have to be set for all actions, only for actions that are related to a group which has a defined open period
    50	    open_period_start = DateTimeField(null=True)
    51	
    52	    class Meta:
    53	        app_label = "notifications"
    54	        db_table = "sentry_notificationmessage"
    55	        indexes = [
    56	            models.Index(
    57	                fields=["group", "action", "date_added"],
    58	                name="idx_notifmsg_group_action_date",
    59	            ),
    60	            models.Index(fields=["date_added"]),
    61	        ]
    62	
    63	    __repr__ = sane_repr("id", "message_identifier", "error_code")
